Title: Lead Data Analyst
Description:
🚀 Lead Data & Analytics | Remote (Netherlands)




We’re looking for a Lead Data & Analytics professional to own and shape a growing Data function into a scalable, modern platform that enables decision-making across the business.

This is a hands-on leadership role where you will combine technical depth with strategic influence, helping transform an evolving DataOps setup into a unified, product-oriented data capability.




You’ll lead a cross-functional team, define the data strategy, and ensure data engineering, analytics, and BI work together as a coherent ecosystem. At the same time, you’ll stay close to the technology-shaping architecture, improving pipelines, and setting standards for quality, governance, and scalability.




You’ll also play a key role in stakeholder alignment, prioritisation, and turning ambiguity into clear direction and execution.




What you bring:

Strong experience leading data teams in Agile environments, solid background in data engineering and analytics, hands-on SQL and Python in production, and the ability to balance technical leadership with people management and communication.




If you’re ready to build structure, elevate a team, and have real impact on how data is used across an organisation, this role offers that opportunity.

Title: Data Analist / BI Specialist
HTML:
Als Data Analist / BI Specialist zorg jij ervoor dat organisaties hun data omzetten in waardevolle inzichten. Je analyseert complexe datasets, vertaalt deze naar heldere rapportages en dashboards en adviseert stakeholders over datagedreven beslissingen. Met moderne BI-tools zoals Power BI, Tableau of Qlik maak je informatie toegankelijk en begrijpelijk. Daarmee help je onze klanten – zoals de Belastingdienst, Gemeente Den Haag, Centraal Justitieel Incassobureau en VGZ – om hun beleid en processen te verbeteren en toekomstgericht te sturen.


Waarom past deze vacature bij jou?

Je hebt een passie voor data en ziet het als jouw missie om cijfers en informatie te vertalen naar verhalen die impact maken. Je bent analytisch sterk, communicatief vaardig en kunt schakelen tussen business en techniek. Je vindt het leuk om samen te werken met collega’s en stakeholders en voelt je thuis in een omgeving waar data centraal staat.


Wat kan je van ons verwachten?

Bij CIMSOLUTIONS krijg je de kans om te werken aan uitdagende en innovatieve data- en BI-projecten bij toonaangevende organisaties. Wij investeren in jouw ontwikkeling en bieden naast een inspirerende werkomgeving:

  • Een vast dienstverband voor onbepaalde tijd, een max. salaris van € 6.000, bonusregeling, leaseauto, pensioenregeling en maandelijkse onkostenvergoeding;
  • Opdrachtkeuzevrijheid: Jij bepaalt in overleg waar je aan werkt, met de begeleiding van een personal coach;
  • Per jaar € 5.000,- opleidingsbudget en daarnaast ook nog eens 5-10 dagen studietijd;
  • Kennissessies met vakgenoten en inhoudelijke events;
  • Minimaal 25 vakantiedagen, met de mogelijkheid om extra dagen op te bouwen of te verkopen;
  • Sociale events zoals sportdagen, weekend weg en gezellige borrels;
  • Een vestiging bij jou in de buurt.


Persoonlijke ontwikkeling

Bij CIMSOLUTIONS geloven we dat leren de sleutel is tot succes. Learn, create and make it work is niet alleen onze slogan, maar zit in ons DNA. Daarom investeren we continu in jouw groei. Je krijgt de ruimte om jezelf te ontwikkelen tot bijvoorbeeld Senior Data Analist, BI Architect of Data Platform Specialist. Je wordt begeleid door ervaren collega’s, krijgt toegang tot kennisplatforms en blijft altijd scherp op de nieuwste ontwikkelingen binnen jouw vakgebied.


CIMSOLUTIONS heeft een actief diversiteitsbeleid en streeft naar een evenwichtige verdeling van professionals naar onder andere leeftijd, gender, etnische achtergrond en cultuur, arbeidsvermogen, kennis en competenties. Daarbij benadrukken wij dat gelijke kansen voor iedereen een belangrijk uitgangspunt zijn in onze selectie.


Wat breng jij mee?

  • Afgeronde hbo- of wo-opleiding in Bedrijfskunde, Econometrie, Data Science, Business Intelligence of vergelijkbaar;
  • Minimaal 4 jaar ervaring als Data Analist of BI Specialist;
  • Ervaring met BI-tools zoals Power BI, Tableau of Qlik;
  • Sterke kennis van SQL en ervaring met het analyseren van grote datasets;
  • Affiniteit met datavisualisatie, datakwaliteit en rapportage;
  • AI-affiniteit is een pré;
  • Goede beheersing van de Nederlandse en Engelse taal.


Waarom CIMSOLUTIONS?

  • 15 keer Top Employer!
  • Uitstekend salaris;
  • Vrije keuze leaseauto;
  • € 5000,- opleidingsbudget;
  • Aantrekkelijke bonussen;
  • Veel sociale evenementen;
  • Uitdagende projecten;
  • Gevarieerde opdrachten;

Title: Data Analyst
Description:
Amsterdam | 40 hours




Urban Gym Group is an industry pioneer within the European wellness and fitness community, with over 40 clubs and studios in major cities across the Netherlands and The United Kingdom. You might know us from brands such as Trainmore, ClubSportive, Renessence, THE CLB, TRIB3, PILAT3S and Gymbox. Our ambitious domestic and international growth plans require an expansion of our Business Intelligence team.




We are looking for a highly motivated and detail-oriented Data Analyst to join our team. In this role, you will play a key part in driving our data-driven decision-making, making a significant impact on our continued success. Working closely with our Finance, Marketing, and Analytics teams, you will extract valuable insights from our data, helping us optimise operations, enhance member experiences, and support our ambitious growth plans.




Key responsibilities:

Conduct market research and analyses to identify new club locations.
Develop business cases for club expansion, including new builds, mergers, and acquisitions.
Build ETL processes to structure data from various sources for business use.
Create data views, tables, and reports supporting daily operations and strategic goals.
Design dashboards that enable informed, data-driven decisions.
Analyze growth to identify high-performing areas and new opportunities.
Query databases for insights on key metrics like revenue and member retention.
Use data to boost member retention, inform marketing strategies, and support revenue growth.




You:

2 to 4 years of experience in a data-focused role (e.g., Data Analyst, Data Engineer, or BI Specialist).
Proven expertise in data pipeline creation, data visualization, and analytics.
Industry experience in fitness/wellness or retail is preferred, with insights into customer retention, revenue optimization, and business expansion.
Advanced proficiency in SQL with a strong understanding of Azure SQL.
Hands-on experience building ETL pipelines, ideally using Python or Azure Synapse Analytics.
Proficient in Power BI, with a track record of designing impactful dashboards and reports.
Strong attention to detail and problem-solving abilities.
Experience in consulting or retail is an asset, especially in roles focused on addressing business challenges.
Able to communicate complex data insights in a clear, actionable format for non-technical audiences.
Are located in the Netherlands




To foster collaboration, coaching, and team energy, we expect our colleagues to work on-site (head office or clubs) at least 4 days per week (and up to 5 days depending on the role).




We offer:

A competitive salary plus 8% holiday pay
26 holidays per year (based on full-time employment) and the option to exchange public holidays
Pension plan to support your future.
The chance to join our Work in Club program and contribute to charity work once a year
Unlimited access to all our high-end fitness locations for you and a +1
Unlimited access to well-being services for you and your family via OpenUp
An inspiring head office, a short 10-minute walk from Amsterdam Central Station
A diverse and energetic team of 500 colleagues pushing boundaries and supporting one another




* A personality assessment may be part of the hiring process.

  • Proven expertise in data pipeline creation, data visualization, and analytics.
  • Industry experience in fitness/wellness or retail is preferred, with insights into customer retention, revenue optimization, and business expansion.
  • Advanced proficiency in SQL with a strong understanding of Azure SQL.
  • Hands-on experience building ETL pipelines, ideally using Python or Azure Synapse Analytics.
  • Proficient in Power BI, with a track record of designing impactful dashboards and reports.
  • Strong attention to detail and problem-solving abilities.
  • Experience in consulting or retail is an asset, especially in roles focused on addressing business challenges.
  • Able to communicate complex data insights in a clear, actionable format for non-technical audiences.
  • Are located in the Netherlands


To foster collaboration, coaching, and team energy, we expect our colleagues to work on-site (head office or clubs) at least 4 days per week (and up to 5 days depending on the role).


We offer:

  • A competitive salary plus 8% holiday pay
  • 26 holidays per year (based on full-time employment) and the option to exchange public holidays
  • Pension plan to support your future.
  • The chance to join our Work in Club program and contribute to charity work once a year
  • Unlimited access to all our high-end fitness locations for you and a +1
  • Unlimited access to well-being services for you and your family via OpenUp
  • An inspiring head office, a short 10-minute walk from Amsterdam Central Station
  • A diverse and energetic team of 500 colleagues pushing boundaries and supporting one another


* A personality assessment may be part of the hiring process.

Title: Data Analist / BI Specialist
HTML:
Als Data Analist / BI Specialist zorg jij ervoor dat organisaties hun data omzetten in waardevolle inzichten. Je analyseert complexe datasets, vertaalt deze naar heldere rapportages en dashboards en adviseert stakeholders over datagedreven beslissingen. Met moderne BI-tools zoals Power BI, Tableau of Qlik maak je informatie toegankelijk en begrijpelijk. Daarmee help je onze klanten – zoals de Belastingdienst, Gemeente Den Haag, Centraal Justitieel Incassobureau en VGZ – om hun beleid en processen te verbeteren en toekomstgericht te sturen.


Waarom past deze vacature bij jou?

Je hebt een passie voor data en ziet het als jouw missie om cijfers en informatie te vertalen naar verhalen die impact maken. Je bent analytisch sterk, communicatief vaardig en kunt schakelen tussen business en techniek. Je vindt het leuk om samen te werken met collega’s en stakeholders en voelt je thuis in een omgeving waar data centraal staat.


Wat kan je van ons verwachten?

Bij CIMSOLUTIONS krijg je de kans om te werken aan uitdagende en innovatieve data- en BI-projecten bij toonaangevende organisaties. Wij investeren in jouw ontwikkeling en bieden naast een inspirerende werkomgeving:

  • Een vast dienstverband voor onbepaalde tijd, een max. salaris van € 6.000, bonusregeling, leaseauto, pensioenregeling en maandelijkse onkostenvergoeding;
  • Opdrachtkeuzevrijheid: Jij bepaalt in overleg waar je aan werkt, met de begeleiding van een personal coach;
  • Per jaar € 5.000,- opleidingsbudget en daarnaast ook nog eens 5-10 dagen studietijd;
  • Kennissessies met vakgenoten en inhoudelijke events;
  • Minimaal 25 vakantiedagen, met de mogelijkheid om extra dagen op te bouwen of te verkopen;
  • Sociale events zoals sportdagen, weekend weg en gezellige borrels;
  • Een vestiging bij jou in de buurt.


Persoonlijke ontwikkeling

Bij CIMSOLUTIONS geloven we dat leren de sleutel is tot succes. Learn, create and make it work is niet alleen onze slogan, maar zit in ons DNA. Daarom investeren we continu in jouw groei. Je krijgt de ruimte om jezelf te ontwikkelen tot bijvoorbeeld Senior Data Analist, BI Architect of Data Platform Specialist. Je wordt begeleid door ervaren collega’s, krijgt toegang tot kennisplatforms en blijft altijd scherp op de nieuwste ontwikkelingen binnen jouw vakgebied.


CIMSOLUTIONS heeft een actief diversiteitsbeleid en streeft naar een evenwichtige verdeling van professionals naar onder andere leeftijd, gender, etnische achtergrond en cultuur, arbeidsvermogen, kennis en competenties. Daarbij benadrukken wij dat gelijke kansen voor iedereen een belangrijk uitgangspunt zijn in onze selectie.


Wat breng jij mee?

  • Afgeronde hbo- of wo-opleiding in Bedrijfskunde, Econometrie, Data Science, Business Intelligence of vergelijkbaar;
  • Minimaal 4 jaar ervaring als Data Analist of BI Specialist;
  • Ervaring met BI-tools zoals Power BI, Tableau of Qlik;
  • Sterke kennis van SQL en ervaring met het analyseren van grote datasets;
  • Affiniteit met datavisualisatie, datakwaliteit en rapportage;
  • AI-affiniteit is een pré;
  • Goede beheersing van de Nederlandse en Engelse taal.


Waarom CIMSOLUTIONS?

  • 15 keer Top Employer!
  • Uitstekend salaris;
  • Vrije keuze leaseauto;
  • € 5000,- opleidingsbudget;
  • Aantrekkelijke bonussen;
  • Veel sociale evenementen;
  • Uitdagende projecten;
  • Gevarieerde opdrachten;
  • Gerenommeerde klanten.


Heb je interesse?

Wij vinden het leuk om op ons hoofdkantoor of één van onze lokale vestigingen of online kennis te maken. Solliciteer via onderstaande button of neem voor meer informatie contact op met ons recruitment team via:

  • Mail: recruitment@cimsolutions.nl;
  • Telefoon: 0499-366 000;
  • of bezoek onze website: www.cimsolutions.nl/werken-bij/

Title: Data Analyst
HTML:
For a client in Amsterdam, we are looking for a Data Analyst (Marketing Analytics) on a payroll basis. In this role, you’ll sit at the heart of marketing performance, helping connect data to real business outcomes. You’ll work with both global and local stakeholders, translating complex datasets into clear, actionable insights that shape future marketing investments.


Responsibilities

  • Design, build, and maintain Power BI dashboards to track marketing performance, channel contribution, and ROI;
  • Work with large-scale datasets using BigQuery, dbt, and SQL to create reliable analytical pipelines;
  • Conduct both recurring and ad hoc analyses to support business decision-making;
  • Apply statistical techniques (e.g. regression, hypothesis testing) to uncover deeper insights;
  • Collaborate with cross-functional teams across data, marketing, and product;
  • ranslate complex findings into clear visualisations and stakeholder-ready insights;
  • Explore and apply AI tooling to improve efficiency in analytics workflows.


Requirements

  • Strong hands-on experience with Power BI (data modelling, DAX, dashboard design);
  • Solid experience with SQL, dbt, and BigQuery;
  • Experience working with marketing data platforms;
  • Knowledge of Python for analytics and modelling is beneficial;
  • Strong stakeholder management and communication skills.


What We Offer

  • Interim position (payroll)
  • Hybrid working: 2–3 days per week in the Amsterdam office (non-negotiable)

Title: Associate Data Analyst - SQL, Pyspark, Tableau
Description:
Associate Data Analyst

We are supporting a global tech company.

7 Months – 40 hours per week –  73 EUR per hour (+ VAT) – 2 days onsite in Amsterdam office

Technical - Well versed in Sql, Pyspark, Tableau OR PowerBI
Soft-skill - Good communication skills, team player.


Are you passionate about transforming data into impactful insights? We are seeking a motivated and detail-oriented Associate Data Analyst to support our business processes through data analysis, visualization, and storytelling. In this vital role, you'll contribute to delivering meaningful reports and dashboards that guide strategic decisions, working alongside a collaborative team dedicated to excellence and continuous learning. If you're eager to grow your craft and make a tangible difference through data, this is the opportunity for you!
What You Will Do:

Support the development and delivery of data analytics solutions aligned with business needs.
Collect, prepare, and analyze data with limited supervision, translating complex data into clear insights and actionable recommendations.
Create compelling visualizations, dashboards, and reports to communicate findings effectively to stakeholders.
Collaborate with team members and stakeholders to ensure data quality, stability, and relevance.
Follow best practices in data analytics while continuously enhancing your technical skills and knowledge.
Required Skills:

Strong analytical and problem-solving abilities with an eye for detail.
Proficiency in data visualization tools such as Tableau, Data Studio, Power BI, or Mixpanel.
Experience with data analysis languages or tools like SQL, R, or Python.
Excellent interpersonal skills with an open, friendly attitude and the ability to communicate complex insights clearly.
Driven, results-oriented, and receptive to feedback with a desire to improve and learn.
Nice to Have Skills:

Knowledge of additional data analysis or visualization tools.
Experience in supporting data quality initiatives or data stability projects.
Familiarity with multiple data sources and data collection processes.
Preferred Education and Experience:

A Bachelor’s degree or higher in fields such as Data Science, Analytics, Computer Science, or related disciplines.
3 to 5 years of relevant experience or entry-level understanding of data analysis practices.
Other Requirements:

A curious mindset with a passion for data storytelling.
Ability to collaborate effectively within diverse teams and stakeholders.
Commitment to ethical data handling and confidentiality.

Title: Junior Data Analyst
Description:
Vacancy: Junior Data Analyst who is ‘equipped for impact’

Do you want to be part of a globally operating team of innovators? Do you also think bigger than big? And, do you have what it takes to make an impact? Then we might be looking for you. Find out more about our vacancy for a Junior Data Analyst.

About Our Organisation

We are Huisman. We develop step changing technical solutions and translate these into the design and construction of the largest onshore and offshore installations. For decades, we have been working all over the world for offshore wind, geothermal energy, oil & gas, leisure, civil and port logistics industries.

With our passionate workforce and worldwide production, service & sales facilities we are equipped for impact in these times of energy transition.

“Huisman combines engineering and digitalisation to create solutions for the future of renewable energies. It's a great place to be!”

Martijn Schoonus, Manager IT Applications

About The Position Of Junior Data Analyst

As a Junior Data Analyst, you will analyse equipment performance data and help develop reliable, reusable analytics for our engineers and customers. You will work closely with senior programmers and mechanical/maintenance engineers on complex, real-world engineering challenges. In addition, you will play a key role in clearly communicating complex findings to both technical and non-technical stakeholders, ensuring that your insights deliver a lasting impact.

What do we expect of you?

Responsibilities

As a Data Analyst at Huisman Digital, you will transform equipment performance data into actionable insights for our clients and internal engineers. Your responsibilities will include:


Analysing equipment performance data;
Maintaining and improving our codebook and project configuration files;
Collaborating with mechanical and maintenance engineers to identify improvement opportunities and deliver data-driven solutions;
Maintaining, validating, and enhancing analytical tools and models that address complex engineering challenges;
Communicating findings clearly to stakeholders to drive meaningful action.


Wondering if you are 'equipped for impact'?

If you enjoy mechanical and control engineering challenges and love analysing equipment data to generate insights, tackling programming challenges, dashboards, JSON files, or presentations, then you are a good match for this role.

You Are


Analytically strong;
Proactive, with a focus on results and practical, hands-on problem-solving;
An effective communicator;
A good team player with a collaborative spirit;
Passionate about continuous improvement and development.


You Have


A bachelor’s or master’s degree in mechanical engineering or a related technical engineering field.
Hands-on experience with programming, statistical analysis, and dashboard development.
Proficiency in Python for data analysis and familiarity with SQL (PostgreSQL).
Experience maintaining and improving codebooks and project configuration files.
Experience contributing to the maintenance, validation, and enhancement of analytical tools and models.


Additionally, It Is Nice To Have


Familiarity with Grafana.
Basic version control (Git), Jupyter Notebooks, and simple ETL/data-cleaning workflows.
Interest in heavy equipment, offshore/wind, or industrial IoT/telemetry.


Some Practical Things We Need You To Have


An EU passport/valid work permit for The Netherlands;
A good command of the English language verbally and in writing.


More About Huisman

We design and produce in-house and deliver our turn-key products to clients worldwide. Our clients choose us for our entrepreneurial spirit, skills, reliability, and innovative solutions. Examples? Consider the Fibre Rope System. So, if you join Huisman make sure you are equipped for impact. In other words, surprise us, our clients and… yourself!

What do we have to offer?


An excellent benefits package tailored to your experience and education level;
An international work environment;
Various growth and development opportunities;
Open, informal, no-nonsense culture with short communication lines.


Does this sound like you?

Apply online for the position of Junior Data Analyst by using the application form at the bottom of this page. We are curious to see what kind of impact you will make.

Still need a bit more info or looking for something that suits you better?

please contact us via our contact form for more information or sign up for our job alert to receive details of new opportunities in your inbox.

Disclaimer For Recruitment And Selection Agencies

We don’t accept any unsolicited applications or CVs from recruitment and selection agencies. Proposing candidates without invitation is not appreciated and will not be considered by Huisman. Applications received are property of Huisman. We don’t appreciate acquisition and this also applies to Huisman colleagues or associated Huisman companies.

Title: Data Analyst Intern
Description:
Job Description

Data Analyst Intern

Start date: August/September 2026

Duration: 6 months

Allowance: 625 euro per month (40hrs, 5 days) & full reimbursement of travel expenses  

Platform/Project: R&D Digital Transformation Team

Location: Amsterdam/Nijmegen (Primary location: Amsterdam)

Teams: R&D Digital Transformation

R&D Digital Transformation

You will be part of an international small team with the ambition to drive digital technology forward for Kraft Heinz R&D International Zone. Expect to be part of the team from day one. Strong learning experience on data visualization with many practical deliverables which will be used directly by the organization. 

Assignments And Key Activities


Support dashboard creation to support day to day work of R&D functions 
Support digital transformation across the R&D team (e.g. communication about changes- identification of digital tools required by R&D team) 
Investigate new tools and trends in Digital world 
Participate & contribute into meetings related to Digital strategy 
Collaboration with Digital Ambassadors 


Basic Requirements For All

You should have strong communication skills and fluency in English, both written and verbal (Dutch is not a requirement but other languages are a plus) 


EU - Students: Need to be enrolled at a Dutch university for at least 50% of the duration of the internship.
Non - EU Students: Need to be enrolled at a Dutch University for the whole duration of the internship.


NOTE: EU working rights are required (Either you have EU Citizenship, you are studying in the Netherlands or have relevant student work visa) 

Specific Team Requirements


Master’s or Bachelor’s student in Business Analytics, Data Science, Statistics, or a related field.
Proficiency in SQL
Data visualization skills (Power BI is a big plus)
Being comfortable with working with big data
Having a structured personality with ability to learn fast
Previous internship with data visualization or experience with data analytics is a plus


Recruitment Process


CV review – We screen applications for key eligibility criteria (e.g., EU working rights).
Online Pymetrics assessment
Screening interview – An initial interview conducted via Microsoft Teams.
Hiring Manager interview
Offer – Successful candidates will receive an offer to join the program.


Please note: The recruitment process can take 4–6 weeks in total, from application to offer.

What We Offer You

We take interns seriously. You can expect us to give you 1-on-1 mentorship from one of our experienced colleagues. We will work together on your personal learning goals and are keen to hear all the good ideas you have! We will give you the space and support to make mistakes and grow. Become a part of a close intern community as well as a young and ambitious work environment (including online).

As a Kraft Heinz intern you’ll be the owner of your own projects and especially of your own career. At Kraft Heinz, we provide the playing field. You change the game. Are you up for the challenge?   

About Us 

The Kraft Heinz Company is one of the largest food and beverage companies in the world, with eight $1 billion+ brands and global sales of approximately $25 billion. We’re a globally trusted producer of high-quality, great-tasting, and nutritious foods for over 150 years. Our brands are truly global, with products produced and marketed in over 40 countries. These beloved products include condiments and sauces, cheese and dairy, meals, meats, refreshment beverages, coffee, infant and nutrition products, and numerous other grocery products in a portfolio of more than 200 legacy and emerging brands. 

We spark joy around mealtime with our iconic brands, including Heinz, Kraft, Bull's-Eye, HP, Lea and Perrins, Quero, ABC, Master, Banquete, Orlando, Benedicta, Honig, Pudliszki, Wattie’s among others. 

No matter the brand, we’re united under one vision: To sustainably grow by delighting more consumers globally. Bringing this vision to life is our team of 39,000+ food lovers, creative thinkers, and high performers worldwide. Together, we help provide meals to those in need through our global partnership with Rise Against Hunger. We also stand committed to responsible, sustainable practices that extend to every facet of our business, our consumers, and our communities. Every day, we’re transforming the food industry with bold thinking and unprecedented results. If you share our passion – and are ready to create the future, build a legacy, and lead as a global citizen – there’s only one thing to do: join our table and let’s make life delicious! 

Our Culture of Ownership, Meritocracy & Collaboration 

We're not afraid to think differently. Embrace new ideas. Dream big. We empower our people at every level – from entry-level intern to senior leader – to own their work. We share a responsibility to think like Owners – to be mindful of the collective and sustained success of Kraft Heinz – which we apply to every situation, every day. As part of Kraft Heinz, you're supported to grow and achieve. You’re expected to bring your authentic self to work every day, to lead with humility, and drive outstanding performance at every level – and you’ll be rewarded. You’re given opportunities to leave a mark and build a legacy. But you won’t do it alone.  You’re supported by passionate teammates along the way, and our collective, collaborative spirit fuels our incredible progress. 

Location(s)

Amsterdam

Kraft Heinz is an Equal Opportunity Employer – Underrepresented Ethnic Minority Groups/Women/Veterans/Individuals with Disabilities/Sexual Orientation/Gender Identity and other protected classes.

Title: Data analist
Description:
Het geven van extra zekerheid over de werking van de beheersingsmaatregelen in alle processen. Daarvoor staat het team Verbijzonderde Interne Controle (VIC), met jou als data-analist, in Apeldoorn/Utrecht of vanaf je werkplek (hybride).

Je werkt binnen Centrale Functies, onderdeel van de directie Centraal Administratieve Processen. Op dit moment zijn we bezig met de toekomstige organisatorische inbedding van de VIC- functie binnen de Belastingdienst. Deze moet in 2027 zijn vormgegeven. Het kan zijn dat dat invloed gaat hebben op vanuit welke directie je je werkzaamheden gaat uitvoeren.

Dit doe je bij de Belastingdienst

Als data-analist verzamel, zuiver en interpreteer je grote hoeveelheden gegevens om patronen en trends te ontdekken. Je vertaalt ruwe data naar bruikbare inzichten en rapportages, waarmee de organisatie datagedreven beslissingen kan nemen om processen te verbeteren, de efficiëntie te verhogen of strategische doelen te behalen.

Kernactiviteiten Van De Data-analist


Data verzamelen en opschonen: gegevens uit diverse bronnen (bijv. belastingmiddelen- en -procesapplicaties) halen en onjuistheden verwijderen.
Analyseren: patronen, trends en correlaties in de data ontdekken met behulp van statistische methoden en modellen.
Visualisatie en rapportage: complexe resultaten omzetten in begrijpelijke dashboards en rapportages, vaak met tools als Power BI, SQL en dergelijke.
Adviseren: de inzichten presenteren aan stakeholders en adviseren over acties om processen & efficiëntie te verbeteren.


Je verzorgt daarnaast de periodieke aanlevering van selecties en cijferbeoordelingen voor controles, waaronder steekproeven. Je ontsluit hiervoor de data uit de diverse applicaties voor de belastingmiddelen en -processen. Je ondersteunt op het gebied van procesanalyse en data-analyse. Bij operationele processen zet je data uit om inzicht te krijgen in de kwaliteit. Ook adviseer je hoeveel en welke posten moeten worden gecontroleerd om betrouwbare uitspraken te doen over het totaal. Verder geef je aan welke handmatige interne controles volgens jou kunnen worden geautomatiseerd.

Een bijzondere baan, omdat...


je een breed werkpakket hebt waarbij jouw zelfstandigheid en flexibiliteit volledig tot z’n recht komt.
je bijdraagt aan analyses die worden afgestemd met auditmanagers en controleleiders.
je zowel een controlerende als adviserende rol vervult.
je alle mogelijkheden krijgt om als mens en professional verder te groeien.


“Binnen de Belastingdienst analyseer ik data voor interne onderzoeken. Met de informatie die dat oplevert kunnen we de effectiviteit en efficiency van organisatieprocessen beoordelen. Maar ook cijferbeoordelingen uitvoeren en vaststellen of de verwerking van transactiegegevens betrouwbaar is.” Peter, auditor data-analist.

Wanneer past deze baan bij je?

Je hebt hbo-werk- en -denkniveau.

Je hebt ervaring met data-analyse en kennis van statistiek.

Je hebt bij voorkeur een gedegen kennis van SAS, EG of Base, Excel en SQL.

Je hebt bij voorkeur kennis van de belastingmiddelen en/of de bronbestanden.

Jouw competenties

samenwerken

plannen en organiseren

creativiteit

analyseren

resultaatgerichtheid

De afdeling - Directie Centrale Administratieve Processen

De directie Centrale Administratieve Processen (CAP) is het kloppend hart van de Belastingdienst. Iedereen in Nederland heeft met ons te maken. Jaarlijks versturen en verwerken wij meer dan 316 miljoen brieven, e-mails, aanslagen en aangiften. De verantwoordelijkheid voor de ontvangst, verwerking en verzending van alle fiscale gegevens van én aan burgers en bedrijven ligt bij ons. CAP is georganiseerd in vier onderdelen: Gegevens, Heffing, Inning en Centrale Functies.

Samen zorgen zij ervoor dat iedereen in Nederland een aanslagbiljet krijgt, aangifte kan doen of een toeslag ontvangt. Om al deze gegevens op een verantwoordelijke en juiste manier te kunnen verwerken voert CAP miljoenen handelingen door, grotendeels geautomatiseerd. Onze ambitie? Massaal persoonlijk maken. Door zoveel mogelijk te digitaliseren, optimaliseren en slim gebruik te maken van data kunnen we dit mogelijk maken. Dit vraagt om medewerkers die mee- en vooruitdenken, nieuwsgierig zijn en zichzelf continu willen blijven ontwikkelen. Kijk voor meer info: https://werken.belastingdienst.nl/centrale-administratieve-processen-cap.

De organisatie

Belastingdienst

Bij de Belastingdienst werken ongeveer 25.000 mensen verspreid over het hele land aan een financieel gezond Nederland. Dit doen we door eerlijk en zorgvuldig belastingen en premies volksverzekeringen te heffen, te innen en te controleren en fraude op te sporen. De Belastingdienst maakt het burgers en bedrijven zo gemakkelijk mogelijk om verplichtingen na te kunnen komen en gebruik te kunnen maken van rechten. Dat gebeurt met passende dienstverlening, door massale processen juist en tijdig uit te voeren én door adequaat toezicht uit te oefenen. De Belastingdienst is sterk in ontwikkeling en heeft de ambitie om de beste Belastingdienst voor Nederland te zijn met focus op fiscale taken en vakmanschap. Lees meer over de Belastingdienst op: https://over-ons.belastingdienst.nl

Bijzonderheden

We ontvangen je brief en cv graag in een Word- of PDF-bestand. Neem in de bestandsnaam jouw voor- en achternaam op. Als je een beroep doet op een voorrangspositie, stuur dan ook een kopie van die kennisgeving mee.

Onze selectieprocedure voor deze functie ziet er als volgt uit:


Allereerst wordt beoordeeld of een voorrangskandidaat binnen de Rijksoverheid in aanmerking komt voor deze functie.
Daarna wordt beoordeeld of jouw cv en motivatiebrief voldoen aan de gestelde functie-eisen.
Op maandag 8 juni vinden de selectiegesprekken plaats via video-bellen.


Onderaan deze vacature vind je meer uitleg over de stappen die je doorloopt in de sollicitatieprocedure.

De voorkeur gaat uit naar kandidaten die het meest aansluiten bij het opgestelde profiel. Dit heeft betrekking op zowel de genoemde kennis en werkervaring als op het opleidingsniveau. Vermeld deze daarom duidelijk in je cv.

De Belastingdienst zet zich in voor de banenafspraak: zoveel mogelijk banen realiseren voor mensen met een arbeidsbeperking. Val jij in deze doelgroep? Wij zullen je ondersteunen zodat je optimaal kunt functioneren, en daarmee jouw bijdrage aan Nederland kunt leveren. Of het nu gaat om aanpassingen op je werkplek, bepaalde voorzieningen in het vervoer of bijvoorbeeld vergoedingen van het UWV: samen met jou, je leidinggevende én een jobcoach gaan wij kijken naar jouw kwaliteiten en mogelijkheden om een passend werkpakket uit te voeren. Wil jij weten of je hierbij hoort en wat dit exact betekent voor jou? Lees meer over werken met een arbeidsbeperking.

Een (online) voorselectietest en/of een assessment kan deel uit maken van de sollicitatieprocedure, evenals het maken van een casus, het opvragen van referenties en (online) screening, bijvoorbeeld via databanken, (zoekmachines) en sociale netwerken.

Jouw sollicitatie wordt alleen in behandeling genomen als je met de sollicitatie-button reageert en je cv en motivatiebrief zijn bijgesloten. Na sluitingsdatum is het niet meer mogelijk om je sollicitatie in behandeling te nemen. Houd er rekening mee dat het solliciteren technische problemen met zich mee kan brengen. Wacht hiermee dus niet tot het laatste moment.

Wanneer je solliciteert op een functie maak je eenmalig een account aan. Met je account kan je de status van je sollicitatie inzien.

Title: Data Analyst
Description:
Data Steward / Data Governance Consultant (3-Month Contract | Netherlands / Hybrid-Remote)

Overview




We're supporting a major enterprise data transformation program and are looking for an experienced Data Steward / Data Governance Consultant to help shape and improve customer domain data products within a modern Data Mesh environment.

This is a hands-on role focused on data quality, governance frameworks, metadata management, and stakeholder enablement - ensuring customer data products are trusted, discoverable, and built to consistent standards across the organization.




You'll work closely with data engineers, product teams, and business stakeholders to improve data reliability, define governance best practices, and embed a strong data-as-a-product mindset.




Key Responsibilities

Define and maintain data quality rules, validation tests, and monitoring dashboards (primarily using SQL, dbt & Elementary)
Lead root cause analysis on data quality issues, driving corrective actions through to resolution
Maintain and improve business definitions, metadata, and harmonization rules across customer datasets
Review data models, data contracts, and data mappings to ensure alignment with governance standards
Champion data governance best practice, improving discoverability, ownership, and usability of data products
Present data quality insights and governance recommendations clearly to stakeholders
Collaborate closely with Data Engineers, Product Owners, Software Engineers, and Country Data Leads
Support adoption of Data Mesh principles, embedding ownership and accountability into domain data products




Required Experience

Strong hands-on experience in data quality management and implementation
Advanced SQL skills, with experience writing and evaluating dbt data tests
Experience working with data quality frameworks, including rule definition, testing, issue investigation, and remediation
Strong understanding of:
Data modeling
Data warehousing
Metadata management
Data cataloging
Data governance frameworks
Experience using data visualization tools such as Power BI or Looker Studio
Familiarity with cloud environments (ideally GCP)
Python / Regex experience beneficial
Experience working with customer data (profiles, transactions, behavioral events) highly desirable




What Makes Someone Successful in This Role:

✔ Strong analytical and problem-solving capability

✔ Ability to translate business requirements into measurable data quality standards

✔ Comfortable influencing stakeholders across technical and non-technical teams

✔ Proactive, collaborative mindset - someone who can help define the playbook, not just follow it

✔ Passion for Data Governance, Data Mesh, and Data as a Product principles




Contract Length: 3 months (extension likely)

Location: Netherlands / Hybrid-Remote

Start: ASAP

Rate: 65 per hour or equivalent to €80k per year (including holiday, pension and expenses)

Interim payroll contract - No ZZP

HTML:
Data Steward / Data Governance Consultant (3-Month Contract | Netherlands / Hybrid-Remote)

Overview


We're supporting a major enterprise data transformation program and are looking for an experienced Data Steward / Data Governance Consultant to help shape and improve customer domain data products within a modern Data Mesh environment.

This is a hands-on role focused on data quality, governance frameworks, metadata management, and stakeholder enablement - ensuring customer data products are trusted, discoverable, and built to consistent standards across the organization.


You'll work closely with data engineers, product teams, and business stakeholders to improve data reliability, define governance best practices, and embed a strong data-as-a-product mindset.


Key Responsibilities

  • Define and maintain data quality rules, validation tests, and monitoring dashboards (primarily using SQL, dbt & Elementary)
  • Lead root cause analysis on data quality issues, driving corrective actions through to resolution
  • Maintain and improve business definitions, metadata, and harmonization rules across customer datasets
  • Review data models, data contracts, and data mappings to ensure alignment with governance standards
  • Champion data governance best practice, improving discoverability, ownership, and usability of data products
  • Present data quality insights and governance recommendations clearly to stakeholders
  • Collaborate closely with Data Engineers, Product Owners, Software Engineers, and Country Data Leads
  • Support adoption of Data Mesh principles, embedding ownership and accountability into domain data products


Required Experience

  • Strong hands-on experience in data quality management and implementation
  • Advanced SQL skills, with experience writing and evaluating dbt data tests
  • Experience working with data quality frameworks, including rule definition, testing, issue investigation, and remediation
  • Strong understanding of:
  • Data modeling
  • Data warehousing
  • Metadata management
  • Data cataloging
  • Data governance frameworks
  • Experience using data visualization tools such as Power BI or Looker Studio
  • Familiarity with cloud environments (ideally GCP)
  • Python / Regex experience beneficial
  • Experience working with customer data (profiles, transactions, behavioral events) highly desirable


What Makes Someone Successful in This Role:

✔ Strong analytical and problem-solving capability

✔ Ability to translate business requirements into measurable data quality standards

✔ Comfortable influencing stakeholders across technical and non-technical teams

✔ Proactive, collaborative mindset - someone who can help define the playbook, not just follow it

✔ Passion for Data Governance, Data Mesh, and Data as a Product principles


Contract Length: 3 months (extension likely)

Location: Netherlands / Hybrid-Remote

Start: ASAP

Rate: 65 per hour or equivalent to €80k per year (including holiday, pension and expenses)

Interim payroll contract - No ZZP

Title: Data Analyst, Demand Planning
Description:
What To Expect
The Data Analyst – Demand Planning, EMEA Service Parts role is responsible for Analytics to support the Demand Planning for Service Parts in EMEA. The role will be in charge of performing analytical deep dives, building new data models and generating insights to support strategic decision making for the team. The role will be a link between the Demand Planners and Data Scientists in the team and will work with a wide range of stakeholders from Material and Inventory Planning to Parts and Service teams.

The role requires solid technical skills and the ability to bridge the gap between business requirements and a complex data landscape. Additionally, a successful candidate needs to be able to communicate insights clearly to a wide audience.

What You'll Do


Perform analyses and propose solutions to improve the overall quality of our forecasts
Build KPIs and data models to support the analytical requests of our stakeholders and automate existing workflows where possible
Collaborate with stakeholders from Material/Inventory Planning, NPI, Reliability Engineering and Parts and Service teams
Communicate insights clearly to a wide audience including senior leadership



What You'll Bring


Degree in an analytical area such as Operations Research, Statistics or Data Science
Proficiency in SQL and Python
Continuous improvement & growth mindset. Strong intellectual curiosity, and ability to structurally analyze problems and propose data-driven solutions
Fluent in written and spoken English



, Tesla

Title: Internship 26' - Business Intelligence
Description:
Are you ready to kick-start your career at one of the world’s most iconic companies?

At Unilever, we’re not just about creating products – we’re about building dreams, opportunities, and innovation. With over 400 brands like Dove, Knorr, and Lipton, we’re committed to making sustainable living commonplace.

This is more than just an internship; it’s a chance to learn, grow, and leave your mark at one of the largest FMCG companies on the planet.

What makes this internship exciting?

As an intern in the Business Intelligence Department, your ideas will drive impact, and your creativity will take center stage.

The team will provide you with opportunities to support projects that will help you gain hands-on experience in data analysis, modeling, and developing data-driven solutions, enhance skills in collaborating with cross-functional teams and communicating data insights effectively.

This isn’t just about learning – it’s about leading! Here’s what your journey will look like:


Collect, analyze, and interpret large datasets to provide actionable insights.
Develop and maintain dashboards and reports to track key performance indicators (KPIs) and business metrics.
Create visual representations of data to communicate findings effectively to stakeholders.
Work closely with cross-functional teams to understand their data needs and provide solutions.
Ensure data quality and integrity by managing data sources and performing regular audits.
Identify opportunities to improve data processes and implement best practices.
Learn and apply tools and technologies such as SQL, Power BI, Tableau, and Excel.
Assist in various projects by providing data-driven insights and recommendations.


You’re The Perfect Fit If


You’re available for 5 to 6 months starting August-September 2026 (minimum 32 hours/week).
You’re enrolled (3rd or 4th year) in Bachelor or Master of Economics, Econometrics, or Data Science.
You have strong problem-solving and analytical skills.
Experience with SQL and/or Python is a plus.
You have experience with PowerBI.
Experience with AI (LLM or ML) is a plus.
You demonstrate innovation and creativity.
You have advanced Excel skills.
You are fluent in English.


Why you’ll love working with us?

We know internships are all about growth and exploration – and we’ve got you covered.


A €625 gross/month allowance.
Travel reimbursement (if you don’t have a student travel card).
Fun and engaging intern programs – onboarding, events, and activities with fellow interns.
Access to extensive training programs for personal and professional growth.
A flexible working environment tailored to your success.
A behind-the-scenes experience at one of the world’s largest and most innovative FMCG companies.


Application Process


Click "Apply now" and upload your CV and motivation letter. Don't forget to complete the questionnaire we send you. It's your chance to shine!
Our team will carefully review your application. We receive a lot of applications, so please bear with us as we give each one the attention it deserves.
If we think you might be a great fit, we'll schedule a friendly phone call to get to know you better and hear about your motivations.
We'll present your profile to the manager. If they’re impressed, you'll be invited for an interview to discuss the opportunity further.
After the interview, the manager will provide feedback on your performance. If you are selected, you'll receive an offer letter with all the exciting details about your internship.
Once you accept the offer, we'll guide you through the onboarding process, including any necessary paperwork, training sessions, and introductions to your awesome team.
You'll kick off your internship, get integrated into the team, and start working on your cool projects and responsibilities.


Unilever is more than a workplace – it’s a space to bring your passions to life while creating impact. Are you ready to be part of something extraordinary? Let’s make it happen together!

Title: Business Data Analyst
Description:
Assignment description




Are you a data analyst who thrives in ambiguity? Someone who can connect fragmented systems, build a structure where there isn't any, and turn raw HR data into decisions that can move the needle?




A Dutch national infrastructure company is looking for a Business Data Analyst to lead the data-driven transformation of their talent and recruitment function. This is a high-impact role sitting at the intersection of HR, recruitment, and data strategy.




The challenge




Inflow, throughput, and outflow data live alone. ATS and HR systems do not talk to each other. Strategic decisions are being made without trustable insight. You come in and fix that, end to end.




What you will be doing




Mapping intern to permanent conversion flows and full employee journeys
Building Power BI dashboards that actually get used by leadership
Bridging ATS and HR systems and connecting fragmented data sources
Defining KPIs, cleaning recruitment data, and setting up structural reporting
Advising senior stakeholders and translating insights into concrete action
Running ad hoc analyses on labour market and recruitment topics, often via SAS




What we are looking for




Experience as a data or business analyst
Strong Power BI skills, building dashboards independently rather than just maintaining them
Experience with HR and/or recruitment data
Comfortable with web or marketing data
Process mapping experience in Visio or a similar tool




Why this role?




This is not a dashboard maintenance role. You are laying the analytical foundation for how one of the Netherlands' largest public organizations plans its workforce for the next decade. Real scope. Real impact.




Interested? Drop me a message or apply directly and I would love to tell you more.

Title: PowerBI Specialist
Description:
Wil jij met jouw data-skills écht impact maken op werkgeluk, arbeidsomstandigheden en gezondheid? Dan hebben wij een mooie plek voor je.




Wij zijn een kleine, betrokken organisatie die zich inzet voor het verbeteren van werkgeluk, arbeidsomstandigheden en vitaliteit bij onze aangesloten klanten. We werken in een hecht team met korte lijnen, veel ruimte voor eigen initiatief en een sterke focus op maatschappelijke impact.




Wat ga je doen?

Als Power BI Specialist ben jij dé schakel tussen data en inzicht. Je helpt ons én onze klanten om beter onderbouwde keuzes te maken op het gebied van inzetbaarheid en welzijn.

Ontwikkelen en beheren van dashboards en rapportages in Power BI
Vertalen van data naar heldere inzichten en adviezen
Samenwerken met collega’s en klanten om informatiebehoeften scherp te krijgen
Ontsluiten, modelleren en transformeren van data (o.a. met SQL)
Meedenken over en verbeteren van onze data-architectuur




Functie eisen

Aantoonbare ervaring met Power BI (modelleren, DAX, visualisaties)
Goede kennis van SQL
Ervaring met of interesse in Azure en/of Databricks is een pré
Analytisch sterk en in staat om complexe data begrijpelijk te maken
Proactieve werkhouding en plezier in samenwerken binnen een klein team

Salaris tussen de €4.500 en €6.000,- Afhankelijk van kennis en ervaring

vaste 13e maand
Hybride werken(1 vaste kantoor dag)
Goede pensioenregeling
Werken in een klein en sociaal team

Title: Data Analyst
Description:
Senior Data Analist (Zuid-Holland)




🚀 Over de rol

Voor een van onze partners in Zuid-Holland zijn wij op zoek naar een Data Analist die een belangrijke rol gaat spelen binnen een recent gebouwd data analytics platform.

Binnen deze functie maak je onderdeel uit van het data team en werk je actief mee aan het verder professionaliseren en uitbreiden van dit platform. Je hebt een sterke focus op data analyse en draagt bij aan het ontwikkelen van data-gedreven inzichten voor de business.




🎯 Wat ga je doen?

Meedenken over de verdere ontwikkeling en professionalisering van het data platform
Analyseren van data en vertalen naar concrete business inzichten
Bouwen van dashboards voor de nieuwe data-omgeving (Power BI & Microsoft Fabric)
Ontwikkelen van data modellen en logica in SQL (en eventueel Python)
Opzetten en onderhouden van datasets die dagelijks accuraat en betrouwbaar zijn
Optimaliseren en automatiseren van dataflows en processen
Samenwerken met Data Engineers en BI Developers aan data-gedreven oplossingen




🧠 Jouw profiel

Bachelor of Master opleiding afgerond
4-5 jaar ervaring als Data Analist, BI Developer of Analytics Engineer
Sterke ervaring met dashboarding & reporting (Power BI)
Sterke kennis van SQL (Python is mooi meegenomen)
Ervaring met cloud omgevingen (Bij voorkeur Azure Fabric)
Sterk analytisch vermogen en communicatief vaardig
Proactieve houding en een echte “can-do” mentaliteit
Goeie stakeholder management ervaring




🌱 Wat bieden wij?

💰 Een goed salaris
🛫 25 vakantiedagen
🎒 Flexibiliteit in hybride werken (thuis & kantoor)
💻 MacBook en iPhone
📊 Uitdagende projecten binnen data en analytics
📚 Trainingsbudget om je technische skills verder te ontwikkelen waar nodig
🤝 Mentorship en begeleiding vanuit ervaren data professionals tijdens je projecten




🏢 Over ons

Xccelerated is onderdeel van de Xebia Group en richt zich op het versnellen van groei binnen Data, Cloud & AI. Wij werken nauw samen met onze partners en investeren in zowel projecten als de ontwikkeling van onze mensen.




📩 Interesse?

Lijkt dit je een interessante volgende stap? We maken graag kennis met je!

sgoncalves@xccelerated.io of 0613889770

Title: Data Engineer
Description:
🔔 Data Engineer

💰 €75k - €100k + 5–25% annual bonus

📍 Amsterdam / Utrecht / Rotterdam




Most Data Engineering roles keep the lights on. This one helps decide billion-euro deals.




If you're a Data Engineer who wants real ownership, high-stakes technical challenges, and direct business impact, this is the kind of role that rarely comes to market. A leading boutique in data-driven financial due diligence is hiring a Data Engineer to help transform how M&A decisions are made.




Every year, their teams support 150+ acquisition investigations for private equity firms, investment funds, and corporates, using data, AI, and cloud technology to uncover what others miss.

This isn’t another “build pipelines and maintain dashboards” job.




This is your chance to sit at the core of a highly specialised Data Science function, building the infrastructure that powers live deal intelligence in one of the most intense environments in finance.




What makes this Data Engineer role different?

You’ll be:

✅ Designing cloud-native data architectures

✅ Building scalable pipelines from the ground up

✅ Developing tools that give analysts a decisive edge during live M&A negotiations

✅ Making real architectural decisions from day one

✅ Embedding AI into financial analysis workflows

You’ll also collaborate on a proprietary BI platform already used by leading investment firms across the Netherlands.




What the successful Data Engineer needs:

✔️ 3+ years in Data Engineering or Data Architecture.

✔️ Strong Python, SQL, and Azure expertise.

✔️ An entrepreneurial mindset and confidence working autonomously.

✔️ Fluency in Dutch and English.




🎁 What’s on offer:

💰 €75k - €100k + 5–25% annual bonus

📍 Amsterdam / Utrecht / Rotterdam

🏡 Hybrid working after onboarding (2–3 office days)

🏖️ 30 days holiday

🤝 High-performance, close-knit culture with genuine team energy

👤 Who they’re looking for




Plus something engineers genuinely value: the freedom to make the right technical decisions yourself.




Why this stands out for a Data Engineer:

🔍 Real complexity — every dataset, deal, and deadline is different

🤖 AI at the core — automation and intelligence built into workflows

🏛️ Unique domain — M&A due diligence is fast-moving, technical, and commercially critical

🛠️ Technical autonomy — shape the engineering direction, not just execute tickets

📈 Clear progression — senior engineering or technical leadership in a scaling business




💬 If you’re ready to build systems that influence major investment decisions, let’s talk.

Title: Data Analyst
Description:
At BESTSELLER, we believe better decisions start with better data. And better data comes from people who know how to turn complexity into clarity.

We’re looking for a Data Analyst to join our international E-commerce team in Amsterdam. Someone who enjoys digging into data, building solid foundations, and helping teams make smarter decisions every day.

If you like connecting business questions with data solutions and want to work where fashion meets tech, keep reading.

What’s the role about?

As a Data Analyst, you’ll sit right in the middle of business and tech. You’ll work with teams across Marketplaces, Logistics, and Finance, helping them understand what’s happening, why it’s happening, and what to do next.

From raw data to real impact, you’ll own the full journey.

At BESTSELLER, we believe in freedom with responsibility. You’ll have the space to shape your work, take ownership, and grow your skills along the way.

What you’ll be working on


Partner with stakeholders to turn business questions into clear analytical problems
Build and maintain data models that teams can trust and use daily
Transform raw data into actionable insights
Connect the dots between systems, teams, and data sources
Own technical requirements and keep documentation clear and structured
Help teams make better, faster decisions through data


What you bring

You don’t need to know everything, but you do need curiosity, structure, and a strong sense of ownership.

You’ll likely bring:


3–5 years’ experience in a Data Analyst or similar role
Experience with modern data tools like Snowflake, dbt, and Airflow
Strong skills in data visualisation (Power BI or similar)
The ability to explain complex topics in a clear, simple way
A structured mindset because you like building things that last
A proactive attitude: you take ownership and don’t wait to be told what to do


Nice to have:


Experience with Python or scripting
Exposure to AI tools in data workflows
Knowledge of e-commerce business models


Your team

You’ll join a diverse, international team working across brands like VERO MODA, ONLY, SELECTED, and JACK & JONES all supported from our Amsterdam hub.

We collaborate closely, challenge each other, and move fast, always with the goal of building better solutions together.

Why BESTSELLER?

Because here, you can own your work. You’ll have the freedom to explore, improve, and build while being supported by a team that values trust, feedback, and growth.

You’ll work in a fast-moving environment where data has real impact, not just dashboards, but decisions.

What you’ll get


Hybrid working model and flexible hours
27 holidays + option to buy 5 more
50% discount on all BESTSELLER brands
Travel allowance
Collective health insurance or a monthly allowance
Access to OpenUp (wellbeing support)
Daily lunch at the office
Plenty of learning and development opportunities


Please note that for this role, you need to be eligible to work in the EU.

We are BESTSELLER. And together, we own it.

About BESTSELLER E-Commerce

At BESTSELLER, we are more than 25,000 people working for over 25 fashion brands such as JACK & JONES, SELECTED, VERO MODA, ONLY, PIECES, VILA, OBJECT, MAMALICIOUS, NAME IT, NOISY MAY and Y.A.S. At BESTSELLER E-Commerce, we are an international team of 170 people from more than 40 nationalities with the purpose to drive all e-commerce functions at BESTSELLER. With our office in the heart of Amsterdam as well as locations in Poland and Denmark, we are supporting all fashion brands of the BESTSELLER family and sell online in 15 countries around the world. We work in an environment where fashion meets technology. Challenging ourselves to offer the best experience to our customers and stakeholders and to work with the latest technologies is what drives us. As a team, we value autonomy and flexibility. We thrive in a flat organisation, where everyone has the chance to grow and learn. For more information, please go to www.bestseller.com.

Title: Data Controller
Description:
Location: Hengelo, Netherlands

Are you looking for a role where accuracy, cooperation and helpfulness are central? We are looking for an enthusiastic Data Controller for our team. Someone who takes charge of our data with a smile.

About The Team

As part of the supportability engineering group (ca. 65 domain experts) within the Services Segment, you will be member of a great and inspiring team of Logistic Engineering and Obsolescence Management consisting of approximately 22 colleagues.

You enjoy working in a team and are a facilitator in the broadest sense.

About The Job

The main part of the work consists of control and maintaining our database which are essential for delivering our services to our customers. Consistency and accuracy are essential here.

The work requires knowledge of several standards which are used in the field of Logistic Engineering within a military environment. You should find it inspiring and challenging to acquire, maintain, and expand this knowledge and experience to become a professional in this field. To gain this knowledge you will be supported by your team members and additional trainings.

You're the main contact with the agencies responsible for disseminating and maintaining the required standards and regulations.

A technical education is not a requirement, but affinity within a technical environment is beneficial.

Personality and attitude (fitting in the team) is of more importance than the specific knowledge of handling our databases, you need to have the capability and eagerness to adapt this knowledge during your period of training on the job.

About You


We're looking for a candidate with a degree on MBO/HBO level.
Knowledge and experience with controlling and maintaining databases. (i.e. Windchill, Oracle, Teamcenter)
Strong communicative and social skills with a high degree of initiative, creativity, pragmatism and persuasiveness;
Proactive and facilitating attitude.
Accurate and well-organized.
Interested in maintenance and service logistics (e.g. corrective, preventive and condition-based maintenance, Integrated Logistics Support)
You like working in a new and challenging environment and contributing to the further development of our team.
Proficiency in English, both spoken and written.



What's In It For You

A role with a lot of freedom and responsibility.

An inspiring work environment with enthusiastic colleagues.

Room for initiative and personal growth.


A challenging job in a great team within our high-tech, innovative work environment. Includes the freedom for initiative and good ideas.
Room for personal and professional growth within our organization, for example in regards to technical, commercial or international skills.
Valuable international collaboration with coworkers from 68 different countries around the globe.
A comfortable work space in a modern environment, with a focus on flexibility and the right work life balance.
You can usually work where and whenever you want. Within Thales, we aim for a 60/40 balance, where you work from home at least two days a week, when your role allows this.
A good salary, fitting travel allowance, and the ability to personalize your employment conditions: for instance, you can trade PTO for internet costs, our bicycle plan, company fitness and relocation costs.
40 days off per year (PTO).



Interested?

Apply now! Click on the button below to upload your profile and show your interest. For questions you may reach out to our Talent Acquisition Partner, Laurence Janssen at laurence.janssen@external.thalesgroup.com.

To ensure that we are a good match, we can ask you to participate in an assessment.

Diversity Statement

We actively support a working pattern that suits your lifestyle and helps you reach your ambitions. That means that equal opportunities, inclusion and an informal culture are integral to our success. It also means that your well-being and happiness matter to us! That’s why we offer you the flexibility to do what’s important to you; whether that’s part time hours, job sharing, remote working, or the ability to flex your start and finish times.

Security Requirements

In line with Thales' Baseline Security requirements, candidates will be asked to provide evidence of identity, eligibility to work in The Netherlands and employment and/or education history. Some vacancies may require a security clearance which can require further evidence to be provided. For further details of the evidence required to apply for security clearance please refer to the General Intelligence and Security Service.

Title: Global BI & Analytics Internship
Description:
GLOBAL BI & ANALYTICS INTERNSHIP

AMSTERDAM, COTY HQ

About Coty

At Coty, we are seeking motivated Global BI & Analytics Interns to join our team as part of Coty’s Data Talent Factory, a structured 6-month rotation program designed for students eager to build hands-on skills in data, analytics, and generative AI. You’ll spend your first 2 months training with the Global BI team at our HQ in Amsterdam-Zuid, then rotate through 2 business analytics teams (2 months each), applying your skills to real projects across the organization.

We’re Coty, one of the world’s largest beauty companies with an iconic portfolio of brands across fragrance, color cosmetics, and skin and body care. Coty is the global leader in fragrance and number three in color cosmetics. Coty’s products are sold in over 150 countries around the world. Coty and its brands are committed to a range of social causes as well as seeking to minimize its impact on the environment.

About The Business Facing BI Team In Amsterdam

The Business Facing BI team plays a pivotal role in implementing a variety of business intelligence and data analytics solutions to enable data-driven decision-making globally. We actively set and refine Power BI guidelines to ensure scalable, secure, and innovative BI practices across Coty. Although our team is small, we are diverse with members based in Amsterdam and New York City. We embrace continuous learning and rapid adoption of cutting-edge technologies. We are actively exploring how generative AI is reshaping the way we build and deliver analytics. As an intern, you’ll be part of that journey.

What We Offer

At Coty, we offer an exceptional opportunity for our Global BI & Analytics interns to gain hands-on experience in a vibrant and dynamic environment.


Months 1-2: Training in Power BI and Databricks fundamentals, with a focus on Coty’s standards and best practices. Includes exploration of generative AI capabilities within these platforms.
Months 3-4: First project rotation with a business analytics team.
Months 5-6: Second project rotation with a different business analytics team.
By the end of the program, you’ll have a portfolio of real deliverables, cross-functional exposure, and a strong foundation for a career in data & analytics. You will receive direct mentorship from the Business Facing Power BI Manager, fostering both personal and professional growth. Additionally, you’ll benefit from an inclusive, supportive work environment that encourages collaboration and innovation.


Read a testimonial from a former intern about their time with the Global BI & Analytics team:

“My internship with the Global BI & Analytics team was truly unique. It went far beyond dashboards and data, offering a deep dive into strategy, stakeholder management, and the complexities behind scalable BI products. I was exposed to projects across finance, HR, and transformation teams, which helped me understand the company better while building a strong foundation that supports my current role as a Supply Finance Analyst.” – Esmat Fayaz, Supply Finance Analyst

ARE YOU A COTY FIT


Student (Bachelor/Master), it is important that you remain enrolled with your university.
Demonstrated passion for data and an interest in specializing in Power BI and Databricks.
Strong analytical skills and proficiency with numbers.
Familiarity with data analysis tools such as Excel, Python, SQL, and R is highly advantageous.
Strong interest in generative AI and its practical applications in data & analytics.
Adaptability and willingness to work across different teams and business domains.
Keen eye for detail to ensure high-quality, aesthetically pleasing outputs.
Excellent verbal and written communication skills in English.


Our Benefits


Great opportunities to work across multiple analytics teams and manage end-to-end projects.
Internship allowance, NS business card (if you were not eligible for a student public transport card), staff discount of Coty products, access to Coty Academy.
Convenient office location (near Amsterdam Zuid station) with lots of fun in-house events.
First-hand knowledge about the beauty industry.


Period

This internship is full-time (40 hours per week), for a duration of 5-6 months and starts from Summer 2026. Remote working is possible up to 50% of your time, in alignment with your line manager. You will receive an internship allowance and NS Business Card if you do not have a student public transport card. The internship can be combined with a thesis but be aware that this will largely have to be done in your own time.

INTERESTED?

At Coty, we believe in equality and inclusion for all, and stand together with those fighting for freedom, justice, and equal rights regardless of who you are or who you love.

Our people make us who we are. They are the brightest minds bringing the boldest ideas to life every day. If you’re the kind of person who refuses to settle, if you want to think bigger, learn faster and go further than you ever thought possible – we’d love to hear from you.

Please send us your motivation letter together with your CV, stating your availability and for how long.

Title: Data Analyst
Description:
As Data Analyst, you play a key role in driving data-informed decision-making across one of LYNX’s most strategic product and business domains.

We are looking for four Data Analysts to join one of our core squads: Onboarding, Demand Capture, Client Experience, or Client & Product Integrity.


Onboarding Squad: Analyze and improve the customer onboarding journey to make it faster, smoother, and more efficient.
Demand Capture Squad: Use data to understand customer interest and help turn prospects into qualified leads and customers.
Client Experience Squad: Analyze client behavior, feedback, and platform usage to improve the trading experience, increase loyalty, and reduce churn.
Client and Product Integrity Squad: Support smarter, data-driven compliance and due diligence processes tailored to client needs and risk profiles.


In this role, you will help turn data into actionable insights that improve client journeys, optimize commercial performance, strengthen compliance processes, and support better business decisions.

You will own the full lifecycle of data-driven discovery, from connecting and exploring data to modelling, visualizing, and deploying solutions. Your work will directly influence how we grow, serve, and protect our clients.

What You’ll Do


End-to-End Analytics Ownership: Own the full process of data-driven discovery, from sourcing and exploring data to modelling, dashboarding, and delivering scalable solutions.
Business Problem Solving: Translate complex business challenges into practical, data-based solutions that create measurable impact.
Dashboard & KPI Development: Build dashboards, reporting frameworks, and tracking models that support decision-making and performance management over time.
Stakeholder Partnership: Work closely with Product, Marketing, Operations, Compliance, and Commercial teams to understand priorities and turn questions into insights.
Data Exploration & Insight Generation: Analyze structured and unstructured datasets to identify patterns, risks, opportunities, and growth levers.
Experimentation & Optimization: Support testing, experimentation, and continuous improvement initiatives with strong analytical input.
Data Quality & Governance: Ensure data accuracy, consistency, and reliability across reporting and analytics outputs.
Squad Impact Contribution: Depending on your squad, contribute to improving onboarding flows, lead conversion, client loyalty, churn reduction, or smarter compliance processes.﻿


Who You Are

You are a curious and proactive analyst who enjoys solving business problems with data. You combine technical skills with commercial awareness and communicate insights in a clear and practical way.


You have strong analytical skills and confidence working with structured and unstructured data
You have solid Python and SQL skills, including advanced querying
You have a proactive, self-driven mindset and take ownership of your work
You communicate clearly and can explain complex topics in a simple way
You have a high standard for quality and strong attention to detail
You are comfortable working with cross-functional stakeholders
You are motivated by continuous improvement and measurable impact


Nice to have


Knowledge of financial markets and products such as options and futures
Experience in fintech, brokerage, or other data-rich environments
Experience with BI tools and dashboarding platforms
Experience working in agile product squads


What Do We Offer


A competitive salary
a fixed 13 th month;
8% holiday allowance;
a pension scheme fully paid by LYNX;
public transport travel allowance;
home office equipment and home office allowance;
continuous growth potential, including a personal training budget of € 2,000 per year to invest at your own discretion;
daily lunch from our chef, beverages & fruit, Friday drinks at our own bar;
Multiple trips & activities


Who We Are

“LYNX helps you invest in yourself.”

“Working at LYNX means joining the LYNX family.”

“The development opportunities make LYNX the best place to work.”

This is what employees say about LYNX — and it reflects exactly what we aim to offer: an environment where you can get the best out of yourself.

From our offices in Amsterdam, Ghent, Berlin, and Prague, we work every day to provide private and professional clients with the best trading opportunities through a fast, user-friendly, and reliable platform.

LYNX is an international FinTech company where around 300 colleagues contribute to client success across Europe in an informal, collaborative, and growth-oriented environment.

We're an equal-opportunity employer. We welcome applications from all suitably qualified and eligible candidates without attention to gender identity/expression, age, racial, ethnic and cultural background, religion and beliefs, sexual orientation/identity, disability or neurodiversity.

Title: Finance Data Analyst Internship
Description:
What To Expect
Start date: August/September 2026

Duration: 6 months

Location: Amsterdam

Fulltime position (40hours a week)

NOTE: YOU MUST BE ENROLLED IN EDUCATION DURING THE ENTIRE INTERNSHIP TO BE CONSIDERED

Join Tesla's Finance team to drive insights that power the sustainable energy revolution. As a Finance Data Analyst Intern, you'll dive into financial and accounting data analysis while exploring AI for process optimization—balancing hands-on data tasks with innovative AI applications in accounting. Collaborate primarily with finance and IT stakeholders (including Data Engineering, Dev, and AI teams) in a fast-paced setting; we value curiosity over experience.

What You'll Do


Gather, clean, and analyze financial data to support team needs in accounting and finance processes
Assist in developing dashboards and data models using BI tools to optimize workflows and insights
Perform ad-hoc analysis and support team on data modeling for finance applications
Help identify opportunities for AI techniques into finance processes, explore tools and support proof of concept and integration
Contribute to AI initiatives by testing agents and evaluating LLM use for automating financial queries and processes
Communicate findings from data and AI projects to broader organizational stakeholders



What You'll Bring


Pursuing a degree in Finance, Data Analytics, Computer Science, or related STEM fields
Strong analytical and problem-solving skills for independent and team work in dynamic environments
Basic finance/accounting knowledge; eagerness to learn data and AI concepts
SQL, Python, or BI tool experience (e.g., Tableau, Power BI) is a plus—we'll provide training
Interest in AI for finance automation; experience with RAG, LLM, Langchain is highly valued for immediate contributions



, Tesla
