HTML:
Are you our new Junior BI Analyst with a passion for data, business support, and developing Insight?

At DESMI, we are strengthening our data and analytics capabilities to support better decision-making across the organization. We are looking for a curious and analytical Junior BI Analyst who thrives on transforming data into meaningful insights and supporting the business with high-quality analysis. In this role, you will play a key part in running our BI support function, ensuring that stakeholders across DESMI have access to reliable data, insightful reporting, and clear answers to their business questions.

DESMI is a dynamic and innovative company where collaboration, continuous learning, and professional development are at the core of our culture. In this role, you will gain hands-on experience with modern BI tools, work closely with stakeholders across functions, and contribute to strengthening our data-driven culture.

A glimpse into the role

As our Junior BI Analyst, you will be the responsible for delivering business intelligence support and analysis. You will work closely with colleagues across departments, helping them understand data, answer key business questions, and translate requirements into impactful Power BI solutions.

You will be part of a dedicated Finance & BI environment and collaborate with both technical and business stakeholders, contributing to the continued development of our DESMI Insights platform.

Your Primary Responsibilities Will Include

  • Running the BI support function: Act as the first point of contact for BI-related questions, supporting users and ensuring they get value from our data and reports
  • Developing Power BI reports: Design, build, and maintain dashboards and reports that provide clear and actionable insights
  • Delivering business analysis: Conduct thorough and structured analyses to support decision-making across the organization
  • Translating business needs into data solutions: Work closely with stakeholders to understand their requirements and turn them into scalable BI solutions
  • Ensuring data quality: Validate and reconcile data to ensure consistency, reliability, and trust in reporting
  • Supporting continuous improvement: Contribute to developing and optimizing our BI platform, data models, and reporting standards Your next opportunity starts here

We are looking for a structured and curious analyst who enjoys diving into data and turning complexity into clarity. You are motivated by understanding the business behind the numbers and delivering insights that make a real impact.

We also imagine that you:

  • Hold a master’s degree in economics, business administration (e.g. Cand.merc), with a strong understanding of business strategy or performance management.
  • Have a solid understanding of how data supports business decision-making, including financial and operational metrics
  • Have experience with or a strong interest in Power BI both analysis and report development
  • Are analytical and detail-oriented, with the ability to perform thorough and well-documented analyses
  • Are proactive and service-minded, enjoying close collaboration with stakeholders across the organization
  • Communicate complex data insights in a clear and understandable way
  • Have a structured approach to problem-solving and a strong sense of ownership

Whether you are at the beginning of your career or have gained some initial experience, this role offers a unique opportunity to develop your skills in a global organization and become a key player in driving data-driven decision-making at DESMI.

The perks of being at DESMI

  • Flexible working hours – including possibilities for remote work, but on-site attendance is encouraged
  • Global career prospects – work with colleagues across Europe, Asia, and the U.S.
  • Skills development and career growth – Training, job rotation, and internal mobility
  • Pension – attractive employer contributions
  • Private healthcare and life insurance
  • On-site canteen – light breakfast, lunch buffet, and snacks
  • Company sports & social activities – from badminton and bowling to art clubs and summer parties

What We’re About

But we’re more than just pumps. At DESMI, we believe teamwork and innovation drive success.

When You Join DESMI, You Can Expect

  • A collaborative and supportive team environment.
  • Opportunities for continuous learning and professional development.
  • A chance to contribute your ideas and make a real impact.
  • A company that values sustainability and innovation.

We are a flat organization with an open-door policy, where decisions are made together, and ideas are always welcome. Here, doers thrive – if you see an opportunity for improvement, you have the freedom to act on it.

As innovators in reliable and sustainable flow solutions, we serve a wide range of industries from Aquaculture and Defense to Green Technologies – the list goes on. Sustainability is highly appreciated while developing high-performance and long-lasting technologies that help industries adapt to evolving markets while reducing environmental impact.

Ready to apply?

If you're a dedicated and motivated individual seeking a challenging and rewarding career where you can contribute to a more sustainable future, we encourage you to apply!

To learn more about this opportunity or to submit your application, please contact Lead BI Manager Mathias Dichmann-Schack (mdsc@desmi.com). We review applications on an ongoing basis and encourage you to apply as soon as possible, as the position will be filled once the right candidate is found.

Title: Head of Insights
Place: Copenhagen, Capital Region of Denmark, Denmark
Description:
About Dreamdata

Dreamdata is the industry leader in B2B marketing attribution and activation. We're on a mission to help B2B marketing leaders finally connect their efforts directly to real revenue — solving a complex problem that has plagued the industry for years.

Founded in 2018 by ex-Trustpilot (TRST:LSE) product and engineering leaders, Dreamdata was born from a real-world need: building the platform they always wished they'd had.

It's working. We have achieved strong product-market fit and are experiencing explosive, triple-digit YoY growth. This momentum is now backed by a recent $55,000,000 Series B funding round set to fuel our rocketship as we scale globally

Requirements

The Opportunity

Dreamdata's software is used by leading B2B companies to do marketing attribution and activation. The byproduct is something rare: a uniquely complete view of how B2B buying actually happens in customer journeys across every channel a modern B2B buyer touches.

We have already started sharing what we see. Our LinkedIn Ads Benchmarks report has been downloaded thousands of times and routinely shows up in third-party newsletters, decks, and LLM answers when people ask how B2B marketing actually performs.

Now we're looking for a Head of Insights to take Dreamdata's data storytelling and thought leadership to the next level. We want to make Dreamdata the canonical, cited source of truth for what's working in B2B marketing. Think of the work that Peter Walker has built at Carta around startup data.

This role sits within our marketing team and reports to the CMO. It is a senior IC role that is both public-facing and deeply technical. You'll spend your first six-to-eight months embedded with our engineering team, learning the data model end-to-end and building the tools you need to do data storytelling at scale so that you're never blocked when an idea is hot. After that you operate with a mandate to be out in the ecosystem finding the right things to say.

If you are motivated by owning a brand-new function at a fast-growing company this is a unique opportunity to have outsized impact through data analysis and storytelling,

You will:

Ship one good chart at a time: Treat a single, well-designed chart as the atomic unit of your work. Publish to LinkedIn (and elsewhere) on a consistent cadence — multiple times per week, working up to daily. Lead with the *why this matters* headline, not the *what the data is*.

Build the production system: so the time from "interesting question" to "shipped chart" keeps falling. Today our marketing team often waits on engineering to run a query; your job is to remove that bottleneck and own the pipeline end-to-end (warehouse → analysis → viz → design → post).

Aggregate the best charts into bigger assets: quarterly benchmark reports, ebooks, virtual events, podcast segments. Use the social channel to learn what the market actually cares about, *then* invest in the long-form.

Earn citations: Build relationships with the 40-50 journalists, newsletter writers, and analysts who cover B2B marketing, so Dreamdata data shows up in their stories. Optimize for LLM-sourcing (AEO/GEO) so we're the answer when buyers ask their AI assistant how B2B marketing performs.

Own Dreamdata's data storytelling function: the strategy, the cadence, the channel mix, and eventually the team.

Must-Have

You:


Have strong analytical engineering skills, including production SQL — you can write the query yourself rather than briefing someone else to write it
Are comfortable setting up and owning data infrastructure (e.g. modeling in a cloud warehouse, building reusable views, owning your own dbt project)
Possess a strong grounding in statistics and data science. Enough to know when a finding is real and to be transparent about sample size and methodology on every chart
Have excellent written and visual communication skills. You can take a messy dataset and produce a chart that's clear at thumbnail size on a phone, with a headline that makes a stranger stop scrolling
Can communicate complex concepts in an approachable way without sacrificing rigor — you'd rather be the impartial observer than start a controversy
Love sharing knowledge on LinkedIn and social media. You already do this or you're keen to get started
Are comfortable using AI tools (Claude, Cursor, etc.) to compress your cycle time. The
Take ownership of outcomes and delivery with little guidance. Nobody is going to hand you a content calendar; you make it
Are genuinely curious about B2B marketing. This is one trait we cannot teach. You should already be reading the newsletters, following the right people, and forming opinions about where the industry is going


Nice-to-Have

Previous experience doing public data storytelling — a portfolio of charts, a personal LinkedIn following, a substack, a past benchmark report you can point to.

Exposure to cloud data warehouses (we use BigQuery on Google Cloud).

Hands-on with Tableau, Figma, or equivalent visualization in one tool, polish in another.

Experience in B2B martech, RevOps, or adjacent (attribution, CDPs, ABM, growth analytics).

A working point of view on B2B SaaS, GTM, and how buyers actually behave.

Comfort representing Dreamdata in person. This can mean speaking at conferences, joining closed-door dinners with marketing leaders, doing podcast appearances.

Benefits


Location: Our office is in the heart of Copenhagen with a harbor view
Ownership: Equity in a fast-growing company
Lunch: Delicious lunch available in our canteen
Inclusive Culture: Be part of a supportive, dynamic team making a real impact in the B2B marketing space
Private insurance: Health insurance that comes with our pension plan
Office dogs


At Dreamdata, you'll have the opportunity to make a real impact. Join us in shaping the future of B2B marketing analytics and activation while developing your skills and growing your career.

We are committed to creating an inclusive, diverse workplace where everyone can thrive. If you're passionate about telling true, useful stories with data — and helping B2B marketers finally see what's working — we'd love to hear from you.

Title: Student Assistant, IT Data Analyst
Place: Brøndby Municipality, Capital Region of Denmark, Denmark
Description:
At Milestone, we pioneer solutions that solve real problems - and we need curious people like you.




Impact & Purpose




Ready to make an impact? In this role, you’ll have the opportunity to learn how data supports real business decisions.




Location




We are looking for a Student Assistant, IT Data Analyst to join the team at our headquarters in Brøndby, Denmark.




Role Summary




As a Student Assistant, IT Data Analyst, you'll support data-related work across the organization, with a focus on the IT department. You'll contribute to data-driven decision-making by managing and analyzing IT data - including costs associated with software, hardware, and other IT resources - and helping ensure accuracy and transparency in this area.




You’ll have room to influence your role as your skills grow. Initially, you’ll focus on creating reports and insights that help decision-makers, and over time you can move closer to data analysis and business discussions.




What You Will Do




Create and Update Reports:




Аssist in creating new reports to address specific needs, guided by stakeholders' requirements.
Collaborate closely with team members to gather and organize data, and contribute to report development through data loading, transformation, and visualization techniques.
Support the updating of existing reports as needs evolve, ensuring accuracy and relevance.




Maintain Data Integrity:




Assist in routine maintenance tasks to ensure data accuracy and consistency.
Proactively identify and troubleshoot errors and communicate resolutions effectively to team members.




Support Other Tools and Platforms:




Provide assistance in creating PowerPoint presentations and Excel spreadsheets as needed.




What You Bring to The Team:




Currently studying a related field within IT, Data Science, Data Analytics or studying business with a flair for data analytics and insights
Comfortable using Power BI for data visualization and reporting.
Familiarity with DAX, the coding language of Power BI, or exposure to SQL basics.
Ability to recognize and perform common data preprocessing tasks, such as data cleaning and transformations
High level of English skills, both verbal and written




How You Will Make a Difference:




You have strong communication skills, both written and verbal, which is vital for collaborating with stakeholders across teams.
You can create clear, user-friendly reports that communicate insights effectively.
You can conduct thorough investigations into the root causes of errors or anomalies.




About the Team




You will be joining the IT Operations & End User Services team within Global IT, responsible for delivering the Digital Workplace and end-user support to all Milestone employees, while ensuring the availability and reliability of our IT services.




As part of a global organization that provides IT solutions across software development, cloud services, cybersecurity, data analytics, and more, you will contribute to a team that safeguards enterprise architecture, drives secure and compliant IT practices, and supports business and customer needs.




You will collaborate with colleagues across EMEA, Americas, and APAC, working in a diverse and multicultural environment where teamwork, knowledge sharing, and different perspectives are valued.




How We Support You:




Individual development plans aligned with your career goals
Access to training, learning resources, and certifications
Flexible working arrangements
Supportive leadership and regular feedback
Opportunities for cross-functional and cross-regional collaboration
Social activities and spaces to connect with colleagues




Excited to join a team that values people and innovation? Hit the Apply button and submit your CV in English today.




You will experience an inclusive and unbiased recruitment process where all employment decisions are based on qualifications rather than race, color, ethnicity, gender identity, age, sexual orientation, and religion. We advise you not to attach a cover letter to your application but instead, we encourage you to write a few lines about why you are applying for the position in your CV. Additionally we recommend you not to add a photo.




About Us




Milestone Systems is a leading provider of data-driven video technology software. Our portfolio includes XProtect video management software, Arcules video surveillance as a service, and BriefCam analytics. We amplify what organizations of any size can see, do, and achieve with video. Guided by our People First mindset-freedom, inclusion, and togetherness-we always consider our employees, partners, and communities in everything we do.

Title: Data Analyst, 2150, Copenhagen
Place: Copenhagen, Capital Region of Denmark, Denmark
Description:
The Company

2150 is a global climate tech investment firm with ~€500m AUM, backing companies reshaping the built environment and urban ecosystem. We work across venture capital, growth investing, and platform initiatives to support the transition to a more sustainable future.

About The Role

We are looking for a Data Analyst to join our Copenhagen team.

This is a hands-on role sitting close to the operational core of the firm. You will work directly with the COO and our Data Lead across reporting, fund operations, data workflows, CRM maintenance, and day-to-day execution. With this hire, the operational back-office team will consist of three people in total.

The role is ideal for someone early in their career who enjoys bringing structure to complexity, has strong attention to detail, and gets genuine satisfaction from making systems run smoothly.

This is not an investment role. It is an operations and data role for someone who enjoys building reliable processes, improving workflows, and helping a fast-moving organization operate at a high level.

From quarterly and annual reports to investor lists, data gathering, CRM upkeep, and operational clean-up work, you will help keep the engine running- and take pride in doing it well. You see well-designed automations and clean data as a genuine competitive advantage, not just a nice-to-have.

Key Responsibilities


Support quarterly, annual, and bespoke investor reporting
Gather, clean, organize, and process data across internal systems
Help maintain and improve CRM infrastructure and operational workflows
Support fund operations and day-to-day back-office processes
Build lightweight automations and improve operational efficiency where possible
Assist with ad hoc operational and administrative tasks across the firm
Work closely with the COO and Data Lead on execution-heavy, detail-oriented projects


Your Experience & Skills

You have a strong academic foundation in finance, economics, or a related quantitative field – for example, a master’s degree in Finance/Economics. Some foundational exposure to venture capital or private equity (e.g. through university coursework or internships) is a plus. You have a solid understanding of investment concepts – how capital is deployed, what risk and return mean in practice, how portfolios are constructed, and how venture capital works as an asset class. You do not need to have worked in VC, but you should be able to hit the ground running on the investment context this role operates in.

You are comfortable working with data and modern tooling, but first and foremost you have strong financial acumen and high attention to detail. Technical skills – whether Python, AI workflows, or investment data platforms – can be developed on the job. A genuine curiosity for investments, financial data, and how capital is put to work is what we value most in this hire.

What Matters Most Is That You Are


Highly organized and detail-oriented
Curious and proactive
Comfortable operating with ambiguity
Low ego and willing to roll up your sleeves
Reliable, structured, and execution-focused
Motivated by helping a team operate efficiently
A self-starter with a strong ownership mentality


You likely have 0–2 years of experience, including internships or student work.

Experience with tools such as Excel, PowerPoint, think-cell and modern AI tools (e.g. ChatGPT, Claude) is a plus. Familiarity with PitchBook or similar investment data platforms, or tools such as Python, is a bonus - but you should be comfortable learning quickly and using modern tools pragmatically.

What We Offer

You will gain hands-on experience with a purpose-driven urban investment platform and get exposure to real asset investment strategy, tools, and decision-making processes. The role offers opportunities to learn and grow in a collaborative, high-performing team and is embedded in an inclusive culture that values curiosity, responsibility, and personal development. 


Make a difference by helping create urban spaces and places that enrich people’s lives in the most sustainable way. 
Lead the industry with an innovator that is pioneering new approaches while raising standards and inspiring the industry. 
Learn and grow as part of a powerhouse of brilliant people where teamwork, feedback and different ways of thinking are at the core. 
Be your whole self in a purpose-driven culture with a strong sense of belonging, based on trust, celebration and caring for each other. 


About 2150

We are based in Copenhagen and work primarily in-office, with flexibility where needed. We value thoughtful execution, strong collaboration, and people who take pride in doing the small things exceptionally well.

This role is well suited to someone who enjoys operational excellence, wants broad exposure to how a global investment firm operates, and is excited to build systems that scale.

Join Us

If this sounds like you, please send us your application as soon as possible, and no later than 10 June 2026. Please include your CV, grade transcripts, and a short note on why the role is interesting to you using the apply link below. If you have any questions, you are welcome to contact Alexander Kielland, COO.

We look forward to hearing from you!

Familiarity with PitchBook or similar investment data platforms, or tools such as Python, is a bonus - but you should be comfortable learning quickly and using modern tools pragmatically.

What We Offer

You will gain hands-on experience with a purpose-driven urban investment platform and get exposure to real asset investment strategy, tools, and decision-making processes. The role offers opportunities to learn and grow in a collaborative, high-performing team and is embedded in an inclusive culture that values curiosity, responsibility, and personal development. 

  • Make a difference by helping create urban spaces and places that enrich people’s lives in the most sustainable way. 
  • Lead the industry with an innovator that is pioneering new approaches while raising standards and inspiring the industry. 
  • Learn and grow as part of a powerhouse of brilliant people where teamwork, feedback and different ways of thinking are at the core. 
  • Be your whole self in a purpose-driven culture with a strong sense of belonging, based on trust, celebration and caring for each other. 

About 2150

We are based in Copenhagen and work primarily in-office, with flexibility where needed. We value thoughtful execution, strong collaboration, and people who take pride in doing the small things exceptionally well.

This role is well suited to someone who enjoys operational excellence, wants broad exposure to how a global investment firm operates, and is excited to build systems that scale.

Join Us

If this sounds like you, please send us your application as soon as possible, and no later than 10 June 2026. Please include your CV, grade transcripts, and a short note on why the role is interesting to you using the apply link below. If you have any questions, you are welcome to contact Alexander Kielland, COO.

We look forward to hearing from you!

Title: Student Assistant, Strategy and Intelligence
Place: Copenhagen, Capital Region of Denmark, Denmark
Description:
Are you eager to work with Master Data Governance and help build the structure, quality, and control needed for trusted data? Do you enjoy supporting data standards and data quality improvements that enable better business decisions? Join our newly created Strategy and Intelligence team as a Student Assistant and gain hands-on experience supporting global product and commercial master data governance. Our dynamic and passionate team will support you every step of the way.

As a Student Assistant in Strategy and Intelligence within Global Merchandising, you will support the team in strengthening Master Data Governance across product, assortment, and commercial data used across markets and collections. You will assist with data quality controls, governance documentation, data standards, and validation processes to ensure that master data is accurate, consistent, and fit for business use.

Through analytical tasks and cross-team collaboration, you will contribute to stronger data stewardship, transparent governance structures, and improved master data quality, helping enable better decision-making across planning and trade execution.

Your Main Responsibilities Will Include


Support Master Data Governance activities by maintaining, validating, and updating master data in line with defined standards, controls, and business rules
Assist in monitoring data quality and governance performance through regular reporting, issue tracking, and analysis of master data across key tools and processes
Monitor data quality, completeness, consistency, and compliance with established governance standards to identify issues and improvement opportunities
Support the development and maintenance of governance documentation, data definitions, and master data frameworks used across merchandising and trade processes
Provide analytical and operational support for governance processes, including data issue resolution, process improvements, and cross-functional coordination
Support data governance activities by coordinating master data requests, maintaining documentation, and helping ensure alignment between data owners, users, and business requirements


A collaborative team-player with an analytical mindset

We are looking for a structured and detail-oriented student with strong analytical capabilities and an interest in Master Data Governance. You enjoy working with data quality, standards, and process discipline, and you are motivated by helping create reliable, well-governed data that supports better business decisions. You thrive in a fast-paced and collaborative environment and are comfortable balancing multiple tasks while maintaining high quality in your work.

Additionally, We Hope To See That You Can Demonstrate


Pursuit of a Bachelor’s or Master’s degree within Data Science and Management or similar
Strong analytical skills with the ability to work with master data, identify governance and data quality issues, and translate findings into clear insights and actions
Structured and detail-oriented approach to data governance, documentation, validation, and process adherence
Proactive mindset with the ability to support tasks and identify opportunities for improvement
Teamwork and can-do attitude, taking pride in supporting colleagues and shared goals
Flexibility and adaptability in a dynamic work environment
Excellent written and verbal communication skills in English
Strong Microsoft Excel skills; experience with Power BI or working with governance reporting and large data sets is considered a plus
Experience from internships or student positions in international companies, preferably within retail, fashion, lifestyle, or FMCG, is a plus


You will, as a Student Assistant in the Strategy and Intelligence team, gain valuable experience and opportunities for both personal and professional development in a global organisation. This is a chance to join a globally recognised brand and work alongside dedicated colleagues in a role that will challenge, inspire, and develop your skills.

You will get


Work experience from a globally recognised brand
A fast-moving and dynamic environment where people and collaboration are at the centre
A chance to apply and develop your analytical skills in a global business context
Support and coaching from your manager as well as collaboration with a diverse international team
Flexible working hours, especially around exam periods


Working Requirements


Up to 15–20 hours per week, with flexibility around exams
Please note: To be considered for this role, you need to be enrolled in a Danish University.


Icing on the cake

You will be working out of our beautiful Head Office in Copenhagen V, established in 2016 and designed to unite and inspire our teams. Besides our beautiful interior design we are also proud of our LEED Gold Certification for Energy and Environmental Design. This means that we use as few natural resources as possible and that we use them with respect. The result is a professional and dynamic space that is unique, comfortable and inspirational – and delivers a true Pandora experience to anyone who works here or comes to visit.

About Pandora

Pandora is the world's largest jewellery brand, specialising in the design, crafting and marketing of accessible luxury jewellery made from high-quality materials. Each piece is created to inspire self-expression, allowing people to share their stories and passions through meaningful jewellery. Pandora jewellery is sold in more than 100 countries through 7,000 points of sale, including more than 2,800 concept stores.

Headquartered in Copenhagen, Denmark, Pandora employs around 39,000 people worldwide and crafts its jewellery with 100% recycled silver and gold. Pandora is committed to leadership in sustainability and has set out to halve greenhouse gas emissions across its value chain by 2030. Pandora is listed on the Nasdaq Copenhagen stock exchange and generated revenue of DKK 32.5 billion (EUR 4.4 billion) in 2025.

Title: Data Engineer
Place: Copenhagen, Capital Region of Denmark, Denmark
Description:
Spirii develops world-class charging products and digital solutions for electric vehicles. Our Digital Development department fosters a listen, learn, and lead culture — allowing us to continuously improve how we work. We're looking for a hands-on Data Engineer to build and evolve Spirii's data platform and pipeline infrastructure. Working closely with our Data Lead and domain teams, you'll design and maintain reliable ELT pipelines, ensure data arrives in Amazon Redshift correctly and on time, and support the analytics layer that powers insights across EV charging events, subscriptions, and financial transactions. This is a unique opportunity to shape Spirii's data infrastructure from an early stage — establishing standards for ingestion, orchestration, and data reliability as we scale.

Your Responsibilities

Pipeline & Ingestion


Design, build, and maintain scalable ELT pipelines that bring data from product backends, third-party services, and operational systems into Amazon Redshift.
Own ingestion reliability: monitoring, alerting, and incident response for pipeline failures.
Evaluate and improve orchestration tooling and scheduling.


Data Platform & Infrastructure


Evolve Spirii's data platform architecture: storage, compute, access control, and cost optimisation in Redshift.
Implement and maintain infrastructure-as-code practices for data workflows (Terraform/CDK or similar).
Build and maintain CI/CD pipelines for data infrastructure and transformations.


Data Quality & Governance


Implement data quality checks, freshness monitoring, and anomaly detection across ingestion and transformation layers.
Work with Analytics Engineers and domain teams to define and enforce data contracts and schema standards.
Ensure GDPR-compliant data handling: PII identification, access control, and data retention policies.


Collaboration & Stakeholders


Partner with Engineering and Product teams to instrument events correctly at the source — schemas, identifiers, and change management.
Collaborate with Data team to ensure the analytics layer is built on reliable, well-modelled foundations.
Contribute to a strong data culture by creating and enhancing documentation, runbooks, data lineage, discoverability and incident response capabilities.


About You


You care deeply about data reliability and building pipelines that teams can depend on.
You're hands-on: you build, test, and ship pipelines and infrastructure improvements yourself.
You think of data pipelines as products — you care about uptime, observability, and the downstream consumers who depend on them.
You're pragmatic: you know when to engineer robustly and when to keep things lean.
You collaborate well across teams and communicate clearly about trade-offs and status.


Qualifications


Bachelor's or Master's degree in Computer Science, Data Engineering, or a related field (or equivalent experience).
3+ years of experience in data engineering or a related role with a strong data focus.
Strong SQL skills and solid experience building production data pipelines.
Hands-on experience with a modern data warehouse (Redshift preferred; BigQuery/Snowflake/Databricks also relevant).
Experience with orchestration tools (Apache Airflow or similar).
Proficiency in Python for data work (pipeline scripts, transformations, tooling).
Solid understanding of ELT/ETL concepts, including incremental loading, CDC, and idempotency.
Familiar with infrastructure-as-code (Terraform, CDK, or similar).
Familiar with data governance concepts: access control, PII handling, GDPR-minded practices.


Nice to Have


Experience with data observability tooling.
Experience with event/data contract patterns in product analytics (schemas, versioning, instrumentation).
Experience with finance data and/or subscription/transaction domains.
Experience with dbt (understanding of transformation layer and how it interfaces with pipelines).
Machine learning infrastructure experience is a plus, but not required for this role.


Benefits

It is important for us to create a healthy, diverse, fun, and creative working environment, so besides all the basic benefits, we also focus a lot on social activities, flexible working hours and a good work-life balance.


Flexible Work Environment: Enjoy a collaborative, inclusive, and open workplace designed to fit your needs and support work-life balance.
Competitive Package: A competitive salary, an 11% company pension plan, and a comprehensive benefits package.
Time to Recharge: Benefit from 32 paid vacation days (5 weeks + 5 extra) and paid parental leave to focus on what matters most.
Career Growth: Access professional development opportunities and grow your career in a high-impact, high-growth industry.
Stay Energized: Enjoy excellent coffee, tea, free snacks, and subsidized lunches at the office.
Stay Active: Use our Spirii gym and shared company car to keep moving.
Make an Impact: Be part of a team driving the future of sustainable energy while working on meaningful projects.
Two innovation days per month for YOU to choose what you work on.


The position is a permanent position in Copenhagen, Denmark. Danish language is not a requirement, but fluent English language skills are required.

About Spirii – Who We Are

Spirii is a full-service e-Mobility provider, which offers hardware and software charging solutions to companies in the private and the public sector. Spirii has recently celebrated its six-year anniversary as a founded company; however the people in Spirii have extensive experience working with electric cars and charging, as well as the development of future transport and energy systems.

Spirii is an equal opportunity employer. We encourage applications from candidates of all backgrounds and experiences. Spirii strives to create an inclusive and diverse workplace, and we encourage all qualified applicants to apply regardless of age, gender, race, sexual/religious orientation, or disability.

Title: Senior Business Analyst – Data, Insights & AI
Description:
Job Description

As a Senior Business Analyst in Falck’s Data, Insights & AI department, you are the key link between the business and our technology. You turn complex business challenges into data- and AI-enabled solutions, drive discovery and value framing, and ensure that what we build delivers measurable impact for Falck’s customers, patients, and operations.

You will work across Business Units and teams, shaping how we use data, analytics, automation, and AI to advance sustainable healthcare and emergency response.

We are looking for a T-shaped profile – someone with deep expertise in business analysis who is also continuously expanding their skills across data, analytics, and AI as technologies and ways of working evolve. You will join a growing community of 60+ data and AI professionals in Copenhagen and Warsaw. Your focus is to find the right problems, define the right solutions, and ensure they are adopted and deliver value.

Your Main Responsibilities Will Involve


Identifying and framing high-impact business problems where data, analytics, automation, and AI can create strategic value as well as looking beyond the initial request to understand the underlying business need, challenge assumptions, and reframe problems to maximize value.
Building ROI-based business cases: articulate expected costs, benefits, and risks; quantify value (e.g., revenue uplift, cost savings, efficiency, quality, experience).
Acting as a trusted business partner to senior stakeholders across multiple business units.
Facilitating workshops, interviews, and working sessions to align on problems, priorities, and solution options.
Translating business problems into clear, testable requirements (epics, user stories, acceptance criteria) plus translate business concepts into logical and conceptual data models, defining how entities, relationships, measures, and KPIs should be represented in the data platform.
Collaborating daily with Engineers to validate feasibility and data availability, co-design solution approaches, and refine requirements as learning emerges.
Owning the business side of the solution lifecycle – from discovery to adoption: requirements and design, testing and validation, business readiness and go-live, and post-launch optimization.
Leading or coordinating User Acceptance Testing (UAT), including designing test cases that match real-world scenarios and edge cases.
Mentoring and support more junior team members, especially colleagues working with business analysis and insights, sharing best practices and providing feedback on complex challenges.
Being recognized as a go-to domain expert in your business area and share lessons learned across teams and projects.


About youExperience & track record


Several years of experience as a Business Analyst preferably in data, analytics, digital, or AI-driven environments.
Proven track record of delivering measurable business outcomes through applied analytics, automation, or AI initiatives.
Demonstrated experience in end-to-end delivery – from discovery and business case, through requirements and solution design, to testing, deployment, adoption, and benefit tracking.
Solid experience with stakeholder management at senior levels, including navigating conflicting priorities and driving alignment.
Strong problem-structuring and problem-solving skills.
Ability to break down complex challenges into clear, prioritized increments.
Experience writing high-quality user stories, use cases, and acceptance criteria.
Ability to convert data into insight narratives that drive decisions.
Familiarity with AI and ML concepts
Work with technical teams on data modelling approaches, helping to shape how business concepts are implemented in our data lakehouse architecture and in dimensional models so analytics and insights are trustworthy and easy to consume.
Excellent communication skills in English, both written and spoken; ability to make complex and “black box” topics understandable and actionable.
Strong facilitation skills for workshops, interviews, and cross-functional sessions.
Curious and continuously learning – especially about AI, data, and customer behaviour.
Collaborative – you work in cross-functional groups and share knowledge openly.
Structured and able to manage multiple parallel initiatives without losing sight of detail or big picture.


About us

Falck’s Data, Insights & AI department brings together experts in data engineering, analytics, AI, automation, and business partnering to support a global healthcare and emergency services organisation. With 60+ professionals in Copenhagen and Warsaw, we combine deep technical expertise with strong business understanding to build data and AI solutions that are reliable, scalable, and actually used.

We work closely across the business and with central technology teams, including a mission-critical Data Platform team that provides shared standards and frameworks to accelerate delivery. We invest in our people, encourage T-shaped development, and foster a culture of care, trust, and collaboration – turning care into action through data and AI.

About Us

At Falck we build on a legacy of more than one hundred years in healthcare and emergency services. With a presence in 25 countries, our 25,000 skilled professionals delivered more than 9.5 million services last year. We are committed to care, and to care for others, we need to take care of ourselves. That is why we continuously promote engagement, diversity, health, and safety among our employees. Every day, we work to advance sustainable healthcare. And every day, we turn care into action.

About The Team

Our global functions are integral to enabling the organisation to live our purpose. Through dedication and expertise , these teams support Falck in delivering care and making a meaningful difference in people's lives. If you are motivated by contributing to something greater, Falck is the place to bring care into your career .

Title: Student Assistant - Junior Digital Insight Analyst
Place: Copenhagen Municipality, Capital Region of Denmark, Denmark
Description:
Are you interested in digital marketing and data, and eager to gain hands-on experience in a global analytics setup? We are looking for a student assistant to support our digital analytics function by ensuring data quality, maintaining tools, and assisting with reporting processes.

In this role, you will work closely with our digital marketing team to support global initiatives and help maintain a strong data foundation across markets.

Key Responsibilities:



Support the maintenance and quality assurance of digital analytics tools
Assist in validating tracking and data accuracy, including identifying inconsistencies and flagging issues
Help prepare and update standardized dashboards and reports
Support data extraction and preparation from digital marketing channels
Assist in handling basic data-related requests from sales companies, with guidance from the analytics team
Contribute to documentation (e.g., Dashboard guidelines, naming conventions, process documentation)



About You:



Currently pursuing a bachelor’s or master’s degree (e.g., business, marketing, data, or similar), with at least 2 years remaining
Interested in digital analytics, marketing data, and performance measurement
Detail-oriented and comfortable working with structured tasks and checklists
Proactive in asking questions and clarifying when needed
Organized and able to follow processes and checklists



Professional Attributes



Proficiency in English (both written and spoken) is essential.
Expertise in Excel, Google Analytics, and visualization tools such as Data Studio or Power BI is beneficial.



Your interests include digital marketing, Google products, social media, CRM, and customer behavior. Prior experience in these domains is advantageous.

Work Hours & Location



Approximately 15 hours per week, with flexibility around exams
Based in Smørum, Denmark (hybrid work possible after onboarding)



Why Join Us?

You will gain hands-on experience in a global analytics setup and learn how digital data is used to support marketing decisions across markets. You’ll work in an international environment with supportive colleagues and have the opportunity to build a strong foundation for a future career in analytics or digital marketing.

Ready to Apply?

If this opportunity aligns with your aspirations, please submit your application and CV before June 10th, through our online recruitment system. Applications will be evaluated on a rolling basis, so we encourage early submissions.

For further inquiries about the role, feel free to reach out to Yingrui (Donna) Zhang via Email yizg@demant.com

We eagerly await your application and the possibility of welcoming you to our team.

At Demant we value diversity, equity, and inclusion (DE&I) and we are committed to creating an inclusive working environment. We welcome applications from alle qualified candidates regardless of ethnic background, personality, age, gender, and education. Diversity is part of our cultural DNA. Women in STEM areas are particularly encouraged to apply, as Demant supports United Nations Sustainable Development Goal 5 on gender equality.

Title: Data Engineer
Place: Copenhagen, Capital Region of Denmark, Denmark
Description:
About Weld




The next generation of data infrastructure will be built for AI agents. Weld is built to be operated programmatically through APIs and MCP, so developers and AI agents can build and run the data foundations that modern companies depend on. We move data at scale across hundreds of integrations and increasingly serve as the backbone for agentic data workflows.




You can try Weld at weld.app.




The role




Most of your time is with customers. You onboard new customers, design their data setups on Weld with them, model the data that lands in their warehouse so it is ready for analytics and for agents to operate on, and unblock them when things break. You join pre-sales calls when prospects want to talk to someone technical, and those conversations focus on making their data usable by AI agents and on plugging Weld into their agentic workflows. When customers go deeper, designing transformation layers, structuring data for agent consumption, or building agent-driven systems on top of their warehouse, you consult with them and build alongside them. You do this work using the tools that make modern engineering fast: Claude Code for writing and refactoring, the Anthropic API for the agents and internal tooling that scale your work, and Weld itself, which you will operate through APIs and agents, the way our customers do. What you learn from customers shapes the product. You have a direct line to the founders and the engineering team, and your field insights influence what we build next, especially in how Weld exposes itself to agents through APIs, MCP, and tool interfaces.




What you will work on




Onboard new customers and design their data setups on Weld, from pipelines to warehouse models
Join pre-sales calls and technical evaluations, help prospects understand how Weld fits their stack, and shape early implementations
Diagnose pipeline issues end to end across our integrations and customer warehouses, and unblock customers fast
Help customers model their warehouse data, so it is clean, reliable, and ready for analytics or agent consumption
Consult with customers building agentic systems on top of Weld, helping them design transformation layers and structure data for agent consumption
Ship internal agents and tooling with Claude Code and the Anthropic API to scale how we onboard, support, and serve customers
Turn recurring customer problems into product improvements, and bring field insights back to the team to shape how Weld serves agentic workflows




We are looking for someone who




Has a solid understanding of modern data infrastructure, including ETL, reverse ETL, and data warehousing
Has experience with SQL and data modeling
Has built systems that interact with APIs programmatically
Has shipped something to production with LLMs or agents
Uses Claude Code, Cursor, or similar AI dev tools every day, and has opinions about them
Understands how modern agents are built: tool use, sub-agents, skills, MCP, and structured outputs
Has thought seriously about context engineering and how to evaluate agent behavior in production
Writes Python comfortably when needed
Communicates clearly and enjoys working directly with customers
Experience with TypeScript is a plus
A background in a customer-facing technical role such as professional services, solutions engineering, forward-deployed engineering, or analytics consulting is a plus




How to apply




Apply via LinkedIn with a short note. Include:

• A link or short write-up of something you have built with LLMs or agents. What it does, why you built it the way you did, and what you would change if you started over.

• A sentence or two on how you use AI tools in your own workflow today.




Working at Weld




Working at Weld means real ownership of what you work on, the freedom to make decisions, and a short distance between your work and what customers see. The role is based in our office in central Copenhagen, next to the Opera. We offer a competitive salary and equity, company off-sites, 6 weeks paid holiday, company-paid pension and health insurance, a MacBook Pro, and a company card from day one to buy what you need.

Title: Business Intelligence Intern
Description:
Business Intelligence Intern

Gain invaluable practical experience with data analysis and Business Intelligence – Vitec Visiolink is looking for a Business Intelligence intern for the period August-December 2026.

As an intern in the Business Consulting department at Visiolink, you will be an active player in the exploration of data and trends based on large amounts of data in Google Analytics.

BI prepares reports and other products for the benefit of our customers, ensures data quality and uses data to inspire customers for strategic initiatives that increase reader engagement in their e-newspapers. As an intern, you will become part of the team on an equal footing with other employees.




About the internship

During the internship, you will gain insight into, and practical experience with all the tasks that cross our desks every day. The course will also offer events outside the house such as customer meetings, presentations and external events. You will largely help shape your internship, where the tasks can consist of:

• Analysis of large amounts of data (in Google Analytics 4, Python/R, Google BigQuery)

• Data collection, structuring and visualization

• User behaviour analysis

• Creating reports and presentations

• Automation through Python/R and Google BigQuery

• Data visualizations through Looker studio




About you

You are probably studying Business Intelligence, Business Economics, Information Technology or something in that vein.

We ensure that you have good conditions for your learning, and throughout your stay we focus on strengthening your skills. Together we will find the projects you are the most interested in and develop your internship from there.




• You have experience in data science and analysis

• You have experience with Python, R , or SQL

• You love exploring data, and you have your focus and interests within analysis and dissemination of data

• You are curious, result-oriented and passionate about translating theoretical understanding into a practical and application-oriented reality.




About Vitec Visiolink

Vitec Visiolink is a market-leading supplier of digital publishing solutions for the European media industry. We produce solutions for digital distribution in the form of e-newspapers and magazines, podcasts, newspaper archives, live news, etc. with 250 media houses in Europe, and more than 2,000 titles are distributed every day.




In Vitec Visiolink you will find 50 competent and committed colleagues who are all passionate about making a difference.




We have a flat organizational structure, where there is high ceiling and the opportunity to make your own mark - regardless of whether you are an intern, specialist or manager. Our active staff association ensures that we also have some great experiences together outside of working hours.




Practical information

The internship is 37 hours per week and is unpaid. However, lunch is included, as well as other employee benefits and events. We are processing applications on an ongoing basis, so do not hesitate to send in your application.




Are you interested?

If this sounds interesting, please send a motivated application and CV to Cecilie Vestergaard at cecilie.vestergaard@vitecsoftware.com.




Any questions regarding the position can be directed to business intelligence specialist Cecilie Vestergaard at Cecilie.Vestergaard@vitecsoftware.com or Head of Customer Operations Agnethe Løkke Madsen at AgnetheLokke.Madsen@vitecsoftware.com.

Title: Data Engineer
Description:
Do you want to use data engineering to strengthen global workforce development and accelerate the renewable energy transition?




The Global Wind Organisation (GWO) is looking for a Data Engineer to take a leading role in defining the data, integration, and development standards used in a shared industry platform for workforce development. You will ensure that data structures, integration patterns, and technical guardrails are consistent and scalable across a global ecosystem while still being able to build and troubleshoot solutions hands-on when needed.




This is a unique opportunity to shape the data foundation behind international safety training and certification for wind technicians. Your work will help ensure the right people, skills, and standards are in place globally, enabling safer worksites and a faster, better-supported green energy transition.




Why this role is different

You have real influence: what you design help scale a global, safety-critical workforce by improving how training, certification, and competence data works end-to-end
You are at the center of things, defining practical standards and guardrails used across a shared industry platform delivered by multiple external providers
You stay technical: you can look under the hood, prototype, troubleshoot, and build targeted components when it creates the most value




Global Wind Organisation (GWO) is a non-profit, for-purpose body committed to advancing safety standards and workforce competence in renewable industry. As the green energy transition accelerates and the need for a safety-focused workforce grows, our mission is to ensure technicians are skilled, confident and striving for injury free workplaces.




Through our portfolio of standards delivered across a global network of more than 640 independently certified training providers, we have empowered over 210.000 technicians to safely and effectively play a part in the renewable sector. We are committed to developing this pool of GWO-trained technicians and ensuring a skilled, safe, and job-ready workforce capable of supporting the industry’s long-term growth.




How will you contribute to the development of safety training and workforce development?

You will play a key role in the GWO Secretariat, helping shape and strengthen our data foundation. As Data Engineer, you will design and maintain data models, integrations, and automations that enable reliable reporting and better decision-making across the organisation. Working closely with stakeholders in business insights, Technology, and external partners, you will translate needs into robust technical solutions while also helping define the standards and structures that ensure consistency and scalability. This role is ideal for someone who enjoys being close to the data and the build, and who is motivated by creating clarity, structure, and real impact in a lean and collaborative organisation.




Key Responsibilities:

Design and maintain GWO's overall data model (data standards, entity definitions, and ownership)
Design and govern the integration and automation architecture by establishing patterns, guardrails, and tooling standards
Build and maintain integrations and automations hands-on where direct technical contribution is most effective
Translate reporting requirements and business insights from the Product Owner into data model and source system changes
Own custom development capability (coding standards, policies, tooling, and supplier code review)
Define deployment patterns and environment standards; operate CI/CD pipelines for custom code




As our new colleague you are:

A well-grounded individual with healthy values and excellent interpersonal skills. You know the importance of communicating constructively, and even when you do not know the answer to a question, you take the time to assist the person seeking an answer.




What we expect:

Proven experience as a Data Engineer / Analytics Engineer (or similar) in integration-heavy environments
Strong data modelling skills — defining entities, standards, ownership, and documentation that scale
Ability to set integration and automation patterns/guardrails across systems (architecture mindset) and make pragmatic trade-offs
Hands-on capability to build, debug, and operate production pipelines/integrations (you can look under the hood, not only design)
Experience translating stakeholder reporting/analytics needs into robust technical solutions and data changes
Strong collaboration and communication skills in English, written and verbal
Familiarity with CI/CD pipelines, deployment strategies, and environment management for data products/custom code (considered a plus)
Experience with data governance practices such as data quality controls, coding standards, and supplier/third-party code review (considered a plus)
Experience with modern data stack tooling (cloud data platforms, orchestration tools, transformation frameworks) (considered a plus)




If the above sounds interesting, you should join us!

Seize the exciting opportunity to be part of an expanding industry where one can grow, enjoy, and contribute.




GWO offers the following:

Purposeful work for a non-profit organisation
Flat organisational structure
Emphasis on a balanced work life
Continuous personal development
Strong foundation for professional networking and sparring




As we process applications as they reach us, we encourage you to apply today rather than tomorrow. Please send your application and CV using the “apply” button. In case you have questions regarding the position, please reach out to Anders Munck at +45 20690640. All queries are of course handled with discretion.




GWO encourages applicants of all genders and backgrounds. Applicants must possess the legal right to work in the Denmark at the time of application.




GWO main office is in Copenhagen. Occasional business travel is an expected part of this role.

Title: Data & Performance Specialist, Global Planning, Arla Foods Ingredients - Aarhus
Description:
Are you passionate about turning data into actionable insights and enabling smarter planning decisions through digital solutions? Join us to shape and strengthen our planning capabilities by driving analytics, automation, and digital enablement across our end-to-end planning processes.

“You will play a key role in connecting planning processes, data, and technology — ensuring that our planners have the insights, tools, and system support needed to make better decisions across the business. At the same time, you will help drive the continued development of Global Planning by strengthening automation, enabling data-driven ways of working, and improving how planning processes are supported across systems, data, and functions,” says Head of Global Planning, Jacob Møller Herskind.

How you will make an impact?

As Data & Performance Specialist in Global Planning, you will be responsible for enabling data-driven decision-making across demand planning, supply planning, inventory management, and S&OP/S&OE processes. Working closely with business stakeholders, IT, and cross-functional teams, you will help ensure strong system support, trusted data foundations, and scalable digital solutions across our planning landscape. You will act as a key bridge between business and technology, translating planning needs into practical analytics and automation solutions while continuously improving our planning setup and digital capabilities.

Specifically, you will


Build and maintain reports, dashboards, and analytics solutions in Power BI to support operational and tactical decision-making
Support the activation and continuous development of planning tool capabilities, including SAP IBP functionalities supporting S&OP and S&OE
Ensure core planning data readiness and enable a trusted “single source of truth” through structured data management, governance, and system support
Act as Solution Owner for selected digital solutions, translating business requirements into scalable solution designs while ensuring stability, maintenance, and continuous improvement
Collaborate closely with Data & Analytics and IT teams regarding architecture, data access, governance, and deployment of scalable solutions
Support implementation of tools and systems aligned with planning process requirements and future developments


The role sits within AFI’s Global Planning organisation and reports to the Head of Global Planning, and is based in Aarhus.

What will make you successful?

We imagine that you combine strong analytical capabilities with a pragmatic and business-oriented mindset. You enjoy working closely with operations and stakeholders while translating complexity into practical and scalable solutions. You are proactive, structured, and self-driven, and motivated by strengthening the foundational data, reporting, and system capabilities needed to enhance planning processes and support scalable business growth.

Furthermore, you likely bring


4+ years of experience working with Business Intelligence, analytics, or digital transformation initiatives
Hands-on experience with Power BI, including data modelling, report development, and DAX basics
SQL knowledge for extracting and shaping data
Experience with Power Platform tools such as Power Automate and/or Power Apps
Understanding of data governance, data quality, and access management principles
Strong stakeholder management and communication skills across business, finance, and IT functions
A structured and detail-oriented mindset with a practical approach to problem-solving and the ability to deliver maintainable solutions


It is considered an advantage if you also have


Interest in S&OP, S&OE, and end-to-end planning processes and experience from FMCG, supply chain, or planning environments
Familiarity with planning systems and integrated business planning tools (e.g. SAP IBP)


What do we offer?

You will become part of a highly important and evolving area within Arla Foods Ingredients – a global leader in powerful nutrition – playing a key role in enabling scalable planning processes and strengthening planning capabilities across the business. Expect significant responsibility, close collaboration across functions, and the opportunity to help build the data, reporting, and system foundation needed to support strong planning performance and decision-making. This role offers a unique opportunity to influence how planning data, KPIs, and digital capabilities evolve to support operational excellence and future growth.

We work closely across functions and promise a steep learning curve, continuous development, and a supportive culture that helps you deliver on ambitious goals—while contributing to meaningful outcomes for customers, farmer-owners, and the planet.

Would you like to join us?

If you want to help shape the future of global planning at Arla Foods Ingredients, please apply as soon as possible, as we review applications on a continuous basis and will close the recruitment once the right candidate is found. For additional information, please contact Head of Global Planning, Jacob Møller Herskind on email jachz@arlafoods.com.

Shape the Future of Dairy

Arla is a global leader in the dairy industry, committed to enabling good food choices that make life better, providing people with natural, sustainable nourishment, while taking care at every step to ensure Arla is a choice they can feel good about. If you are looking to shape the future with an ambitious global cooperative that truly cares about your growth, where everyone feels valued and empowered, and collaboration is the core of culture, Arla is a choice you can feel good about.

At the same time, you will help drive the continued development of Global Planning by strengthening automation, enabling data-driven ways of working, and improving how planning processes are supported across systems, data, and functions,” says Head of Global Planning, Jacob Møller Herskind.

How you will make an impact?

As Data & Performance Specialist in Global Planning, you will be responsible for enabling data-driven decision-making across demand planning, supply planning, inventory management, and S&OP/S&OE processes. Working closely with business stakeholders, IT, and cross-functional teams, you will help ensure strong system support, trusted data foundations, and scalable digital solutions across our planning landscape. You will act as a key bridge between business and technology, translating planning needs into practical analytics and automation solutions while continuously improving our planning setup and digital capabilities.

Specifically, you will

  • Build and maintain reports, dashboards, and analytics solutions in Power BI to support operational and tactical decision-making
  • Support the activation and continuous development of planning tool capabilities, including SAP IBP functionalities supporting S&OP and S&OE
  • Ensure core planning data readiness and enable a trusted “single source of truth” through structured data management, governance, and system support
  • Act as Solution Owner for selected digital solutions, translating business requirements into scalable solution designs while ensuring stability, maintenance, and continuous improvement
  • Collaborate closely with Data & Analytics and IT teams regarding architecture, data access, governance, and deployment of scalable solutions
  • Support implementation of tools and systems aligned with planning process requirements and future developments

The role sits within AFI’s Global Planning organisation and reports to the Head of Global Planning, and is based in Aarhus.

What will make you successful?

We imagine that you combine strong analytical capabilities with a pragmatic and business-oriented mindset. You enjoy working closely with operations and stakeholders while translating complexity into practical and scalable solutions. You are proactive, structured, and self-driven, and motivated by strengthening the foundational data, reporting, and system capabilities needed to enhance planning processes and support scalable business growth.

Furthermore, you likely bring

  • 4+ years of experience working with Business Intelligence, analytics, or digital transformation initiatives
  • Hands-on experience with Power BI, including data modelling, report development, and DAX basics
  • SQL knowledge for extracting and shaping data
  • Experience with Power Platform tools such as Power Automate and/or Power Apps
  • Understanding of data governance, data quality, and access management principles
  • Strong stakeholder management and communication skills across business, finance, and IT functions
  • A structured and detail-oriented mindset with a practical approach to problem-solving and the ability to deliver maintainable solutions

It is considered an advantage if you also have

  • Interest in S&OP, S&OE, and end-to-end planning processes and experience from FMCG, supply chain, or planning environments
  • Familiarity with planning systems and integrated business planning tools (e.g. SAP IBP)

What do we offer?

You will become part of a highly important and evolving area within Arla Foods Ingredients – a global leader in powerful nutrition – playing a key role in enabling scalable planning processes and strengthening planning capabilities across the business. Expect significant responsibility, close collaboration across functions, and the opportunity to help build the data, reporting, and system foundation needed to support strong planning performance and decision-making. This role offers a unique opportunity to influence how planning data, KPIs, and digital capabilities evolve to support operational excellence and future growth.

We work closely across functions and promise a steep learning curve, continuous development, and a supportive culture that helps you deliver on ambitious goals—while contributing to meaningful outcomes for customers, farmer-owners, and the planet.

Would you like to join us?

If you want to help shape the future of global planning at Arla Foods Ingredients, please apply as soon as possible, as we review applications on a continuous basis and will close the recruitment once the right candidate is found. For additional information, please contact Head of Global Planning, Jacob Møller Herskind on email jachz@arlafoods.com.

Shape the Future of Dairy

Arla is a global leader in the dairy industry, committed to enabling good food choices that make life better, providing people with natural, sustainable nourishment, while taking care at every step to ensure Arla is a choice they can feel good about. If you are looking to shape the future with an ambitious global cooperative that truly cares about your growth, where everyone feels valued and empowered, and collaboration is the core of culture, Arla is a choice you can feel good about.



Ref. 108658

Title: Data Engineer
Place: Copenhagen, Capital Region of Denmark, Denmark
Description:
Azure Data Engineer




Som Data Engineer hos cVation bygger du innovative dataplatforme og datawarehouses i Azure. Du arbejder med ETL-processer og automatisering af dataflows, og du bygger skalerbare, driftssikre løsninger for vores kunder. Vi bruger teknologier som Fabric, Databricks, Spark, Azure Data Factory, Terraform, Git og CI/CD. Fokus er på data engineering, pipelines og cloud-infrastruktur.




cVation er et dansk udviklings- og konsulenthus, som bygger avancerede cloudløsninger på Azure med afsæt i markedets hurtigste og mest agile metoder. cVation blev stiftet i 2014, har modtaget tre Børsen Gazeller og vundet Microsofts Partner Award for App Innovation tre år i træk. Vi arbejder dedikeret med agile arbejdsmetoder, DevOps og Test Driven Development. Vi sætter en ære i at levere software af den højeste kvalitet, og tager desuden principperne fra DevOps og software engineering med i vores dataprojekter, hvilket betyder, at tests, Infrastructure as Code og automatisering er i fokus.




Du kommer til at arbejde på et af vores dataprojekter med et projektteam bestående af arkitekter, softwareudviklere og DevOps‑specialister. Du vil have fuldt fokus på ét projekt og være tilknyttet ét team ad gangen.




Din rolle bliver at:

rådgive om og bygge datawarehouse- og dataplatformsløsninger i skyen
bidrage til design og implementering af den optimale Azure‑infrastruktur
sikre, at alt opsætning og provisionering sker som Infrastructure as Code, fx Terraform
arbejde med fuldautomatiseret overvågning, drift og skalering af løsninger




Hvad forventer vi af dig?

Du har en videregående uddannelse i software engineering, human centered AI el. lign. fra DTU, ITU eller KU samt erfaring i data engineering
Du har erfaring med at bygge datawarehouses og dataplatforme og har lyst til at arbejde med moderne data engineering
Du har erfaring med – og vil gerne blive klogere på – flere af følgende teknologier: Fabric, Databricks, Azure Data Factory, Spark, Data Warehouse, Data Platform, Python, Azure DevOps, PowerShell, Azure CLI, YAML, Bicep, Terraform, Git og CI/CD
Du vil gerne arbejde med de nyeste teknologier i Azure
Du vil gerne indgå i et agilt setup, hvor krav og retning hurtigt kan ændres, men hvor medbestemmelse og frihed er i højsædet
Det vil være en fordel, hvis du er vant til at arbejde som konsulent i et udviklingsteam i tæt samarbejde med arkitekter og software udviklere
Du har evnen til at omsætte kundebehov til handling
Du er flydende i skrift og tale på dansk og engelsk, og trives med at have den daglige kontakt med kunden




Som person er du selvstændig, ansvarsbevidst, initiativrig, eksekverende og serviceminded. Du er drevet af at lære nyt og vild med at holde dig ajour med de nyeste metoder og teknologier. Du er engageret og motiveret i alt hvad du foretager dig. Hvis du også er glad for at sparre med dygtige kollegaer og trives med at løfte i flok, vil du passe perfekt ind hos os.




Hvad tilbyder vi?

Du kommer til at indgå i vores voksende team, hvor høj faglighed, innovation og arbejdsglæde er i højsæde. Det høje faglige niveau blandt dine kollegaer giver en unik mulighed for at arbejde med de bedste, og vi tager din faglige udvikling alvorligt. Vi har en flad organisation med et minimum af administration, hvor ledelsens døre altid er åbne, og der er god plads til både bordfodbold, fredagsbar, frokost på kajen og et aktivt liv udenfor arbejdstid. Flere gange om året afholder vi hackathons, hvor vi i teams dykker ned i præcis de emner, som udviklerne finder interessante. En del af vores kultur er også, at vi gerne ser alle komme med input til hvordan vi kan optimere, udvikle og forbedre cVation.




Hvorfor vælge os som din nye arbejdsplads?

Vores fundament er løsninger bygget på Microsoft Azure Cloud. Alle vores medarbejdere mestrer cloud-native udvikling og DevOps. Vi arbejder i projekter og engagerer os hands-on i fælles teams med vores kunder for at sikre kvalitet og effektiv videndeling.




Vi er passionerede omkring at hjælpe vores kunder med at få det maksimale ud af cloudteknologien. For os er cloud ikke blot infrastruktur eller et nyt datacenter i skyen. Teknologien giver virksomheder mulighed for at skabe væsentligt mere værdi og arbejde med agilitet og innovation på en helt ny måde.




Vi har været med fra starten, da cloud blev udviklet og tilgængeligt. Vi tilbyder således vores årelange erfaring og dybe cloud ekspertise samt en værktøjskasse med alle vores best practices i form af accelerationsplatformen CADD. Den dækker over gennemprøvede værktøjer, rammeværk, effektive udviklingsmetoder og færdige automatiseringskomponenter. Alt dette for at sikre, at projekter kommer hurtigt og sikkert fra start.




Du vil blive en del af en virksomhed, som tror på, at alle har betydning, og at det skal være sjovt at gå på arbejde hver dag. Du vil få kollegaer, der er interesseret i at lære og har ønske om at blive de bedste til det, de gør. Vi tilbyder derfor gode muligheder for at udvikle dig.




Interesseret?

Klik på Easy Apply knappen, hvis du vil arbejde med de nyeste cloud-teknologier. Hvis du har under 5 års erfaring, bedes du også vedhæfte dine karakterblade i samme fil som dit CV. Vi behandler din ansøgning fortroligt og arrangerer løbende interviews.




Har du spørgsmål til jobbet, eller vil du høre mere om cVation, er du meget velkommen til at kontakte:

Maiken Pelby, Head of People & Culture, T/ 42612828, map@cvation.com




Vi ser frem til at høre fra dig!

Title: Student Assistant for Advanced Analytics
Place: Gentofte, Capital Region of Denmark, Denmark
Description:
Join us in this role where you’ll help develop data and statistically driven tools for our trading floor. You’ll influence decisions and handle tasks on your own, thereby contributing with your knowledge and expertise. You're expected to be able to work 15 hours weekly.

Welcome to Operations in Bioenergy

You’ll be part of Advanced Analytics where you, together with your colleagues, will develop forecasts, analyses, and dashboards which provide our trading floor with a strong basis for data and statistical driven decision making in the daily operation at our Danish power stations, wind turbines, solar PV, electric boilers, heat pumps, and P2X. As a team, we continuously strive to do better and develop our skillset. In our team we have an informal and very open culture and you’ll always be able to get help and learn from your colleagues.

You’ll play an important role in:


developing and implementing performance tracking
developing dashboards to visualise decision support and performance tracking
developing and implementing forecasting models using AI/ML/DL, incl. stochastic modelling and statistical data analysis
communicating and collaborating with internal and external stakeholders, i.e., team members, traders, etc.


To succeed in the role, you:


are currently studying data science, applied mathematics, or another relevant course within analytics and/or engineering
have at least two years left before graduating
are a strong Python programmer
have experience with writing and reviewing maintainable and testable code
are familiar with machine-learning concepts (supervised and unsupervised learning) and data analytics
possess knowledge of Git, Docker & Kubernetes, or electricity markets - this will seen as a benefit but however not a requirement.


Maybe you’ve read the above and can see you have some transferable skills, even though they don’t quite match all the points. If you think you can bring something to the team, we still encourage you to apply.

Shape the future with us

Send your application and CV to us as soon as possible. We’ll be conducting interviews on a continuous basis and reserve the right to take down the advert when we’ve found the right candidate.

As an applicant or employee, you may request reasonable work and position accommodation or adjustments via accommodation@orsted.com.

Please note that for your application to be taken into consideration, you must submit your application via our online career pages and answer the screening questions relevant for your country. We don't take applications or inquiries from external recruiters or agencies into account for this position.
