Title: Student Analyst
Description:
Are you looking to develop your analytical skills in a commercial environment? We are looking for two students to join our team as Student Analysts – whether you are motivated by understanding commercial decision‑making or curious about applying and developing data analysis skills in practice.




Drive meaningful change

The German Portfolio Management team is responsible for introducing the right products to the market, ensuring that products are purchased from the best trades, that the purchased stock is efficiently allocated and sold with the highest possible profit. As a Student Analyst, you will be working alongside fellow students and full-time employees alike, across the Portfolio Management team.

You will join a group of students with multiple areas of responsibility – meaning that you will have different tasks during your workday that are essential to improving and maintaining our position in the German market:

Analysing market demand, competitors, and product performance to support successful product launches
Turning data into actionable insights that support commercial decisions
Contributing to the management of German insurance contracts
Identifying opportunities for process optimisation and automation within your own area of responsibility, and contributing to improvement initiatives across the team
Working proactively in a dynamic, learning‑focused environment




Your Profile

We expect you to be self‑driven and to have the maturity to take ownership and handle challenges with a can‑do attitude. You thrive in a dynamic environment, bring a curious and analytical mindset, and take a structured approach to complex problems.

As we are looking for two students to join the team, who ideally are:

Studying a Data & Analytics- or Commercial degree, e.g. Economics, Business Administration, International Business, Finance, or similar
Currently enrolled in a Bachelor’s degree or have at least 2 years left of your studies
Fluent in English and German skills are an advantage

In addition, you:

Bring strong Microsoft Excel skills
Experience with Python, SQL, Power BI or other analytical tools is considered a plus, but not required
Are comfortable working with data and translating analyses into insights that support commercial decisions
Have a solid commercial understanding and are eager to learn
Have an eye for detail




Are you interested?

If this position sounds like a good match for you, we encourage you to submit your application.

We review applications on an ongoing basis and screening conversations may take place before the deadline. We therefore encourage you to apply as soon as possible.




Would you like to know more about us?

Follow us on Instagram and LinkedIn and get an insight into everyday life at Abacus Medicine. For job openings, check our Career page.




Moving Healthcare. Moving You.

In Abacus Medicine Group, you’ll experience the power of Moving Healthcare. With our change mindset, we're moving the industry and improving healthcare as we broaden access to medicine.

But it's not just about moving healthcare, it's about Moving You. You’ll be part of a caring community of more than 1,750 people across the World. With us, you will be valued, empowered, and have a direct impact on driving change. Let's access the future of medicine and make a difference together. Your move.

Title: AI Trainer - Freelance Data Annotator
Description:
Please submit your resume in English and indicate your level of English.

At Toloka, we connect smart, curious people from around the world with freelance online tasks that train and improve artificial intelligence.

What We Do

The Toloka Annotators connects individuals with Generative AI projects from leading tech innovators. Our mission is to unlock the full potential of AI by involving real people from around the world in the development process.

About The Role

Annotation is what helps AI make sense of the world. As an annotator, you may be invited to take part in online projects such as rating AI-generated content, evaluating factual accuracy, or comparing responses - when projects are available.

Responsibilities:


Carefully review provided data (text, images, or videos)
Label or classify content based on project guidelines
Identify and flag factually incorrect, sensitive, inappropriate, or unclear material


Important note: This is project-based work. Tasks are available only when projects are active. You may be invited to one or more projects depending on your profile and current opportunities.

Each project has its own compensation level based on scope and expertise required. On this project, AI trainers earn up to $22 per hour equivalent.

Requirements


Bachelor's degree in any discipline
Minimum 1 year of experience in any professional role
Advanced level of English (C1 or higher), both written and spoken
Logical thinking, fact-checking and reasoning abilities
Strong attention to detail and ability to understand and follow complex instructions
Strong communication skills, including the ability to ask clarifying questions when needed
Genuine interest in technology and artificial intelligence


Benefits

Why this freelance opportunity might be a great fit for you?


Take part in a part-time, remote, freelance project that fits around your primary professional or academic commitments
Work on advanced AI projects and gain valuable experience that enhances your portfolio
Influence how future AI models understand and communicate in your field of expertise

  • Advanced level of English (C1 or higher), both written and spoken
  • Logical thinking, fact-checking and reasoning abilities
  • Strong attention to detail and ability to understand and follow complex instructions
  • Strong communication skills, including the ability to ask clarifying questions when needed
  • Genuine interest in technology and artificial intelligence

Benefits

Why this freelance opportunity might be a great fit for you?

  • Take part in a part-time, remote, freelance project that fits around your primary professional or academic commitments
  • Work on advanced AI projects and gain valuable experience that enhances your portfolio
  • Influence how future AI models understand and communicate in your field of expertise

Title: Senior Data Scientist
Place: Copenhagen, Capital Region of Denmark, Denmark
Description:
Senior Data Scientist




Vil du arbejde med ML-, GenAI- og agentbaserede løsninger, der rent faktisk kommer i drift – og hvor tekniske valg har direkte konsekvenser for forretning og beslutninger? Er du interesseret i, hvordan AI kan bruges til at skabe konkret værdi i den offentlige sektor og i andre regulerede brancher som finans og farma?

Vi søger en senior data scientist, der både kan modellere på højt niveau og engagere sig substantielt i de tekniske og arkitektoniske valg, der afgør om en løsning kommer i produktion. Hvis du trives med at tage ansvar, arbejde tæt med kunder og være med til at præge et stærkt fagligt miljø i et hus i hastig vækst, er du måske den rette tilføjelse til vores team.




Din rolle

Du bliver en del af vores Data & AI-team og arbejder i projekter, hvor data, modeller og systemer hænger tæt sammen. Rollen indebærer både udvikling og implementering og en tæt dialog om, hvordan løsningerne overdrages og anvendes i praksis. Du vil i mange projekter fungere som teknisk drivkraft og være ansvarlig for centrale designvalg og kvaliteten af de løsninger, vi leverer.

Eksempler på projekter:

·      RAG-løsning til understøttelse af sagsbehandling i offentlig sektor

·      Forecasting-model til finansielle beslutningsprocesser

·      GenAI-assistent til automatisering af interne arbejdsprocesser

·      Agentbaseret arkitektur til migrering af legacy-system

I praksis vil din hverdag bestå af opgaver som at:

·      Udvikle og implementere ML- og GenAI-løsninger (klassifikation, regression, forecasting, NLP, RAG, agentflows)

·      Tage modeller fra prototype til stabil drift

·      Bidrage til tekniske og arkitektoniske beslutninger

·      Tæt dialog med både kunder og kollegaer om at omsætte behov til løsninger

·      Bidrage med dataforståelse, feature engineering og klargøring til produktion (test, versionering, integration)

·      Udvikle nye typer ydelser og præsentere oplæg og tilbud

Din hverdag vil typisk bestå af en kombination af hands-on udvikling, dialog med kunder og sparring med kollegaer om design og implementering. Du forventes også at kunne indgå i det markedsvendte arbejde med at udvikle nye kommercielle ydelser og lave/præsentere oplæg og tilbud for kunder.




Kvalifikationer

Minimum:

·      Solid erfaring med Python og relevante ML-biblioteker

·      Erfaring med at udvikle og implementere ML-modeller i praksis

·      Erfaring med at bringe modeller fra analyse til produktion

·      Erfaring med data pipelines, model performance og trade-offs

·      Flydende dansk (skrift og tale)

Højt vægtet:

·      Erfaring med produktionssætning af ML-/GenAI-løsninger i komplekse systemlandskaber

·      Erfaring med cloud (fx Azure, Databricks) og deployment patterns

·      Erfaring med GenAI (fx RAG, LLM-baserede løsninger eller agentflows)

·      Erfaring med at tage teknisk ansvar og træffe arkitekturbeslutninger

·      Evne til at arbejde tæt med forretning og omsætte behov til løsninger




Det er et plus, hvis du har:

·      Erfaring fra konsulentbranchen

·      Kendskab til den offentlige sektor eller andre regulerede brancher

·      Interesse for AI-strategi og use case-udvikling

·      God forståelse for it-arkitektur




Personlige kompetencer:

·      Analytisk og struktureret

·      Kan forklare komplekse problemstillinger klart

·      Tager ansvar og arbejder selvstændigt

·      Samarbejdsorienteret og nysgerrig

·      Dyb interesse for teknologi og AI




Det får du hos os

Hos Ajeto arbejder vi med nogle af de mest udfordrende data- og systemudviklingsprojekter. Du vil være en del af et hands-on-orienteret og stimulerende miljø, hvor du både kan videreudvikle din egen faglighed og være med til at præge virksomhedens faglige profil. Du bliver en del af et team med stærke kompetencer inden for både data, engineering og strategi.




Du vil:

·      Arbejde med reelle produktionsløsninger

·      Få tidligt ansvar og mulighed for at påvirke løsninger og retning

·      Være i tæt sparring med erfarne profiler, både tekniske og forretningsmæssige

·      Have struktureret faglig udvikling gennem bl.a. mentorordning

·      Deltage i konferencer og faglige miljøer

·      Opnå konkurrencedygtig løn og vilkår

·      Være en del af et stærkt socialt fællesskab med samvær, mange fælles aktiviteter, årlig strategitur mv.

Projekter foregår primært i København med begrænset rejseaktivitet. Arbejdsformen varierer mellem on-site og remote afhængigt af projekt og kunde. Vi vægter fleksibilitet højt.

 

Om Ajeto

Ajeto blev etableret i 2024 af erfarne profiler fra it- og konsulentbranchen med ambitionen om at bygge et stærkt alternativ på det danske konsulentmarked med særlig styrke i den offentlige sektor og i regulerede brancher såsom finans og farma, hvor strategi, data og systemudvikling skal tænkes og leveres samlet, og hvor vi tager ansvar for hele kæden fra retning og behovsafklaring til implementering.

Vi arbejder i organisationer med komplekse beslutningsprocesser og mange interessenter. Det kræver, at vi kombinerer analytisk styrke og teknisk gennemførelse med forståelse for de institutionelle rammer og beslutningsrum, organisationer opererer i.

Vi er et konsulenthus i vækst. Dette understøttes af vores hidtidige vækst, hvor vi på under to år gået til at være snart 40 konsulenter. For dig betyder det, at du bliver en del af en udvikling, hvor vi fejrer succeserne sammen, mens du og vi løbende tilpasser os til en voksende organisation. Vores ambition er endvidere at tage en tydelig position inden for modernisering, AI og datadrevne løsninger i Danmark. For dig betyder det mulighed for stort ansvar og indflydelse.




Løn og vilkår

Du ansættes som konsulent og i den lønramme på dette niveau. Hos Ajeto ønsker vi ikke at incentivisere gennem en uigennemsigtig bonus-model, som du selv kun har begrænset indflydelse på. Lønmodellen består derfor af en fast, konkurrencedygtig løn, som giver dig pengene i hånden fra start.

Vi ønsker at skabe de rammer, som gør det muligt for dig at yde dit bedste arbejde på en måde hvor du trives, både når du er på arbejde, og når du er hjemme. Ajeto tilbyder derfor også fem feriefridage i tillæg til ferielovens fem ugers ferie, samt mulighed for fleksibelt arbejde i det omfang, som det kan lade sig gøre på dit aktuelle kundeprojekt.




Hvordan søger du

Send os din ansøgning senest den 12. juni 2026 på: job@ajeto.dk

Introducer dig selv til os som var du allerede vores kollega, og giv os indsigt i dine faglige interesser, styrker og ambitioner. Fortæl os også gerne om dine tidligere erfaringer, roller eller projekter, som du er særlig stolt over. Fortæl os hvorfor du er interesseret i dette job, og hvad du håber på at opnå i din tid hos Ajeto, fx inden for de kommende 1-3 år.




Hvad sker der efter du har søgt jobbet?

Vi vil løbende indkalde relevante kandidater til interview. Du kan forvente et svar på din ansøgning senest den 18. juni 2026.




Hvis du bliver inviteret til samtale, kan der forekomme en case. I så fald gennemgår vi den sammen, hvorefter du får forberedelsestid og derefter blive bedt om at præsentere din tilgang. Casen vil danne rammen om den faglige dialog på ansættelsessamtalen og har alene til formål at give ansøgeren muligheden for at samle sine tanker om scenariet, og italesætte hvordan kandidaten går til opgaver.




Vi opfordrer ansøgere med enhver baggrund at søge dette job; et job hvor vi sammen vil arbejde for at du kan udføre din karrieres bedste arbejde. Vi sætter pris på, at du overvejer at søge hos os, og vi lover at give dig og din ansøgning vores fulde opmærksomhed. Vi ser frem til at høre fra dig!




Forventet opstart

Hvis du bliver tilbudt stillingen, er der forventet opstart den 1. september 2026. Skulle der opstå en mulighed for at starte tidligere eller senere, som både matcher dine ønsker og vores kunders behov, er vi åbne for en fleksibel opstartsdato.




Spørgsmål

For spørgsmål til stillingen bedes du kontakte partner Thor Hvidbak (thv@ajeto.dk, 40447405).

Title: People Data Engineer, People Analytics & Insights
Place: Billund, Region of Southern Denmark, Denmark
Description:
Job Description

Are you passionate about building robust, scalable data foundations? Join our team and help us develop a solid data platform on Databricks that powers the People, Places & Culture team with reliable, high-quality data about our people at the LEGO Group.

Please note This role is at Lead Professional level and we do not offer relocation


Core Responsibilities

You will be part of a team driving scalable, reliable data solutions for the People Places & Culture Organization to enable data-driven decision making across areas such as workforce planning, talent acquisition, workplace experience, rewards & recognition, and more. Your role will include





Build and maintain a robust data foundation on the Databricks platform, designing and implementing dimensional data warehouse models that ensure high data reliability, quality, and accessibility within the People, Places & Culture domain.
Develop automated, standardized, scalable data pipelines using SQL and Python, minimizing manual reporting efforts and ensuring consistent data delivery to support critical reporting processes (including external reporting).
Strengthen people analytics products by implementing accurate, automated data models that support informed decision-making, implementing data quality checks, validation frameworks, and monitoring processes to ensure trusted and reliable data pipelines.
Equip the People, Places & Culture organization with the right data and tools, enabling them to focus on strategic priorities and enhancing the value of people data across the LEGO Group.
Lay the groundwork for advanced analytics by building well-structured and well- documented data infrastructure that can support future generative AI and machine learning capabilities




Play Your Part in Our Team Succeeding

You will be part of the Digital & Data Products team within the People, Places & Culture

Organization, ensuring the accuracy, consistency, and accessibility of people data across the organization. This role is critical to strengthening our data foundations, consolidating data sources, and supporting digital transformation initiatives within People, Places & Culture.

Do You Have What It Takes?

You bring a background in data engineering, analytics, or a related field. You should be

comfortable working in ambiguity and contributing across the data lifecycle in a fast-paced team environment. You may have a couple of years of experience or be a recent graduate with strong foundational skills. In addition, we are looking for





Strong SQL skills and proficiency in Python — you're comfortable writing queries, troubleshooting code and building data pipelines
Understanding of dimensional data warehouse modelling concepts — you know the difference between fact and dimension tables, understand star schemas, and are eager to deepen your modelling expertise.
Experience with or strong interest in modern data platform technologies such as Databricks, Spark, or similar distributed data processing technologies.
A continuous improvement mindset – you identify inefficiencies and propose solutions to optimize workflows and processes
Interest in ML & AI – you are aware of emerging AI & ML capabilities and curious to experiment to identify how they can accelerate the value of data.




It will be an advantage if you bring experience/familiarity with





GitHub or similar tool
Power BI or similar visualization tools
Data governance principles
APIs and semi-structured data
Delta Lake / medallion architecture concepts
CI/CD (Continuous Integration/Continuous Deployment) concepts and deployment practices for managing data workflows and code changes reliably.




It will be an advantage, but not a requirement, if you have experience working with people data (HR data) and an understanding of HCM systems like Workday or SuccessFactors.

Applications are reviewed on an ongoing basis. However, please note we do amend or withdraw our jobs and reserve the right to do so at any time, including prior to any advertised closing date. So, if you're interested in this role we encourage you to apply as soon as possible.

What’s in it for you?

Here is what you can expect

Family Care Leave - We offer enhanced paid leave options for those important times.

Insurances – All colleagues are covered by our life and disability insurance which provides protection and peace of mind.

Wellbeing - We want our people to feel well and thrive. We offer resources and benefits to nurture physical and mental wellbeing along with opportunities to build community and inspire creativity.

Colleague Discount – We know you'll love to build, so from day 1 you will qualify for our generous colleague discount.

Bonus - We do our best work to succeed together. When goals are reached and if eligible, you'll be rewarded through our bonus scheme.

Workplace - When you join the team you'll be assigned a primary workplace location i.e. one of our Offices, stores or factories. Our hybrid work policy means an average of 3 days per week in the office. The hiring team will discuss the policy and role eligibility with you during the recruitment process.

Children are our role models. Their curiosity, creativity and imagination inspire everything we do. We strive to create a diverse, dynamic and inclusive culture of play at the LEGO Group, where everyone feels safe, valued and they belong.

The LEGO Group is highly committed to equal employment opportunity and equal pay and seeks to encourage applicants from all backgrounds (eg. sex, gender identity or expression, race/ethnicity, national origin, sexual orientation, disability, age and religion) to apply for roles in our team.

The LEGO Group is fully committed to Children’s Rights and Child Wellbeing across the globe. Candidates offered positions with high engagement with children are required to take part in Child Safeguarding Background Screening, as a condition of the offer.

Thank you for sharing our global commitment to Children’s Rights.

Just imagine building your dream career.

Then make it real.

Join the LEGO® team today.

Wellbeing - We want our people to feel well and thrive. We offer resources and benefits to nurture physical and mental wellbeing along with opportunities to build community and inspire creativity.

Colleague Discount – We know you'll love to build, so from day 1 you will qualify for our generous colleague discount.

Bonus - We do our best work to succeed together. When goals are reached and if eligible, you'll be rewarded through our bonus scheme.

Workplace - When you join the team you'll be assigned a primary workplace location i.e. one of our Offices, stores or factories. Our hybrid work policy means an average of 3 days per week in the office. The hiring team will discuss the policy and role eligibility with you during the recruitment process.

Children are our role models. Their curiosity, creativity and imagination inspire everything we do. We strive to create a diverse, dynamic and inclusive culture of play at the LEGO Group, where everyone feels safe, valued and they belong.

The LEGO Group is highly committed to equal employment opportunity and equal pay and seeks to encourage applicants from all backgrounds (eg. sex, gender identity or expression, race/ethnicity, national origin, sexual orientation, disability, age and religion) to apply for roles in our team.

The LEGO Group is fully committed to Children’s Rights and Child Wellbeing across the globe. Candidates offered positions with high engagement with children are required to take part in Child Safeguarding Background Screening, as a condition of the offer.

Thank you for sharing our global commitment to Children’s Rights.

Just imagine building your dream career.

Then make it real.

Join the LEGO® team today.

Title: Senior Data Analyst (Relocate to Cyprus)
Place: Copenhagen, Capital Region of Denmark, Denmark
Description:
We are looking for an experienced Data Analyst to join our Player Care team at Scorewarrior. The Player Care team is at the forefront of providing customer service to our players, as well as safeguarding the community integrity through enforcing Fair & Safe Play, improving moderation efforts, and ensuring operational excellence while at it.




Scorewarrior is a game developer and publisher based in Limassol, Cyprus. The company focuses on the 4X strategy genre, with an ambitious goal to grow its hit game, Total Battle, to become the #1 strategy game in the world, and this role is key to achieving this. Our team consists of 200+ passionate, talented, self-driven, and continually developing professionals who love making high-quality games.




Our product: We are currently working on Total Battle — an MMO 4X strategy game with a deep political system and lots of player interaction. Players take on the role of a commander and need to develop their city, forge alliances, fight against monsters, and conquer other players. We're constantly developing new game mechanics, and the project is continuing to grow.




At the heart of our Player Care department, our mission is to excite our players with exceptional customer care, ensuring they feel valued and supported throughout their journey with us.




What you’ll do

Build and maintain the single "Source of Truth" semantic data layer for the entire Player Care department, enabling non-technical leads to perform ad-hoc analysis.
Design, build, and maintain reliable metrics dashboards tailored to the operational needs of different Support and Trust & Safety teams.
Ensure all operational metrics (CSAT, SLA, etc.) are clearly defined, reliable, and calculated correctly across all reports.
Assist and mentor the support team with SQL query composition and data literacy.
Conduct A/B tests to measure the effect of Support and Trust & Safety initiatives on relevant departmental and broader business metrics.
Analyze the correlation between support quality, bug-related friction, and subsequent player behavior, with a strong focus on VIP cohorts.
Partner with the safety team to analyze data for fraud detection, including social engineering and account sharing.
Perform time-series forecasting for workload volume based on the LiveOps calendar to optimize shift headcount and SLA coverage.
As a future ambition, build and maintain an early warning predictive model for VIP player churn.




What you have

Strong SQL skills and data modeling experience
Experience with BI tools (e.g. Tableau or equivalents)
Proven experience in product or operational analytics
Ability to design analytical events and metrics aligned with product goals
Strong analytical thinking and ability to translate data into actionable insights
Confident knowledge of mathematical statistics and probability theory
Hands-on experience with causal inference and A/B testing
Familiarity and advocacy of AI tooling (ideally Claude)
Patience and willingness to advocate for data literacy and help operational teams learn basic data concepts




Nice to haves

Hands-on experience with ClickHouse and dbt
Experience analyzing LiveOps features, complex game economies, or VIP player behavior, ideally in mid-core or 4x strategy games.
Experience working with Customer Support or Trust & Safety (fraud detection, toxicity)
Experience working with large player bases and complex event-driven systems




Where you’ll work

We value face-to-face communication, and we strongly believe that genuine human interactions move us closer to our ambitious goal. This is part of our culture, and this is why this job offers no remote or hybrid work opportunities.

You will be working in Limassol, Cyprus. If you’re not from around here, fear not! Our dedicated relocation team will provide comprehensive support helping you and your family move to Limassol.




What we offer

The best team in the world!
An excellent remuneration package, including a 13th salary.
Fully-covered relocation to Limassol, Cyprus for you and your family.
A modern and sunny office space close to the sea.
Private health insurance for you and your family members.
A monthly school allowance for your children at pre-school and school-age.
Lunch compensation.
Personal development opportunities paid for by the company: professional and language courses, books, etc.
Bonuses for outstanding results, years worked, getting married, and the birth of a child.
Monthly office parties and epic, annual offsite company birthday and Christmas parties.

Title: Student Worker in Data & Analytics
Description:
Do you want to gain hands-on experience with modern analytics tooling and real business-critical datasets? Are you motivated to work in an international green tech organization where your work contributes to a more sustainable future? Do you want to develop your professional skills in close collaboration with experienced and passionate colleagues?

Here Is What The Job Entails

As a Student Worker in Data & Analytics, you will support our Data & Analytics team in rebuilding and modernizing core business metrics and Power BI reporting, as the organization transitions from a legacy BI stack to a modern cloud-based data platform.

More specifically you will be:


Developing and maintaining Power BI reports and semantic models aligned to core business metrics
Defining and documenting business metrics in collaboration with stakeholders to ensure consistent reporting
Supporting report validation and quality assurance across the Power BI environment
Contributing to self-service BI by creating user guides and documentation
Assisting with ad hoc analysis and report requests from business stakeholders



This role offers you the chance to develop your skills in Data & Analytics trough hands-on experience with modern tools like Power BI and Databricks. The role combines hands-on development, close collaboration with business stakeholders, and day-to-day support to ensure reliable, well-documented, and user-friendly analytics solutions.

Meet your future team

With direct reference to our Head of Data & Analytics, you will join our newly formed Data & Analytics team currently consisting of 5 dedicated colleagues. Together, we have a clear mission to build a high-quality analytics foundation while modernizing how we deliver and report on data products. The team works closely with stakeholders across the value chain to translate business questions into clear metrics and robust data models that enable Kamstrup’s future growth. Furthermore, we foster a collaborative environment where sparring is highly encouraged, and student workers are seen as an integral and important part of the team.

Did you know? We are a highly diverse team with members from North America, Central America, Europe and Asia.

Less About Us, More About You

We are looking for a curious and motivated student worker with a strong interest in analytics and reporting that support real business decisions. You achieve work-related goals by working in a structured and service-oriented manner as well as being eager to learn from and collaborate with professional colleagues. As you develop in the role, you will also increasingly take ownership of meaningful projects.

In order to succeed we imagine that you:


Are currently studying a degree within Data Science, IT, Software Engineering, Business Analytics, or similar
Have hands-on experience with Power BI from studies, projects, or related student work
Have a solid understanding of data models and basic SQL skills (Databricks or Python experience is a plus)
Have an interest in business metrics, semantic modelling, and how data drives decisions
Bring an open and growth-oriented mindset



As we are an international organisation it is important that you are fluent in English. Danish is considered an advantage.

Whilst you may not meet all the qualifications listed above, you could still be a good candidate for the position. If you find the role intriguing, do not hesitate to apply.

We are more than an ordinary workplace

We cherish the good colleagueship which we all contribute to, the only limit is your engagement. As we are a big organization you are bound to find one or more colleagues who share the same interest as you. This might be in our board game club, on our cycling team, during our yearly summer festival, or on the padel court. For example, our Team lead, Service Delivery is also our volunteer padel instructor while one of our Software Test Engineers run weekly spinning classes.

Besides the best colleagues you could imagine, we also offer:


Work life balance through flexible working hours
Employee benefits including a healthy canteen, fitness facilities, in-house dental clinic, hairdresser, and a free health clinic
An active employee association that fosters a collaborative and supportive workplace community
Beneficial health care and insurance package



Do you want to be a part of Kamstrup?

Please submit your application by using the link below. Applications are reviewed on a continuous basis, and you can expect a response within six weeks.

The job is a part-time position, and we expect you to be able to work 15 hours per week – however, the details of the role will be agreed on an individual basis. The job will be located at Kamstrup's headquarters in Stilling, between Aarhus and Skanderborg.

If you have any questions about the position, please contact Head of Data & Analytics, Claire Bastholm Dooley on phone: +45 89 93 10 00.

Get to know us

At Kamstrup, you'll become part of a close-knit community that genuinely cares about one another, fostering connections both in and out of the workplace. Our team comprises innovators, dreamers, and doers, united by an unyielding passion to challenge the status quo, find better solutions, and constantly push the boundaries of what's possible by being driving by our purpose: We Make What You Measure Matter. With an unwavering commitment to growth, we offer opportunities for you to leverage your skills and drive our business to new heights, empowering you to leave your mark on our future and grow with us.

Explore more on our website, see our annual report, and stay updated through LinkedIn for the latest news and job openings.

Title: Machine Learning Researcher
Place: Copenhagen, Capital Region of Denmark, Denmark
Description:
Machine Learning Researcher — real feedback cycles!




Do you want to see your models make decisions in live financial markets — not sit in a notebook waiting for a quarterly review? Are you as comfortable thinking about data pipelines and calibration frameworks as you are about model architecture?




A market-leading quantitative trading firm is looking for a Machine Learning Researcher to join their modelling team in Copenhagen. They've been automating financial markets since 2008, and ML sits at the core of how they operate. This isn't a role where you hand off to an engineering team and wait. You'll own the work from data generation through to deployment — and in this environment, that cycle can be measured in hours, not months.




What you'll do




▸ Lead modelling workflow — build and improve the frameworks that turn massive financial datasets into predictive models that move with the markets.

▸ Develop and optimise — work on calibration and benchmarking frameworks, data preparation pipelines, and dataset generation at scale.

▸ Ship novel architectures — prototype and release new predictive models with a particular focus on time-series approaches.

▸ Work with real data, real stakes — your models get validated against live markets. Feedback is immediate and unambiguous.

▸ Collaborate closely — work alongside quant researchers, software developers and traders who all write code and understand the craft.




The team




Eighteen quantitative researchers across six nationalities — thirteen PhDs and five MScs — with expertise spanning statistical analysis, mathematical modelling and machine learning. No red tape, no sales layer between you and your work. Technical discussions are valued, ownership is expected, and the team genuinely enjoys what it does. Successes are celebrated.




What they're looking for




✓ Strong foundations in mathematics and statistics — you think rigorously, not just empirically. ✓ End-to-end ML experience — from data generation and wrangling through model calibration, validation and live monitoring. You've built systems that run in production, not just experiments that ran in a notebook.

✓ Research depth — whether from academia or industry, you can point to work where you designed experiments, validated results, and drew meaningful conclusions from data. Published research is a strong positive signal but not a requirement.

✓ Solid Python skills — data-wrangling, numerical programming, production-quality code.

✓ Computer science fundamentals — you understand what's happening under the hood.

✓ A scientific, inquisitive mindset — you ask why the model works, not just whether it does.




Financial markets knowledge is a plus but not a prerequisite — the team cares more about how you think and build than which domain you come from. If your background is in physical sciences, engineering, or applied mathematics and you've been working on hard prediction problems with real data, we want to hear from you.




Nice to have: If you have experience with time-series forecasting, out-of-core datasets, PyTorch, TensorFlow, XGBoost or CatBoost, C# or C/C++, and a PhD or MSc in engineering, physics, computer science, mathematics or economics that is all awesome!




What they offer




💰 Competitive compensation package

Pension, health insurance and 30 days of vacation
Modern harbourfront office in Copenhagen (Nordhavn)
Hybrid working model - 4 days in the office, 1 day remote
Flat hierarchy — high trust, high autonomy, real freedom to make decisions
Short feedback cycles — from inception to deployment can be hours, not quarters
Strong team culture with company events and trips

Title: Master Data Analyst
Place: Billund Municipality, Region of Southern Denmark, Denmark
Description:
Job Description




We are looking for a dedicated Master Data Analyst to join our dynamic team!

In this role, you will play a critical part in managing and securing data accuracy across our global production facilities, ensuring we have the right information at the right time to support planning and production.




Core Responsibilities

Being a part of the team, you will have a deep understanding of the product lifecycle process from development to production and phase out. More specifically you will:

Complete core operational Master Data tasks in ERP System such as Bill of Material creation and maintenance, Work Center, Routing and Production versions of core line components and products
Handle data of material movements between production sites to support planning requests and load levelling together with master data updates for the best optimized planning
Complete reports for data-validation to enable data governance and high quality
Collaborate with partners handling associated and supporting tasks to master data processes, especially Planning- and Production sites and Novelty Development
Participate as a collaborator in tasks of new initiatives and developments of products that has an impact towards the existing master data structure
Help optimize the way we work to enable execution of tasks with high quality and speed including building upon the development of Master Data functionalities and needs for the future




Play your part in our team succeeding

You will join the Supply Chain Foundation Master Data team, consisting of 10 dedicated employees located in Billund. Data quality is our top priority as Master Data is critical to the full Supply Chain and Planning Operations. With global standards we focus on process alignment related to Master Data; all in all to keep up a high data quality as a prerequisite for stable planning processes.

We are the interface between marketing novelty projects and planning/manufacturing with a central role in the organization to provide the foundation for Supply Chain Planning. You will also play a role to secure knowledge on master data processes and systems are applied in new initiatives – balancing the customers needs with our obligation to safeguard data structure and simplicity.




Do you have what it takes?

Your educational background could be Production Engineer or Master in Supply Chain Management. However, the educational background is secondary to the right person and experience
Experience with Microsoft Office programs especially Excel is a requirement
Experience with SAP as ERP system is a strong value for the job
Experience within master data, supply chain, quality, production planning etc. and knowhow from a production company is an advantage
Proficiency in English both written and spoken
Thrives with a combination of new tasks and daily operational tasks characterized by routines
Very detail oriented, structured and have a systematic and analytical way of working
Curious to new perspectives and brave to challenge known processes
The ability to see the end2end perspective and you continuously focus on growing your functional expertise




Please note that relocation support is not offered for this role.

Applications are reviewed on an ongoing basis. However, please note we do amend or withdraw our jobs and reserve the right to do so at any time, including prior to any advertised closing date. So, if you're interested in this role we encourage you to apply as soon as possible.




What’s in it for you?

Here is what you can expect:

Family Care Leave - We offer enhanced paid leave options for those important times.

Insurances – All colleagues are covered by our life and disability insurance which provides protection and peace of mind.

Wellbeing - We want our people to feel well and thrive. We offer resources and benefits to nurture physical and mental wellbeing along with opportunities to build community and inspire creativity.

Colleague Discount – We know you'll love to build, so from day 1 you will qualify for our generous colleague discount.

Bonus - We do our best work to succeed together. When goals are reached and if eligible, you'll be rewarded through our bonus scheme.

Workplace - When you join the team you'll be assigned a primary workplace location i.e. one of our Offices, stores or factories. Our hybrid work policy means an average of 3 days per week in the office. The hiring team will discuss the policy and role eligibility with you during the recruitment process.




Children are our role models. Their curiosity, creativity and imagination inspire everything we do. We strive to create a diverse, dynamic and inclusive culture of play at the LEGO Group, where everyone feels safe, valued and they belong.




The LEGO Group is highly committed to equal employment opportunity and equal pay and seeks to encourage applicants from all backgrounds (eg. sex, gender identity or expression, race/ethnicity, national origin, sexual orientation, disability, age and religion) to apply for roles in our team.




The LEGO Group is fully committed to Children’s Rights and Child Wellbeing across the globe. Candidates offered positions with high engagement with children are required to take part in Child Safeguarding Background Screening, as a condition of the offer.




Thank you for sharing our global commitment to Children’s Rights.

Just imagine building your dream career.

Then make it real.

Join the LEGO® team today.




We are proud supporters of Women in Data. Connect, engage and belong to the largest free female data community in the UK – visit: www.womenindata.co.uk to join our community.

“Stay connected! Follow us on LinkedIn for updates on career opportunities and more.”

  • Experience within master data, supply chain, quality, production planning etc. and knowhow from a production company is an advantage
  • Proficiency in English both written and spoken
  • Thrives with a combination of new tasks and daily operational tasks characterized by routines
  • Very detail oriented, structured and have a systematic and analytical way of working
  • Curious to new perspectives and brave to challenge known processes
  • The ability to see the end2end perspective and you continuously focus on growing your functional expertise


Please note that relocation support is not offered for this role.

Applications are reviewed on an ongoing basis. However, please note we do amend or withdraw our jobs and reserve the right to do so at any time, including prior to any advertised closing date. So, if you're interested in this role we encourage you to apply as soon as possible.


What’s in it for you?

Here is what you can expect:

Family Care Leave - We offer enhanced paid leave options for those important times.

Insurances – All colleagues are covered by our life and disability insurance which provides protection and peace of mind.

Wellbeing - We want our people to feel well and thrive. We offer resources and benefits to nurture physical and mental wellbeing along with opportunities to build community and inspire creativity.

Colleague Discount – We know you'll love to build, so from day 1 you will qualify for our generous colleague discount.

Bonus - We do our best work to succeed together. When goals are reached and if eligible, you'll be rewarded through our bonus scheme.

Workplace - When you join the team you'll be assigned a primary workplace location i.e. one of our Offices, stores or factories. Our hybrid work policy means an average of 3 days per week in the office. The hiring team will discuss the policy and role eligibility with you during the recruitment process.


Children are our role models. Their curiosity, creativity and imagination inspire everything we do. We strive to create a diverse, dynamic and inclusive culture of play at the LEGO Group, where everyone feels safe, valued and they belong.


The LEGO Group is highly committed to equal employment opportunity and equal pay and seeks to encourage applicants from all backgrounds (eg. sex, gender identity or expression, race/ethnicity, national origin, sexual orientation, disability, age and religion) to apply for roles in our team.


The LEGO Group is fully committed to Children’s Rights and Child Wellbeing across the globe. Candidates offered positions with high engagement with children are required to take part in Child Safeguarding Background Screening, as a condition of the offer.


Thank you for sharing our global commitment to Children’s Rights.

Just imagine building your dream career.

Then make it real.

Join the LEGO® team today.


We are proud supporters of Women in Data. Connect, engage and belong to the largest free female data community in the UK – visit: www.womenindata.co.uk to join our community.

“Stay connected! Follow us on LinkedIn for updates on career opportunities and more.”

Title: Studentermedhjælper til Data og BI i Sampension
Place: Copenhagen, Capital Region of Denmark, Denmark
Description:
Studentermedhjælper til Data & BI i Sampension

Har du lyst til at arbejde med data, Power BI og AI, i en pensionsvirksomhed, hvor du får praktisk erfaring med data, analyser og moderne Microsoft-teknologi og hvor dit arbejde kan ses og bruges i en organisation, der arbejder målrettet med at blive mere datadrevet? Så har vi måske studiejobbet til dig.

I Data & BI teamet arbejder vi for, at Sampensions data er pålidelige, dokumenterede og let tilgængelige, og at de omsættes til indsigter. Vores dataplatform er bygget med en Microsoft-first tilgang og tager afsæt i teknologier som Azure, Fabric, Power BI, Copilot og Purview, alt dette er med til at skabe en reel forretningsværdi i Sampension.

Om jobbet

Som studentermedhjælper bliver du en del, af et fagligt stærkt og hjælpsomt team med Data Engineers, BI-specialister og Data Governance-profiler, hvor vi prioriterer sparring, læring og ansvar, vi forventer at du har lyst til at lære også når det er svært.

Dine opgaver vil bl.a. være at:
Hjælpe med udrulning og governance af Copilot som AI-assistent i Sampension
Støtte udvikling og vedligehold af vores dataplatform i Fabric/Azure
Udvikle og vedligeholde Power BI-modeller og rapporter
Løse og lede mindre selvstændige data- og analyseopgaver


Arbejdstiden forventes at være 15-20 timer om ugen, og der vil være fleksible forhold i forbindelse med både eksaminer og ferie, ligesom der også vil være mulighed for at lægge yderligere timer i perioder, hvis det ønskes.

Hvem er du?

Du er i gang med en relevant videregående uddannelse inden for IT, data eller økonomi (fx datalogi, HA(it.) el.lign.) og har interesse for data, analyse og datadrevne beslutninger. Har du kendskab til Power BI, SQL, Python eller Copilot er det et plus, men ikke et krav.

Vi forstiller os, at du:
går struktureret og ansvarligt til dine opgaver
er nysgerrig, opsøgende og trives med at samarbejde samt selvstændigt arbejde
motiveres af et travlt og resultatorienteret miljø
har gode analytiske evner og er god til skriftlig formidling


Bliv en del af en velfungerende organisation i udvikling

Vi tilbyder dig at blive del af en velfungerende, kvalitetsbevidst og løsningsorienteret organisation. Et miljø med fokus på faglig og personlig udvikling, og på at skabe resultater til fordel for kunderne.

Løn og ansættelsesvilkår fastsættes efter kvalifikationer og omfatter naturligvis en attraktiv pensionsordning betalt af Sampension.

Vi bor med udsigt til vandet i et lyst og åbent domicil tæt på byen. Derudover har vi en række personalegoder, fx idrætsforening, motionsrum, forskellige sociale arrangementer og en rigtig god personalerestaurant.

Vi glæder os til at høre fra dig

Vi indkalder løbende til samtaler. Så send hurtigst muligt dit CV og et par linjer om dig selv og hvorfor du skal være vores nye kollega.

Har du spørgsmål, er du velkommen til at kontakte IT-leder Steen Norstrøm på mail SNS@sampension.dk eller BI Developer Niklas Sachs Tastum på mail NST@sampension.dk.

Sampension er et af Danmarks største pensionsselskaber med ca. 325.000 kunder og medlemmer. Vi er kundeejet og administrerer arbejdsmarkeds- og firmapensioner for Sampension Livsforsikring, Pensionskassen Arkitekter & Designere, Pensionskassen for Jordbrugsakademikere & Dyrlæger samt ISP Pension.

Title: Data engineer hos Alm. Brand Group
Place: Copenhagen Municipality, Capital Region of Denmark, Denmark
Description:
Til Alm. Brand Group søger ITPA Consulting:




Fastansat data engineer til datadomænet




Brænder du for ansvar og direkte indflydelse på modellering og design af dataprodukter så de både tilgodeser rapportlæsere, analytikere og andre udviklere i Datadomænet?




Er du klar til at blive en del af vores rejse? Nu kan du blive data engineer i vores datadomæne, hvor der venter dig et udfordrende job.




Alm. Brand Group er inde i en rivende udvikling, hvor der er stor appetit på data løsninger. Vi ønsker at kunne tiltrække og fastholde de dygtigste medarbejdere, der i fællesskab leverer det bedste til vores kunder og sikrer et stærkt fundament for vækst og lønsomhed. Kendte for ordentlighed, professionalisme og for at kunne indfri vores kunders behov.




Vi har over de sidste par år bygget en ny Enterprise Data Platform (EDP) i Azure Cloud. Platformen er primært bygget vha. Azure Databricks og følger deres Lakehouse tilgang. Vi har haft fokus på at integrere Codan i vores systemlandskab, som vi er lykkedes med. Næste fase er at vi har kvalitetsdata til rådighed, så vores analytikere kan udnytte data optimalt, men også især den transformation, som vi står overfor i forbindelse med AI Agenter.




Datadomænet består af 3 afdelinger – Data Engineering, Data Solutions og AI. Data Engineering afdelingen, som du bliver ansat i, består af 11 danske data engineers og pt. 6 polske konsulenter.




I hverdagen indgår man i et team med 4-6 kollegaer fordelt på forskellige roller – manager, analytiker og data engineer. De forskellige teams er orienteret mod vores kerneområder som f.eks. skade, salg m.fl. Udover dette, så investerer vi også i fagligheden hos os selv via review møder, hvor vi modner løsninger og platformen i fællesskab.




Vi tilbyder

Tæt kontakt til kravstillere i forretningen
Alsidige opgaver med fx workshops, datamodeller og udvikling af data pipelines
Arbejde med data fra flere områder af organisationen
Indflydelse på opgaver og løsninger
Et godt og ambitiøst arbejdsmiljø, hvor vi sætter faglighed højt




Hos Alm. Brand Group har vi fokus på det hele menneske og tilbyder dig en hel palette af medarbejdergoder: Det er bl.a. hybridarbejde, 5 feriefridage, sundhedsforsikring, en flot pensionsordning og bruttolønsordning.




Din baggrund

Vi forestiller os, at du er ambitiøs og brænder for at forvandle rå data til indsigt. Du formår at balancere teori og praksis i en til tider hektisk hverdag. Samtidig er du nysgerrig – både på det forretningsmæssige og det tekniske. Du sidder i dag i en lignende stilling som konsulent eller intern og har bevist, at du både kan samarbejde og omdanne forretningskrav til brugbare dataløsninger.




Vi forventer du kan:

Omsætte forretningskrav til løsningsdesign og data modeller.
Udvikle og vedligeholde data pipelines og data produkter.
Bidrage til vores fælles kodebase og tage ejerskab for elementer heri.
Sikre at vores løsninger er bygget på et godt pragmatisk fundament.
Samarbejde med kolleger i og udenfor teamet




Herudover så har du:

3-5 års erfaring med ETL, ELT, Data Warehouse og generel data-arkitektur i en moderne dataplatform.




En relevant videregående uddannelse, gerne på kandidatniveau




Gode dansk- og engelskkundskaber, skriftligt og mundtligt




Det er et plus, hvis du har erfaring med en eller flere af de teknologier og metoder vi anvender: Azure, SQL, Spark, Git, Python, CI/CD, streaming og terraform.




Har vi fanget din interesse?




Det håber vi meget, og ser frem til at svare på dine spørgsmål vedrørende denne rolle. Du er velkommen til at kontakte Kenneth Valeur Andersen fra ITPA Consulting ApS, på +45 53 74 71 84

Title: Data Engineer
Description:
Who We Are Is What We Do.

Deel is the all-in-one payroll and HR platform for global teams. Our vision is to unlock global opportunity for every person, team, and business. Built for the way the world works today, Deel combines HRIS, payroll, compliance, benefits, performance, and equipment management into one seamless platform. With AI-powered tools and a fully owned payroll infrastructure, Deel supports every worker type in 150+ countries—helping businesses scale smarter, faster, and more compliantly.

Among the largest globally distributed companies in the world, our team of 7,000 spans more than 100 countries, speaks 74 languages, and brings a connected and dynamic culture that drives continuous learning and innovation for our customers.

Why should you be part of our success story?

As the fastest-growing Software as a Service (SaaS) company in history, Deel is transforming how global talent connects with world-class companies – breaking down borders that have traditionally limited both hiring and career opportunities. We're not just building software; we're creating the infrastructure for the future of work, enabling a more diverse and inclusive global economy. In 2024 alone, we paid $11.2 billion to workers in nearly 100 currencies and provided healthcare and benefits to workers in 109 countries—ensuring people get paid and protected, no matter where they are.

Our momentum is reflected in our achievements and customer satisfaction: CNBC Disruptor 50, Forbes Cloud 100, Deloitte Fast 500, and repeated recognition on Y Combinator’s top companies list – all while maintaining a 4.83 average rating from 15,000 reviews across G2, Trustpilot, Captera, Apple and Google.

Your experience at Deel will be a career accelerator. At the forefront of the global work revolution, you'll tackle complex challenges that impact millions of people's working lives. With our momentum—backed by a $17.3 billion valuation and $1 B in Annual Recurring Revenue (ARR) in just over five years—you'll drive meaningful impact while building expertise that makes you a sought-after leader in the transformation of global work.

Responsibilities


Design, build, and maintain efficient data pipelines (ETL processes) to integrate data from various source systems into the data warehouse.
Develop and optimize data warehouse schemas and tables to support analytics and reporting needs.
Write and refine complex SQL queries and use scripting (e.g., Python) to transform and aggregate large datasets.
Implement data quality measures (such as validation checks and cleansing routines) to ensure data integrity and reliability.
Collaborate with data analysts, data scientists, and other engineers to understand data requirements and deliver appropriate solutions.
Document pipeline designs, data flows, and data definitions for transparency and future reference, adhering to team standards.
Handle multiple tasks or projects simultaneously, prioritizing work and communicating progress to stakeholders to meet deadlines.


Qualifications


Bachelor’s or Master’s degree in a relevant field (e.g., Computer Science, Mathematics, Physics).
At least 3 years of experience in a data engineering or similar backend data development role.
Strong SQL skills and experience with data modeling and building data warehouse solutions.
Proficiency in at least one programming language (e.g., Python) for data processing and pipeline automation.
Familiarity with ETL tools and workflow orchestration frameworks (e.g., Apache Airflow or similar).
Experience implementing data quality checks and working with large-scale datasets.
Good problem-solving abilities, plus strong communication and teamwork skills to work with cross-functional stakeholders.


Total Rewards

Our workforce deserves fair and competitive pay that meets them where they are. With scalable benefits, rewards, and perks, our total rewards programs reflect our commitment to inclusivity and access for all.

Some things you’ll enjoy


Stock grant opportunities dependent on your role, employment status and location
Additional perks and benefits based on your employment status and country
The flexibility of remote work, including optional WeWork access


At Deel, we’re an equal-opportunity employer that values diversity and positively encourage applications from suitably qualified and eligible candidates regardless of race, religion, sex, national origin, gender, sexual orientation, age, marital status, veteran status, disability status, pregnancy or maternity or other applicable legally protected characteristics.

Unless otherwise agreed, we will communicate with job applicants using Deel-specific emails, which include @deel.com and other acquired company emails like @payspace.com and @paygroup.com. You can view the most up-to-date job listings at Deel by visiting our careers page.

Deel is an equal-opportunity employer and is committed to cultivating a diverse and inclusive workplace that reflects different abilities, backgrounds, beliefs, experiences, identities and perspectives.

Deel will provide accommodations on request throughout the recruitment, selection and assessment process for applicants with disabilities. If you require accommodations, please inform our Talent Acquisition Team via this link and a team member will be in touch to ensure your equal participation. If you have difficulty accessing the form, please email at recruiting@deel.com.

As part of our hiring process, we primarily rely on interviews and role-related assessments. In limited cases, we may also consider informal background information relevant to the role, in line with our privacy and fairness obligations.

This application process does utilise Automated Employment Decision Tools (AEDT) and AI systems to assist in evaluating candidates based on experience level, technical skills and qualifications. As a fully remote company, we also utilise AI-powered deepfake and fraud detection technologies to verify the authenticity of candidate identities and interactions during assessments and interviews. This processing is conducted in compliance with applicable Data Protection, AI Governance and Labour Laws. We ensure human oversight is maintained in all final hiring decisions. Your personal data is not used to train AI models. For more information on how we process your personal data, please see our Privacy Policy.


For NYC Residents: In accordance with NYC Local Law 144, an independent bias audit has been conducted on AEDT; results are available at Ashby, Covey.

Title: Student Worker within Business Intelligence/Enabling
Place: Billund, Region of Southern Denmark, Denmark
Description:
Job Description

Could you see yourself helping transform global maintenance through data, digital reporting, and business intelligence?

Join Global Maintenance Efficiency Partner – Excellence Team and help create automated digital solutions that give maintenance leaders easy access to maintenance data, KPI insights, and strategic performance overviews.

Bring your analytical mindset and digital skills into Global Maintenance Excellence, where you will help develop Power BI reporting, data insights, and automated solutions that turn maintenance data into business impact.


Core Responsibilities





Design, develop, and maintain Global Maintenance KPI dashboards and reports in Power BI.
Visualize maintenance data by structuring information, creating clear overviews, and turning data into actionable insights for maintenance leaders.
Prepare ad hoc data analyses and visualizations to support business decisions.
Support automation and digitalization of reporting processes across Global Maintenance Excellence.
Collaborate with Maintenance Partners and internal stakeholders to understand reporting needs and translate them into simple, visual solutions.
Contribute to continuous improvement of data quality, reporting standards, and maintenance performance transparency.
Support innovation by identifying opportunities to improve how maintenance data is used in decision-making.




Play your part in our team succeeding

Global Maintenance Excellence supports the transformation of the global maintenance organization by providing insights, processes, standards, and digital tools that create transparency and enable better decision-making.

Our focus is to develop and deploy the foundations, processes, and standards needed to help global maintenance meet future business demand. A key part of this transformation is

strengthening our digital capabilities, enabling future-ready factories through automated reporting, data transparency, and stronger maintenance performance insights.

In this role, you will support the development of KPI reporting and digital overviews that help maintenance leaders understand how maintenance activities, performance, and responsibilities contribute to the global maintenance strategy. You will work closely with Maintenance Partners and stakeholders to turn complex maintenance data into simple, visual, and actionable insights.

Do you have what it takes?





Bachelor’s or Master student in an IT or business related field or equivalent working experience.
Strong Excel skills and experience working with structured data.
Knowledge of Power BI and data visualization.
Experience or interest in Databricks, data preparation, and data modelling.
Basic understanding of PowerApps, Power Automate, and SharePoint.
Strong analytical mindset with the ability to structure complex information.
Ability to communicate detailed information in a simple, visual, and business-relevant way.
Fluent English communication skills, both written and spoken.
Service-minded, curious, and comfortable working with multiple stakeholders in a dynamic environment.
Good sense of humor and an accommodating personality.




Nice to have





Knowledge of maintenance, manufacturing, operations, and business reporting.
Experience with project management or performance follow-up.
Experience with process development or digital workflow improvement.
Ability to combine and reuse existing knowledge to create new solutions.
Proactive mindset with the ability to identify and act on improvement opportunities.
Interest in digital transformation and data-driven decision-making.




Applications are reviewed on an ongoing basis. However, please note we do amend or withdraw our jobs and reserve the right to do so at any time, including prior to any advertised closing date. So, if you're interested in this role we encourage you to apply as soon as possible.

What’s in it for you?

Here is what you can expect

Family Care Leave - We offer enhanced paid leave options for those important times.

Insurances – All colleagues are covered by our life and disability insurance which provides protection and peace of mind.

Wellbeing - We want our people to feel well and thrive. We offer resources and benefits to nurture physical and mental wellbeing along with opportunities to build community and inspire creativity.

Colleague Discount – We know you'll love to build, so from day 1 you will qualify for our generous colleague discount.

Bonus - We do our best work to succeed together. When goals are reached and if eligible, you'll be rewarded through our bonus scheme.

Workplace - When you join the team you'll be assigned a primary workplace location i.e. one of our Offices, stores or factories. Our hybrid work policy means an average of 3 days per week in the office. The hiring team will discuss the policy and role eligibility with you during the recruitment process.

Children are our role models. Their curiosity, creativity and imagination inspire everything we do. We strive to create a diverse, dynamic and inclusive culture of play at the LEGO Group, where everyone feels safe, valued and they belong.

The LEGO Group is highly committed to equal employment opportunity and equal pay and seeks to encourage applicants from all backgrounds (eg. sex, gender identity or expression, race/ethnicity, national origin, sexual orientation, disability, age and religion) to apply for roles in our team.

The LEGO Group is fully committed to Children’s Rights and Child Wellbeing across the globe. Candidates offered positions with high engagement with children are required to take part in Child Safeguarding Background Screening, as a condition of the offer.

Thank you for sharing our global commitment to Children’s Rights.

Just imagine building your dream career.

Then make it real.

Join the LEGO® team today.

Title: Register in our candidate data base
Place: Copenhagen, Capital Region of Denmark, Denmark
Description:
Veo is a global leader in AI-based sports camera technology. Our innovative, fully automatic camera solution enables sports teams to record matches and training sessions without a camera operator. We’re democratizing the world of sports by granting video analysis for teams on all levels—a privilege that used to be only for the few. More than 50,000 clubs in 90+ countries record their games every week.

Growing as fast as we do in Veo means that every day is different, exciting, and challenging, both on the front line and in the back office.

But that’s not the most remarkable thing about us.

The coolest thing is our people. We’ve attracted some of the brightest minds in the industry. They are the reason we can create a great product and do it while enjoying ourselves.

Does your dream job at Veo not appear on the list, then sign up in our candidate data base and we will get in contact with you if the right opportunity arises for you.

Our Copenhagen office mirrors our innovative technology – with an indoor ball court, rooftop terrace, and well-equipped gym. We have created a facility that supports connecting disciplines across the business, facilitates creative thinking, and gives the space to engage with colleagues, our global partners, and the entire Veo community.

Values we hold at Veo:

Dare - We take initiative, stay curious, and challenge the status quo in our work.

Own it - We drive impact by identifying opportunities, taking responsibility, and confidently executing.

Level up - We embrace growth, continuously learning and shaping our skills.

Play as a team - We collaborate across a diverse organization, leveraging shared insights for stronger

outcomes.

Customer Centric - We deliver value by having close partnerships and a deep understanding of our customers (internally and externally)

We value diversity and inclusion and welcome applicants from all backgrounds, even if your experience doesn’t completely match every qualification listed. What matters most is your creativity, potential, and ability to make meaningful contributions.

Title: Quantitative Analyst
Description:
Do you want to gain a deep understanding of the power market and contribute directly to trading decisions? Do you thrive in a high‑paced, data‑driven environment where your analytical skills shape tomorrow’s strategies? If you’re ready for a role with real responsibility and impact, join our team as a Quantitative Analyst and help drive disciplined, high‑quality trading outcomes.




We are seeking a highly motivated individual with strong quantitative skills to join our team as a Quantitative Analyst. In this role, you will work closely with the team, applying data‑driven insights, modelling, and risk thinking to take ownership of disciplined trading decisions. Your ability to transform analysis and code into actionable insights will directly contribute to improving the quality and profitability of our trading decisions.




In this role, you will gain deep insights into our trading strategies and tools that provide a significant competitive advantage. You can expect a steep learning curve and growth opportunities in an environment where flat hierarchy and team spirit drive our success. You will experience a high degree of autonomy and see your own ideas come to life and see your ideas quickly evolve into tangible solutions that make a meaningful impact on our trading operations.




Key Responsibilities

Market Analysis: Conduct in-depth, data‑driven analysis of power markets including supply and demand fundamentals, regulatory developments, weather‑driven patterns, and geopolitical impacts to uncover actionable trading opportunities and support strategic decision‑making.
Risk Management: Implement robust risk management practices by monitoring position limits, conducting stress testing, and assessing exposure to volatile market conditions to ensure controlled and compliant trading activities.
Tool Development: Develop, maintain, and optimize a range of analytical, real‑time monitoring, and reporting tools that enhance trading decision‑making and operational efficiency. Build automated dashboards and data pipelines to produce accurate, timely reports on trading performance, risk metrics, and key market trends.
Model Development: Continuously improve trading models through back testing and market‑driven data analysis to strengthen and support profitable trading decisions.
Collaboration: Work closely with the team to enhance trading strategies and technical setup, ensuring clear and consistent communication across all team members.
Trading Application: Apply insights from analysis, modelling and risk assessment to confidently support and execute well‑founded trading decisions that meet all required risk, compliance, and performance standards.




Qualifications and Requirements

As a candidate, we expect you:

Hold a bachelor’s or master’s degree in mathematics‑economics, economics, statistics, engineering, or another quantitative field.
Have strong quantitative and analytical skills to interpret complex data and translate it into well‑founded trading insights.
Are proficient in Python and comfortable with coding. Experience with other code languages is considered an advantage.
Have a strong interest in financial and energy markets and a desire to deepen your understanding of power‑market dynamics.
Demonstrate excellent problem‑solving skills, high attention to detail, and the ability to adapt quickly to changing market conditions.
Communicate clearly and effectively, presenting technical and analytical concepts in a way that is easy for both technical and non‑technical team members to understand.




What We Offer

As an employer, we offer:

A full-time position that gives you the opportunity to apply your acquired skills.
Support in your role from experienced professionals.
Access to unique technologies and resources.
An opportunity to work at the forefront of power trading, contributing to groundbreaking strategies.
Opportunities for career growth and advancement.
Competitive compensation package.
A modern office in the heart of Aarhus.
Professional and committed colleagues with high ambitions.
Inspiring, collaborative, and innovative work environment.
A place where your contributions are recognized and have a meaningful impact on the company's success.




A Profitable Growth Company with a Clear Vision

ENZEE is an ambitious growth company specialized in energy trading across the European continent. We envision a sustainable world and therefore, every day at ENZEE is a day with purpose. We belief that well-functioning and efficient energy markets are a necessity towards a net zero future which is exactly where we contribute with our expertise.




The ENZEE team consists of highly qualified, experienced, and knowledgeable industry professionals with a wide range of expertise in the energy markets. As a member of our team, you can expect a steep learning curve, great career growth opportunities, and the possibility of making a significant contribution.




Jobstart & Type

As soon as possible. We will be screening candidates on an ongoing basis
Full-time position
Location: Aarhus




ENZEE is dedicated to fostering a diverse and inclusive workplace and therefore we encourage all candidates to apply, regardless of gender, age, ethnicity, sexual orientation, or disabilities.




For further inquiries, please contact Emmett Leach: +45 42 55 31 12
