Title: Social Media Koordinator til DR Koncerthuset
Place: Copenhagen Municipality, Capital Region of Denmark, Denmark
Description:
Brænder du for kultur, musik og markedsføring – og vil du være med til at få nogle af Danmarks stærkeste musikbrands til at leve på sociale medier? Som Social Media Koordinator i DR Koncerthuset bliver du en central del af fortællingen om DR Koncerthuset og DR’s kor og orkestre.

Om os

I DR Koncerthuset arbejder vi hver dag for at skabe store musikalske oplevelser, både live og digitalt – fra DR Symfoniorkestret, DR Pigekoret, DR Big Band og DR Vokalensemblet til talks, særlige events og rytmiske koncerter med tidens største artister som Drew Sycamore, Pil og When Saints Go Machine.

Du bliver en del af et markedsføringsteam, som lige nu består af 11 personer, der arbejder med kampagner, PR, kommunikation, sociale medier, CRM, konceptudvikling, strategisk kommunikation og design.

I social media teamet har vi høje ambitioner og fart på vækstkurven. I 2025 har vi opnået en følgertilvækst på vores sociale kanaler på 50% samt over 20.000.000 visninger på vores organiske indhold på tværs af Facebook og Instagram.

I 2026 er ambitionerne ikke mindre. Derfor søger vi en Social Media Koordinator til at assistere vores to Social Media Managers.

Om stillingen

Som Social Media Koordinator hos DR Koncerthuset får du berøring med opgaver indenfor både den betalte og den organiske del af sociale medier.

Du bliver en del af et dynamisk og engageret team omkring både DR Koncerthuset og DR’s kor og orkestre, hvorfor du både kommer til at arbejde med rytmisk og klassisk musik.

De primære arbejdsopgaver bevæger sig indenfor Community Management, koordinering, publicering samt planlægning af indhold på tværs af 10-12 profiler.

Dine arbejdsopgaver

Din fremtidige rolle indeholder en bred vifte af social media opgaver, som tilsammen udgør brikkerne, der får hele puslespillet til at gå op. På en typisk arbejdsdag, vil du bl.a.:


Besvare kommentarer og beskeder på Facebook, Instagram og YouTube på tværs af alle husets brands
Publicere og planlægge organisk indhold på Facebook, Instagram og YouTube i tæt samarbejde med Social Media Managers
Oprette og opdatere vores Facebook begivenheder
Bidrage med produktion og publicering af stories ifbm. enkelte koncerter og events
Hjælpe med opsætning af annoncer i Meta Ads Manager i tæt samarbejde med Social Media Managers
Booke og koordinere med freelance indholdsproducenter ifbm. optagelser til sociale medier
Oprette kontrakter samt hjælpe med betaling af freelancers
Hjælpe med at ajourføre budgetter
Opdatere Monday (vores interne projektstyringsværktøj) med relevante opgaver og status


Om dig

Du er nyuddannet indenfor kommunikations- og markedsføringsfag eller har få års erfaring med sociale medier. Rollen kan tilpasses dig, uanset om du har 0 eller 2 års erfaring.

Vi forestiller os, at du:


Skriver sikkert, er knivskarp til dansk grammatik og kan tilpasse tone og form på din tekst til målgruppen.
Er struktureret, nysgerrig og trives med både praktiske og koordinerende opgaver.
Er typen, der både kan bevare overblikket og have øje for detaljerne, også i perioder med stramme deadlines.
Har interesse for kultur, musik og formidling - og gerne et blik for gode historier.
Har mod på at arbejde tæt sammen med mange forskellige fagligheder og byde ind med idéer.


Hvad vi tilbyder

Vi tilbyder en spændende stilling med en stejl læringskurve indenfor sociale medier. Du får tæt sparring fra to erfarne Social Media Managers og mulighed for gradvist at få mere ansvar, efterhånden som du bliver tryg i rollen. Samtidig kommer du til at arbejde med og have indflydelse på nogle af Danmarks stærkeste brands og aktører i musikmiljøet.

Du bliver en del af et kreativt og ambitiøst team, der arbejder tæt sammen om at skabe oplevelser i verdensklasse. I vores hus er du sikret en hverdag fyldt med musik og kultur - hvor du er med til at bringe DR Koncerthuset og DR’s kor og orkestre endnu tættere på publikum.

Vi planlægger at afholde 1. samtaler onsdag d. 17. juni.

Om DR Koncerthuset

DR Koncerthuset huser nogle af Danmarks bedste kor og orkestre og byder hvert år publikum velkommen til et bredt udvalg af koncerter og events - fra klassisk musik til indie, talks, pop, rock, jazz og filmkoncerter. Vi arbejder målrettet for at bringe musikken tættere på publikum og skabe store oplevelser - både live og digitalt.

Du kommer til at arbejde i DR Byen på Emil Holms Kanal 20 i København, som har metro lige til døren. DR ønsker at afspejle det danske samfund. Derfor opfordrer vi alle kvalificerede kandidater uanset køn, alder, religion, handicap og etnisk baggrund til at søge.

HTML:
Brænder du for kultur, musik og markedsføring – og vil du være med til at få nogle af Danmarks stærkeste musikbrands til at leve på sociale medier? Som Social Media Koordinator i DR Koncerthuset bliver du en central del af fortællingen om DR Koncerthuset og DR’s kor og orkestre.

Om os

I DR Koncerthuset arbejder vi hver dag for at skabe store musikalske oplevelser, både live og digitalt – fra DR Symfoniorkestret, DR Pigekoret, DR Big Band og DR Vokalensemblet til talks, særlige events og rytmiske koncerter med tidens største artister som Drew Sycamore, Pil og When Saints Go Machine.

Du bliver en del af et markedsføringsteam, som lige nu består af 11 personer, der arbejder med kampagner, PR, kommunikation, sociale medier, CRM, konceptudvikling, strategisk kommunikation og design.

I social media teamet har vi høje ambitioner og fart på vækstkurven. I 2025 har vi opnået en følgertilvækst på vores sociale kanaler på 50% samt over 20.000.000 visninger på vores organiske indhold på tværs af Facebook og Instagram.

I 2026 er ambitionerne ikke mindre. Derfor søger vi en Social Media Koordinator til at assistere vores to Social Media Managers.

Om stillingen

Som Social Media Koordinator hos DR Koncerthuset får du berøring med opgaver indenfor både den betalte og den organiske del af sociale medier.

Du bliver en del af et dynamisk og engageret team omkring både DR Koncerthuset og DR’s kor og orkestre, hvorfor du både kommer til at arbejde med rytmisk og klassisk musik.

De primære arbejdsopgaver bevæger sig indenfor Community Management, koordinering, publicering samt planlægning af indhold på tværs af 10-12 profiler.

Dine arbejdsopgaver

Din fremtidige rolle indeholder en bred vifte af social media opgaver, som tilsammen udgør brikkerne, der får hele puslespillet til at gå op. På en typisk arbejdsdag, vil du bl.a.:

  • Besvare kommentarer og beskeder på Facebook, Instagram og YouTube på tværs af alle husets brands
  • Publicere og planlægge organisk indhold på Facebook, Instagram og YouTube i tæt samarbejde med Social Media Managers
  • Oprette og opdatere vores Facebook begivenheder

  • Booke og koordinere med freelance indholdsproducenter ifbm. optagelser til sociale medier
  • Oprette kontrakter samt hjælpe med betaling af freelancers

Title: Support Analyst
Description:
About the Company







We do not automate for the sake of automation. We do not innovate for the sake of innovation. We specialize in high-precision, reliable outdoor robots for marking and positioning because we want to offer a better way of working. That's why we exist. We are a strong team of committed and dedicated colleagues with a solid background in precision and positioning technologies. We listen to our customers and ensure an optimal customer experience. That's our business. If you want to know more about TinyMobileRobots, please visit: www.tinymobilerobots.com







About the Role







We offer an exciting opportunity to play an important role in the continued growth of TinyMobileRobots working with sports and infrastructure robots. We improve the way our customers work and how automation technology is used in daily work, whether it's a football club marking football pitches or a larger contractor marking a major highway project. We are looking for a service-minded customer ambassador who is patient, has a sense of humour, and an eye for detail, so our customers always receive the best support. Your main responsibilities will be online phone support, including troubleshooting the customers' robots and escalating complex issues to the next level of support. It is a full-time position at our headquarters in Malling near Aarhus.







Responsibilities







Ensuring the absolute best customer support, with positive prompt assistance
Addressing routine issues such as internet, GPS, and common software/hardware troubleshooting.
Responding to customer inquiries through phone, email, or WhatsApp.
Documenting and logging all support calls in Zendesk and our other support tools.
Escalating complex or unresolved issues to Tier 3 support when necessary.




Qualifications







You love speaking to customers and supporting them in finding the best solutions
A positive attitude, motivation, and strong work ethic
Capable of delivering a strong support and troubleshooting experience
Strong ability to communicate both orally and in writing to customers, internal staff, and management
Integrates as a natural part of a strong team
Accustomed to multitasking and moving in an often fast-paced environment with attention to detail
Ability to recognize, analyse, and effectively solve problems in a timely manner
Able to diagnose and troubleshoot via remote access tools
You have a broad range of skills in e.g. network and desktop applications, methods, and procedures
Experience with electronic and electrical components and troubleshooting
Experience with engineering is a bonus but not required




Required Skills







Besides English, additional language skills will only be considered a bonus.







Preferred Skills







We will invest in the best training and onboarding, so you feel comfortable when supporting our valued customers. We offer the opportunity to grow within the support team and take more responsibility over time. We have a strong sense of unity across professional groups and organize, for example, Christmas lunches, summer parties, participation in running events, and much more. You will have the opportunity to participate in a lunch buffet scheme and enjoy free fruit and coffee.







Pay range and compensation package







We offer a competitive salary, with the possibility of a good pension scheme and health insurance. Your new workplace will be TinyMobileRobots' headquarters in Malling south of Aarhus, where you will become part of our fantastic support team. We are ambitious and work together to deliver high quality across the organization. We are located close to light rail and public transport, and we are connected to the E45 motorway via the Giber Ringvej shortcut.







Equal Opportunity Statement







TinyMobileRobots emphasizes balance, responsibility, and development. Balance between work and leisure because it enhances the joy of work and life – and thus productivity – for the individual. That's why, for example, we have a flexible approach to working hours, taking into consideration the work tasks. Responsibility in terms of employing staff from diverse backgrounds, as we see workplace diversity as a strength. And development in the form of regular, annual employee development talks combined with offers of relevant further education, so you continuously improve yourself to your own and our advantage. TinyMobileRobots is an equal opportunity employer and does not discriminate in employment or hiring practices in accordance with the requirements of applicable Danish legislation, including race, color, religion, national origin, ancestry, age, gender, marital status, military status, sexual orientation, disability, or medical condition.







If you have any questions, send them to HR@tinymobilerobots.com or call Henrik Rochat Rasmussen, Group COO, at +45 40103065.

Title: Graduate Program Data Science & Analytics - Denmark
Place: Ballerup Municipality, Capital Region of Denmark, Denmark
Description:
Job Description

Are you looking to kick off your career journey?

Do you like the idea of joining the European PayTech?

If so, you are at the right place!

Our Graduate Program offers you the chance to create your unique career path within our diverse international team, operating in more than 25 countries. Rotate among different departments, learn from experienced professionals, and make a difference by working on real business cases.

About The Program

Our Graduate Program is more than a learning path, it's a journey where you will work on real business cases and have a direct impact on business results.

What Can You Expect?


Career Path: Start with a permanent contract from day one. Over 12 months, experience two rotations across departments and transition into a landing role where you can continue to grow.
Training & Development: Receive a well-structured, dedicated Project Management training program, tailored to strengthen your ability to lead projects and deliver results. Plus, gain access to a wide range of learning opportunities through our Digital PayTech University, empowering you to continuously build your skills and expand your knowledge.
Impact & Networking: Contribute to real business solutions and expand your network by working alongside talented colleagues and senior leaders from across Europe.


Who are We Looking For?

If this sounds like you, we encourage you to apply:


You have graduated within the past 12 months or will graduate within the next 6 months with a master’s degree in Engineering, STEM disciplines, Economics, Business, Law, or related fields, and are available to work full-time.
You are fluent in English and Danish.
You bring international experience, whether through study, work abroad, or collaboration in a global environment.
You are a career starter with no more than one year of professional experience.
You have the legal right to work in Denmark (e.g., residency, visa).
You are committed, curious, and eager to take ownership of projects while thriving in a team environment.
You see formal training as just the beginning and aim to make a difference in everything you do.


Diversity, Equity and Inclusion

We are committed to building a diverse culture where talents from all walks of life feel they truly belong. By applying to our Graduate Program, you are one step closer to joining us. Next starts with you, everyday.

Apply Now!

Don't miss out on this amazing opportunity. Apply today and discover what's in it for you!

Please ensure both your CV and all answers in the application form are completed in English.

About Us

Are you interested in joining one of Europe's leading PayTech companies? Do you want to be part of a diverse team, collaborating with colleagues from across Europe? If so, you're in the right place!

About The Team

We are the result of merging Nexi, Nets, and Sia, with a presence in over 25 countries. With over 80 years of experience, we offer plenty of opportunities for personal and professional growth

At Nexi, we learn, adapt, and think like entrepreneurs. Discover more about our culture by visiting this page: https://www.nexigroup.com/en/people/life-at-nexi/overview/?utm_source=career-platform&utm_medium=job-openings&utm_content=about-the-team

Title: Gym Staff - Kennedys Plads
Description:
Jobtitel

Gym Staff - Kennedys Plads

Bliv en del af holdet bag Danmarks største Fitnessfællesskab

Er du er på udkig efter et deltidsjob med mening, eller drømmer du om at starte din karriere indenfor fitness og sundhed, så glæder vi os til at få dig med på teamet hos PureGym Kennedys plads i Aalborg

Hvorfor blive en del af PureGym?

Hos PureGym ved vi, at vores medarbejdere er vores største styrke. Vi værdsætter din unikke personlighed, dine ideer og din vilje til at skabe og være en del af noget større. Vi arbejder for at skabe en arbejdsplads, hvor du føler dig værdsat, udfordret og inspireret til at vokse – både personligt og professionelt.

Hvis dine fremtidige karriereønsker er indenfor ledelse og fitness har vi karriereveje med interne uddannelser – både via digital læringsplatform og i kurser som holdinstruktør.

Om rollen

Som Gym Staff vil du være PureGyms ansigt udadtil, og være den, der sikrer den gode medlemsoplevelse og det motiverende træningsmiljø. Du vil arbejde både selvstændigt, og tæt sammen med dine kolleger, og din Gym Manager. Dine opgaver vil spænde bredt – fra daglig rengøring af centeret til vedligeholdelse af maskiner. Derudover vil du og dit team være centrets repræsentanter på vores lokale SoMe-kanal og viser stolt PureGyms teal-farve frem i lokalområdet.

Til denne Gym Staff rolle søger vi én til vores team hos Kennedys Plads, til 30 - 34 timer om ugen. De vagter, som kan blive dine, vil som udgangspunkt være: Mandag til fredag - der kan forekomme weekender

Dine Opgaver


Være serviceminded og skabe den gode stemning sammen med vores medlemmer
Hjælpe medlemmer med at navigere i selvbetjeningsløsninger (appen, hjemmeside)
Oprydning, rengøring og vedligeholdelse af udstyr – så centeret altid står skarpt og indbydende
Give vores medlemmer en god start og guide dem i centret i brugen af vores udstyr
Sørge for, at vores salgsautomater er fyldte og klar til medlemskøb
Hjælpe med lagerstyring og lageroptælling
Deltage i centerets SoMe aktiviteter, hvor du også er med på kameraret.
Stå i spidsen for Functional Express, som er træning af en mindre gruppe medlemmer ude på gulvet. Du er med til at formidle øvelser og skabe god energi.
Oplære dine nye kollegaer og samarbejde med instruktører og trænere
Andre ad hoc-opgaver, der sikrer en optimal drift


Dine kvalifikationer

Hvem er du?


Energisk, serviceminded og nysgerrig på at opsøge relationer
Trives med praktiske opgaver såsom rengøring, organisering og vedligeholdelse
Komfortabel med at guide vores medlemmer på digitale platforme (app og hjemmeside)
Kan bidrage til en positiv, inkluderende kultur og være en rollemodel for vores værdier
Kan skabe et både sjovt og trygt miljø
Har gå på mod, er problemløsende og ivrig efter at lære nyt


Vi Tilbyder


En meningsfuld rolle, hvor du gør en forskel for vores medlemmer og bliver en del til Danmarks største fitnessfællesskab, der sikrer et sundere Danmark
Faste arbejdstider der kan ligge både i hverdag og weekend, samt morgen, eftermiddag og aften
Mulighed for personlig udvikling og læring gennem vores trænings- og udviklingsprogrammer, samt holduddannelser – samt en karrievej mod ledelse
En inkluderende kultur, hvor mangfoldighed og kreativitet værdsættes
Et arbejdsmiljø, hvor forandring og feedback er i centrum
Gratis personalemedlemskab så du frit kan træne og deltage på hold i alle PureGym centre i Danmark.


Bliv en del af noget større

Som medarbejder i PureGym bliver du en del af Danmarks største fitnesskæde med over 130 centre fordelt i hele landet. PureGym DK er en del af en af Europas største fitnesskæder, med hovedkontor i London, UK, med centre i Danmark, England, Schweiz, UAE og New York, US.

Vores mission er at inspirere til et sundere liv med ’Fitness for alle’ som omdrejningspunkt. Vi ser os selv som mere end bare en fitnesskæde. Vi hjælper vores medlemmer med at passe på deres fysiske og mentale sundhed i de bedste rammer med professionel support fra vores passionerede medarbejdere.

Ansøgningsfrist

Er du klar til at blive en del af vores team? Vi holder løbende samtaler, så send gerne din ansøgning allerede i dag eller snarest muligt.

Har du nogle spørgsmål er du velkommen til at komme forbi centeret.

Vi glæder os til at læse din ansøgning!

Er vi endt i spam?

Du vil altid modtage en bekræftelsesmail, når du har sendt din ansøgning. Hvis ikke du modtager denne, så tjek dit spamfilter og sørg for at fjerne os derfra, så fremtidig kommunikation lander i din almindelige indbakke.

Kontrakt

Tidsubegrænset

Stillingens lokation

Område

Nordjylland

Center/Afdeling

Aalborg, Kennedy Plads

Rekrutterende part

Startdato

Hurtigst muligt

Jobtitel

Gym Staff - Kennedys Plads

Bliv en del af holdet bag Danmarks største Fitnessfællesskab

Er du er på udkig efter et deltidsjob med mening, eller drømmer du om at starte din karriere indenfor fitness og sundhed, så glæder vi os til at få dig med på teamet hos PureGym Kennedys plads i Aalborg

Hvorfor blive en del af PureGym?

Hos PureGym ved vi, at vores medarbejdere er vores største styrke. Vi værdsætter din unikke personlighed, dine ideer og din vilje til at skabe og være en del af noget større. Vi arbejder for at skabe en arbejdsplads, hvor du føler dig værdsat, udfordret og inspireret til at vokse – både personligt og professionelt.

Hvis dine fremtidige karriereønsker er indenfor ledelse og fitness har vi karriereveje med interne uddannelser – både via digital læringsplatform og i kurser som holdinstruktør.

Om rollen

Som Gym Staff vil du være PureGyms ansigt udadtil, og være den, der sikrer den gode medlemsoplevelse og det motiverende træningsmiljø. Du vil arbejde både selvstændigt, og tæt sammen med dine kolleger, og din Gym Manager. Dine opgaver vil spænde bredt – fra daglig rengøring af centeret til vedligeholdelse af maskiner. Derudover vil du og dit team være centrets repræsentanter på vores lokale SoMe-kanal og viser stolt PureGyms teal-farve frem i lokalområdet.

Til denne Gym Staff rolle søger vi én til vores team hos Kennedys Plads, til 30 - 34 timer om ugen. De vagter, som kan blive dine, vil som udgangspunkt være: Mandag til fredag - der kan forekomme weekender

Dine Opgaver

  • Være serviceminded og skabe den gode stemning sammen med vores medlemmer
  • Hjælpe medlemmer med at navigere i selvbetjeningsløsninger (appen, hjemmeside)
  • Oprydning, rengøring og vedligeholdelse af udstyr – så centeret altid står skarpt og indbydende
  • Give vores medlemmer en god start og guide dem i centret i brugen af vores udstyr
  • Sørge for, at vores salgsautomater er fyldte og klar til medlemskøb
  • Hjælpe med lagerstyring og lageroptælling
  • Deltage i centerets SoMe aktiviteter, hvor du også er med på kameraret.
  • Stå i spidsen for Functional Express, som er træning af en mindre gruppe medlemmer ude på gulvet. Du er med til at formidle øvelser og skabe god energi.
  • Oplære dine nye kollegaer og samarbejde med instruktører og trænere
  • Andre ad hoc-opgaver, der sikrer en optimal drift

Dine kvalifikationer

Hvem er du?

  • Energisk, serviceminded og nysgerrig på at opsøge relationer
  • Trives med praktiske opgaver såsom rengøring, organisering og vedligeholdelse
  • Komfortabel med at guide vores medlemmer på digitale platforme (app og hjemmeside)
  • Kan bidrage til en positiv, inkluderende kultur og være en rollemodel for vores værdier
  • Kan skabe et både sjovt og trygt miljø
  • Har gå på mod, er problemløsende og ivrig efter at lære nyt

Vi Tilbyder

  • En meningsfuld rolle, hvor du gør en forskel for vores medlemmer og bliver en del til Danmarks største fitnessfællesskab, der sikrer et sundere Danmark
  • Faste arbejdstider der kan ligge både i hverdag og weekend, samt morgen, eftermiddag og aften
  • Mulighed for personlig udvikling og læring gennem vores trænings- og udviklingsprogrammer, samt holduddannelser – samt en karrievej mod ledelse
  • En inkluderende kultur, hvor mangfoldighed og kreativitet værdsættes
  • Et arbejdsmiljø, hvor forandring og feedback er i centrum
  • Gratis personalemedlemskab så du frit kan træne og deltage på hold i alle PureGym centre i Danmark.

Bliv en del af noget større

Som medarbejder i PureGym bliver du en del af Danmarks største fitnesskæde med over 130 centre fordelt i hele landet. PureGym DK er en del af en af Europas største fitnesskæder, med hovedkontor i London, UK, med centre i Danmark, England, Schweiz, UAE og New York, US.

Vores mission er at inspirere til et sundere liv med ’Fitness for alle’ som omdrejningspunkt. Vi ser os selv som mere end bare en fitnesskæde. Vi hjælper vores medlemmer med at passe på deres fysiske og mentale sundhed i de bedste rammer med professionel support fra vores passionerede medarbejdere.

Ansøgningsfrist

Er du klar til at blive en del af vores team? Vi holder løbende samtaler, så send gerne din ansøgning allerede i dag eller snarest muligt.

Har du nogle spørgsmål er du velkommen til at komme forbi centeret.

Vi glæder os til at læse din ansøgning!

Er vi endt i spam?

Du vil altid modtage en bekræftelsesmail, når du har sendt din ansøgning. Hvis ikke du modtager denne, så tjek dit spamfilter og sørg for at fjerne os derfra, så fremtidig kommunikation lander i din almindelige indbakke.

Title: Insights Analyst
Description:
The PureGym Way

The PureGym Group is a global gym business with a community of more than 700 gyms and over 2 million members. At PureGym, everything we do comes back to one simple idea: helping you feel good.

We're proud of our people and have a strong focus on internal progression. Championing diversity, we are committed to providing an excellent employee experience and workplace culture. As such, we are a Disability Confident Committed employer, and offer interviews to candidates who meet the essential criteria for a role, and opt-in to the scheme on their application form.

Our gyms are friendly, supportive, and judgement-free spaces where everybody can come in, work out and leave Feeling PureGym Good. We are proud to be certified by Top Employers Institute. See our careers page for full benefits.




We offer

Free nationwide gym membership for you + 1
Hybrid working
A truly flexible working culture
Personal private healthcare, including digital GP
Life insurance x4
Company pension contribution
25 days annual leave, plus 1 personal day
Option to purchase additional holiday (up to 5 days)
Great learning & development resources
Enhanced maternity pay, paternity and adoption leave




Location: Hybrid- London (3 days in the office)

Type: Full Time

Contract type: Permanent

Application: Closing date 22nd May 2026

We are a value business, focused on making gyms and fitness accessible to as many people as possible. Data is at the heart of how we run our business, starting from where to open our gyms, through what we offer within them, to how we engage with past, current, and future members. Combining the data from our 2.5+ million members with data about locations, operations, and performance allows us to unlock industry leading insights and drive a better member experience and commercial performance.

As an Insights Analyst you will be at the heart of solving some of the hardest data challenges in the business, turning data into insights and business value through a combination of in-depth analysis and reporting creation. You will be leveraging one of the biggest fitness data assets in the world, and working with state-of-the-art technologies, to help determine what drives member behaviour and commercial outcomes across all our operating geographies.




The Role

As a small, agile team, Insights works closely with its stakeholders, putting data at the heart of decision making across the business. While reporting to the Insights Manager, in this role you get to work closely with stakeholders at all levels across the business. You will also work in close collaboration with the wider Insights & Analytics functions, which provides ample opportunity to learn from a group of talented and experienced Data Scientists and Data Engineers.

In this role, you will have the opportunity to:

Work on business-critical analytics projects and reporting, identifying outcomes and communicating findings and recommendations to stakeholders across the business - Commercial, Finance, Operational and Marketing teams, in addition to Data Science and Data Engineering.
Build and maintain the central data product serving KPIs and insights to the entire organisation.
Work with Azure Databricks, PowerBI and dbt technology, leveraging modern cloud-native technologies and data lakehouse architecture.
Drive continuous improvement to how we enable data driven decision making in PureGym.




The Person

We would expect some or all of the following:

Experience in data analytics / insights
Proficient with SQL, Power BI, Excel and PowerPoint
Additionally, experience with dbt would be an advantage
A drive to turn insights into compelling stories for stakeholders to understand, for them to make more effective, data-led decisions
Strong commercial awareness with an interest in B2C

We are looking for an ambitious individual with a passion for leveraging analytics to drive business outcomes. The following core attributes are essential:

Excellent problem-solving: someone who enjoys applying their mind to many and varied business problems and be a driving force behind solving them
Innovative and curious: constantly seek to learn and find new and better ways of doing things; not just solving problems but seeking them out; always interested to find new and better methods and technologies
Biased towards action: take the initiative and get things done under their own steam and in collaboration with others
Collaborative: driven to engage with colleagues from across the business and support and enable your team members
A great communicator: able to translate complex concepts and analyses into digestible recommendations that can be easily interpreted and acted upon




PureGym is proud to be an equal opportunities employer. Our company mantra is ‘Everybody Welcome’ and we are dedicated to promoting a diverse and inclusive place to work. From a hiring standpoint, we welcome applicants from all backgrounds and are committed to ensuring that our PureGym colleagues reflect the diversity of the nation as well as our millions of gym members we serve.




Please note: Qualifications and Skills represent the essential criteria for the role, which need to be met to be offered an interview under the Disability Confident Scheme. Volume of eligible applications may impact everyone being able to be offered an interview.

Applications will be reviewed on a rolling basis, and the advert may be withdrawn at any time. Early application is encouraged.

Title: Data Analyst
Description:
Data Analyst




Industry: Designer toys and collectible pieces

Location: London, Canary Wharf

Job type: Full time Permanent




About POP MART




POP MART (09992.HK) is a leading global company in the trend culture and entertainment industry. Centered around IP, POP MART has built a comprehensive platform for creative incubation and IP operation, empowering global creators while delivering exciting products, services and immersive entertainment experiences to consumers.




POP MART identifies and nurtures emerging artists and designers worldwide, creating popular character IPs through a well-established development and operation system. Our portfolio includes iconic characters such as MOLLY, SKULLPANDA, DIMOO, THE MONSTERS and Hirono. POP MART operates over 500 physical stores and more than 2,300 Robo Shops across more than thirty countries and regions, and through multiple cross border e commerce platforms reaches audiences in over ninety countries and regions, bringing joy to young consumers around the world.




POP MART has developed a comprehensive IP commercialisation ecosystem that spans multiple industries, deepening emotional bonds between fans and IPs while exploring broader opportunities for value creation. Our ventures include the world’s first immersive intellectual property themed park in the art toy industry, the expansion of its IPs into global markets through licensing and co-branding with leading global brands, and the curation of art toy expos and intellectual property themed exhibitions internationally. In Europe, POP MART has built a strong presence in key markets including the United Kingdom, France, Italy, Germany, the Netherlands and Spain, and is expanding into new high potential markets. Our expansion brings POP MART closer to European fans and offers new ways for them to connect with our IPs and stories.




For more information, please visit www.popmart.com and our LinkedIn Career page.




What to Expect




POP MART is a fast-paced company with a young and multicultural team where you can expect to:




Contribute to a fast-growing European business that combines retail, art, and creative storytelling
Work with passionate teams across different countries, all connected by a shared love for designer toys and unique customer experiences
Grow your skills through hands-on projects, collaborative learning, and opportunities to explore new ideas




Benefits




POP MART offers our employees:




Monthly appreciation gifts featuring our latest POP MART designs
A structured bonus scheme linked to both company and individual performance
25 days of annual leave, in addition to bank holidays
4x life insurance, together with group medical and travel insurance
A confidential Employee Assistance Programme, alongside access to a discount platform for selected online retailers
Training and development programmes, together with regular team events that encourage learning, collaboration, and celebration




The Role and Candidate




We are looking for a data driven and detail oriented professional to support business performance monitoring and analysis across POP MART’s European business.

This role focuses on operational reporting, performance tracking, and business insight generation across key revenue channels, including physical stores, vending machines, and online business. You will support the business by monitoring core operational indicators, identifying trends and issues through data, and helping teams better understand performance through clear, accurate, and practical analysis.

You will also work closely with teams including Finance, Retail Operations, Marketing, Merchandising, and Business Development to support regular reporting, operational reviews, and day to day data requests. In parallel, the role will contribute to improving reporting consistency, data quality, and the practical use of data in business operations.

This position would suit someone with solid analytical capability, structured thinking, and a hands on approach, who is comfortable working with detailed data while supporting wider business needs in a fast paced environment.




Responsibilities




Daily Operation Monitoring and Performance Review




Establish daily, weekly and monthly monitoring for core operational indicators, including revenue, gross profit, expense ratio, payment collection, labour efficiency and store efficiency, across all revenue channels such as physical stores, vending machines and online business
Prepare daily, weekly, monthly and quarterly operational reports in a timely manner, benchmark performance against budget, year on year and month on month data, and analyse the causes of performance gaps
Track key business indicators including sales volume, average transaction value, conversion rate and profit margin, and provide regular operational analysis to support performance improvement
Analyse the performance of different markets, stores and vending machines, identify trends and issues, and support the development of targeted actions to improve profitability
Support the Business Development team with data input for store and vending machine site selection, based on market data and operational analysis
Support monthly and quarterly operational review meetings by preparing review materials and presentations, highlighting business progress, key issues and improvement priorities, and tracking follow up actions
Work closely with the Finance team to verify operational, cost and expense data, align statistical definitions, resolve data discrepancies, and ensure management reporting is accurate and reliable




Business Insights and Reporting




Work closely with business teams on a daily basis to understand key business pain points, and support practical data solutions based on business needs and operating scenarios
Conduct data analysis across sales operations, marketing and consumer insights, identify the business logic behind the data, and provide insights to support business optimisation
Improve the quality and depth of recurring insight reports by moving beyond data listing and strengthening business focused conclusions and recommendations
Build and maintain structured data records and archives to support data traceability, consistency and reusability
Extract and process data from multiple sources, including business systems, CRM and ERP systems, and use SQL for regular queries, data cleaning, deduplication, correlation and aggregation
Build and maintain basic dashboards and automated reports to reduce manual reporting, improve data visibility, and support more efficient access to information




Cross Functional Collaboration and Data Governance




Support Sales, Marketing, Product, Supply Chain, Finance and other teams with day to day data requests, including customised queries, report exports and basic analysis support
Provide guidance to business teams on basic data viewing and analysis methods to improve the practical use of data in daily operations
Support the standardisation of data fields, statistical rules and analysis templates, and help establish data logs and archiving practices to strengthen data governance
Contribute to the development of a more consistent and practical data culture across the business




Required Qualifications




3–5 years of business analysis experience; candidates with experience in management consulting, internet business analysis, retail operations analysis, or other relevant analytical experience, or those with extensive professional experience are preferred




Excellent communication and organisational skills, capable of handling complex issues with structured thinking and analytical abilities




Proactive, hands-on, and entrepreneurial mindset with experience working in a fast-paced business environment




Outstanding problem-solving, analytical, written and verbal communication skills




Data-sensitive, able to identify business trends and issues through data analysis; proficiency in data analysis and visualisation tools such as Excel, SQL, Tableau is preferred, but strong willingness to learn is a must




Proficiency in English (listening, speaking, reading and writing), with extensive experience in cross-team project collaboration and communication







Desirable but Not Essential




Experience in the toy industry or pop culture retail sector
Familiarity with Local and European retail landscapes
Understanding of designer toy and collectible trends, particularly in the art toy market
Experience supporting operational reporting across multiple countries or regions
Understanding of retail KPIs including conversion rate, ATV, margin, labour efficiency and inventory performance
Exposure to data governance, reporting automation, or process improvement initiatives
Experience within retail, consumer goods, eCommerce, or multi-site operations environments
Experience working with ERP, CRM, POS or business intelligence systems
Familiarity with dashboard tools such as Tableau, Power BI or Looker










If you are excited to be part of POP MART’s journey and believe you have the skills and passion to contribute, click Apply Now and send us your CV.

Title: Data Analyst
Description:
Airtime is a leading data-driven rewards platform, transforming how consumers save and how retailers connect with their customers. Our app is used by millions of members, helping them reduce everyday costs while delivering measurable value back to our retail partners.




We work with a wide range of well-known brands, using our proprietary technology, real-time transaction data and advanced analytics to power smarter, more effective performance marketing. By combining data, insight and creativity, we enable retailers to better understand and engage their customers, driving incremental revenue and long-term loyalty.




Our platform delivers a seamless, best-in-class experience for both members and partners, and we continue to invest in new features and capabilities to support our growth and ambition.




At Airtime, we are driven by innovation and this is what keeps us ahead. We are a team with a shared ambition to succeed, holding ourselves to high standards while working collaboratively to achieve our goals




The Opportunity




Airtime is looking for a Data Analyst with 3+ years of experience in a data analytics role. This is an opportunity for someone who can move beyond basic reporting and play a meaningful role in driving partner performance and commercial decisions across the business.




You’ll work across multiple areas including Sales, Marketing, Product, and Finance, giving you broad exposure to how the business operates. Alongside supporting ongoing analysis across a range of business needs, you’ll focus on shaping problems, uncovering insights, and delivering clear, actionable recommendations that support strategy and performance.




Reporting to the Analytics Manager, you will:




Analyse performance data to identify trends, risks, and opportunities across multiple areas of the business
Partner with stakeholders across Sales, Marketing, Product, and Finance to shape problems and deliver actionable insights that drive decision-making
Translate business questions into structured analysis and clear, actionable recommendations
Communicate complex data through clear, compelling narratives tailored to both technical and non-technical audiences
Develop a strong understanding of Airtime’s product, platform, and commercial model to provide context-rich analysis
Proactively identify opportunities to optimise performance, improve processes, and support business growth
Support ongoing analysis across a range of business needs, ensuring insights are consistent and relevant
Contribute to improving data quality, definitions, and consistency across the business




Requirements:

3+ years of experience in a data analytics role
Experience working cross-functionally and building relationships with stakeholders across different business areas
Strong commercial awareness and understanding of how data impacts business performance
Experience translating business requests or problems into structured analysis and actionable recommendations

Title: Data Analyst
Description:
Data Analyst

Up to £50 per hour Inside IR35

Filton, UK (Hybrid)

We are seeking an experienced Data Analyst to join our client on a contract basis, supporting critical aerospace projects. This is an exceptional opportunity to contribute to innovative initiatives within the aerospace industry, renowned for its technology-driven environment and commitment to excellence.

The successful candidate will play a vital part in shaping data-driven decision-making processes that impact aircraft safety, efficiency, and operational excellence.

Key Responsibilities

Analyse aircraft health data utilizing Skywise and Python tools to identify trends and insights.
Collaborate with Systems Engineering teams to interpret and enhance aircraft system performance.
Support health engineering activities related to aircraft lifecycle management.
Use in-service data to improve in-service operations understanding and system design.
Contribute to the development of fleet diagnostic and predictive maintenance models.
Support technical investigations with aircraft fleet data analysis.

Essential Skills & Experience

Proven experience in data analysis within the aerospace sector or similar high-technology environment.
Proficiency in Python programming for data manipulation and analysis.
Practical knowledge of Skywise or comparable aviation data platforms.
Strong understanding of aircraft systems and health engineering principles.
Ability to interpret complex data sets into actionable insights.
Experience with Jira, Palantir Foundry (Skywise), Extended Platform (Jupyter), Github.
Data Analytics & Data Visualisation skills.
Good stakeholder management skills.

Desirable Skills & Experience

Knowledge of Skywise Predictive Maintenance (SPM).
LG System understanding.
Experience with Manage Fleet Diagnostic and Predictive Maintenance process (SU.CP.05).
Previous experience in Predictive Maintenance and ZAOG plateau.
Familiarity with AI and Machine Learning techniques.
Highly organised, delivery-oriented, and capable of working independently.

If you are looking to apply data analytics within a dynamic aerospace setting and are eager to make a tangible impact, we invite you to submit your CV for consideration. This role offers the chance to work on innovative projects in a forward-thinking organisation committed to technological advancement and professional development.

Title: Junior Data Analyst
Description:
Graduate / Early Career Specialty Data Analyst - Marine, Cargo and Logistics





We're looking for a curious, analytical graduate or early‑career Data Analyst to join our Marine, Cargo and Logistics Specialty team.





This is a hands‑on role where you'll learn how data is used in business decisions, working closely with brokers, data specialists, and technology teams. You'll help turn complex datasets into clear insights that support clients and business across sport, media, and entertainment. You don't need to know everything on day one. We're looking for strong foundations, curiosity, and the motivation to learn fast.





With support and mentoring from experienced analysts, you'll:


Work with brokers and business stakeholders to understand real‑world problems and translate them into data questions.


Explore and prototype data ideas, helping shape user stories and analytical approaches.


Clean, analyse and validate data from multiple sources to ensure accuracy and reliability.


Build insights, visuals and case studies that are used in client discussions and proposals.


Spot patterns and trends in data and help explain what they mean in practice.


Use modern data and analytics tools to turn raw data into clear, actionable outputs.


Collaborate with data, technology and analytics teams across the wider group.


Document your work and follow best‑practice data standards.





What we are looking for:





Essential


A degree (or equivalent) in a numerate or analytical subject (e.g. maths, statistics, data science, economics, engineering, sciences)



Strong attention to detail and logical thinking


A genuine interest in how data can influence business decisions


Confidence explaining ideas to both technical and non‑technical audiences


An inquisitive, problem‑solving mindset





Technical skills (you don't need all of these on day one):


Experience using Excel for analysis


Exposure to Python, SQL and/or R through study, projects or internships


Familiarity with data visualisation tools such as Power BI (or similar)


Interest in learning modern data platforms (Databricks)





Nice to have (but not required)


University or personal projects involving real datasets


Basic understanding of data modelling or data pipelines


Curiosity about GenAI / LLMs and how they can support analytics


An interest cargo. marine, logistics, insurance and commercial analytics.





What you'll get





A structured early‑career role with on‑the‑job learning and mentoring



Exposure to client and operations work


Training and professional qualifications (e.g. Chartered Insurance Institute)


Competitive salary, generous pension and private medical cover

Title: Junior Data Analyst
Description:
JOB TITLE: Junior Analyst

REPORTS TO: Senior Business Analyst

DEPARTMENT: Support

LOCATION: Penta Consulting Ltd, Oaks House, 16-22 West Street, Epsom, Surrey, KT18 7RG




ABOUT PENTA CONSULTING LTD




At Penta Consulting, we believe technology reaches its full potential when powered by the right people. As a leading provider of Technology Resource Solutions, we connect businesses with top-tier talent to drive digital transformation and innovation.




With over 25 years of experience, we’ve built a global reputation for delivering high-quality expertise across key areas such as software development, artificial intelligence, project management, cloud computing, cybersecurity, enterprise architecture, and data analytics.




Our clients span the globe, relying on our team of specialists to deliver tailored, impactful solutions. We’re proud to be a trusted partner in helping organisations stay ahead in a rapidly evolving digital landscape.




If you're looking to join an award-winning, forward-thinking, and solution-focused team, Penta Consulting is the place to grow your career.




KEY STAKEHOLDERS




Executive Management, Senior Leadership, Professional Services Department, Resource Operations Department, Company Operations, International Team




JOB PURPOSE




This person will play an active role in maintaining the best practices and adherence to our internal playbook.




DUTIES & RESPONSIBILITIES




This person will be responsible for:




Data Integrity & Governance

Conduct scheduled data quality checks within Mercury CRM, including:
Mandatory field validation
Vacancies Open for more than 6 months
Reason code completeness
Duplicate and inconsistent record checks
Detection of backdated changes
Support enforcement of standardised definitions, dropdowns, and workflows across teams.
Log and escalate data integrity risks to the Senior Business Analyst.




Mercury CRM Workflow Compliance

Monitor user adherence to workflows across Sales, Care, Operations, and Compliance in line with Mercury playbook
Surface issues such as missing paperwork, incorrect placement statuses, and overdue approvals.
Maintain compliance dashboards and provide weekly summaries of key findings.




Departmental Reporting Support

Own the operational data inputs for departmental dashboards, including:
Business performance (Frequency)
Trend analysis
Compliance tasks
Report on company performance
Validate accuracy of departmental submissions and ensure alignment with reporting standards.
Identifying deficiencies withing the database and the CRM systems




Architectural Alignment

Coordinate between frontline systems (Mercury CRM, Business Central) and downstream models (Power BI).
Track, document, and communicate system issues or enhancements for BI and Mercury development teams.
Support change management as reporting logic, rules, or calculations evolve.




Documentation & Change Control

Maintain all process documentation related to reporting, data controls, and audit routines.
Manage version control for reporting logic, definitions, and workflows.
Create clear summaries of issues, resolutions, and schema or field changes.




EXPERIENCE & QUALIFICATIONS




Strong background in operational reporting, data quality, or business operations.
High attention to detail and accuracy; confident validating complex datasets.
Experience working with CRM or ERP systems (Mercury CRM or Business Central advantageous).
Competent in Excel; familiarity with Power BI preferred.
Ability to manage recurring reporting cycles with precision and consistency.
Strong communication skills for cross functional collaboration.




KEY COMPETENCIES




Analytical thinking - ability to identify inconsistencies, anomalies, and patterns in data.
Process ownership -takes responsibility for operational routines and service-level commitments.
Cross-functional coordination - collaborates effectively across Sales, Delivery, Finance, and Compliance.
Proactive problem solving -identifies issues early and escalates with solutions.




THE IDEAL CANDIDATE




High data quality in Mercury CRM and departmental submissions.
Well maintained templates and reporting processes.
Clear documentation and traceability of all reporting changes.

A fast-growing scale-up building innovative products in a rapidly evolving market.




Company description

A fast-growing scale-up using data to drive smarter decisions and unlock growth.

Role overview




The Junior Data Analyst will support the team in analysing data, generating insights, and improving decision-making across the business.




You’ll work with internal teams to collect, clean, and analyse data, helping identify trends, measure performance, and surface insights that inform strategy. Title: Data Analyst - TikTok LIVE
Description:
Responsibilities
The Regional LIVE Operation Strategy Team leads various cross-functional projects. Our key initiatives include developing strategic plans to drive widespread adoption of LIVE at scale among creators, localizing new product features, creating user engagement strategies, ensuring a positive creator experience through education on the Community Guidelines and best practices, and providing ad hoc analysis and support to cross-functional partners.

We're looking for a Senior Data Analyst to join our dynamic LIVE team. In this role, you’ll uncover insights and deliver data-driven recommendations that directly impact how we grow and support our creator ecosystem. You'll work cross-functionally with product, strategy, and operations teams to shape our growth priorities using data and analytical thinking.

- Analyze local and global content and user trends to identify growth opportunities within the EU LIVE ecosystem.
- Create, automate, and maintain reports and dashboards to track performance, OKRs, and key strategic initiatives.
- Develop data models and perform in-depth analyses to support content strategy, creator segmentation, and user engagement.
- Translate complex data into actionable insights for regional teams and leadership.
- Collaborate closely with cross-functional teams (e.g., Creator Management, Product, Strategy) to inform decision-making.
- Present findings clearly to both technical and non-technical stakeholders to influence business strategy.

Examples of Problems You’ll Be Solving
- What are the best content verticals for us to focus our creator recruitment on?
- How can we build a predictive model to identify high-potential creators early?
- What are the right leading and lagging indicators to measure success against our key OKRs?
- How can we segment creators and users to better personalize support and growth strategies?
- When and how should we educate creators to maximize their long-term retention and performance?
- How can we simplify complex data sets using statistical tools to better diagnose issues and recommend scalable solutions?

Qualifications
Minimum Qualifications
- 5+ years of experience in data analysis, preferably in a high-growth or tech environment.
- Strong mathematical and analytical skills, with the ability to interpret large datasets and generate actionable insights.
- Proficiency in SQL and at least one programming language (Python preferred).
- Solid understanding of statistics and data modeling techniques.
- Experience using data visualization tools such as Tableau, Power BI, or Looker.
- High attention to detail and commitment to data accuracy.

Preferred Qualifications
- Experience working in a consumer technology, content, or creator economy business.
- Background in building predictive models or recommendation systems.
- Familiarity with A/B testing and experimental design.
- Strong communication skills, with the ability to simplify complex topics for non-technical audiences.
- Knowledge of the live streaming industry or digital content ecosystems is a plus.

About TikTok
TikTok is the leading destination for short-form mobile video. At TikTok, our mission is to inspire creativity and bring joy. TikTok's global headquarters are in Los Angeles and Singapore, and we also have offices in New York City, London, Dublin, Paris, Berlin, Dubai, Jakarta, Seoul, and Tokyo.​


Why Join Us
Inspiring creativity is at the core of TikTok's mission. Our innovative product is built to help people authentically express themselves, discover and connect – and our global, diverse teams make that possible. Together, we create value for our communities, inspire creativity and bring joy - a mission we work towards every day.​
We strive to do great things with great people. We lead with curiosity, humility, and a desire to make impact in a rapidly growing tech company. Every challenge is an opportunity to learn and innovate as one team. We're resilient and embrace challenges as they come. By constantly iterating and fostering an "Always Day 1" mindset, we achieve meaningful breakthroughs for ourselves, our company, and our users. HTML:
Responsibilities
The Regional LIVE Operation Strategy Team leads various cross-functional projects. Our key initiatives include developing strategic plans to drive widespread adoption of LIVE at scale among creators, localizing new product features, creating user engagement strategies, ensuring a positive creator experience through education on the Community Guidelines and best practices, and providing ad hoc analysis and support to cross-functional partners.

We're looking for a Senior Data Analyst to join our dynamic LIVE team. In this role, you’ll uncover insights and deliver data-driven recommendations that directly impact how we grow and support our creator ecosystem. You'll work cross-functionally with product, strategy, and operations teams to shape our growth priorities using data and analytical thinking.

- Analyze local and global content and user trends to identify growth opportunities within the EU LIVE ecosystem.
- Create, automate, and maintain reports and dashboards to track performance, OKRs, and key strategic initiatives.
- Develop data models and perform in-depth analyses to support content strategy, creator segmentation, and user engagement.
- Translate complex data into actionable insights for regional teams and leadership.
- Collaborate closely with cross-functional teams (e.g., Creator Management, Product, Strategy) to inform decision-making.
- Present findings clearly to both technical and non-technical stakeholders to influence business strategy.

Examples of Problems You’ll Be Solving
- What are the best content verticals for us to focus our creator recruitment on?
- How can we build a predictive model to identify high-potential creators early?
- What are the right leading and lagging indicators to measure success against our key OKRs?
- How can we segment creators and users to better personalize support and growth strategies?
- When and how should we educate creators to maximize their long-term retention and performance?
- How can we simplify complex data sets using statistical tools to better diagnose issues and recommend scalable solutions?

Qualifications
Minimum Qualifications
- 5+ years of experience in data analysis, preferably in a high-growth or tech environment.
- Strong mathematical and analytical skills, with the ability to interpret large datasets and generate actionable insights.
- Proficiency in SQL and at least one programming language (Python preferred).
- Solid understanding of statistics and data modeling techniques.
- Experience using data visualization tools such as Tableau, Power BI, or Looker.
- High attention to detail and commitment to data accuracy.

Preferred Qualifications
- Experience working in a consumer technology, content, or creator economy business.
- Background in building predictive models or recommendation systems.
- Familiarity with A/B testing and experimental design.
- Strong communication skills, with the ability to simplify complex topics for non-technical audiences.
- Knowledge of the live streaming industry or digital content ecosystems is a plus.

About TikTok
TikTok is the leading destination for short-form mobile video. At TikTok, our mission is to inspire creativity and bring joy. TikTok's global headquarters are in Los Angeles and Singapore, and we also have offices in New York City, London, Dublin, Paris, Berlin, Dubai, Jakarta, Seoul, and Tokyo.​


Why Join Us

Our innovative product is built to help people authentically express themselves, discover and connect – and our global, diverse teams make that possible. Title: Graduate Data Analyst
Description:
Graduate Data Analyst Opportunity – Launch Your Career in Data, Business Insight, and Digital Transformation




24-Month Fixed Term Contract | Bristol | £26,000 (Year 1)




Are you a technically-minded graduate eager to launch your career in the dynamic world of data, business improvement, and IT transformation? Here’s your chance to join ea Change, a leading firm dedicated to developing the next generation of business and IT leaders.




About Us

ea Change has over two decades of expertise driving Business and IT transformation. We help organisations achieve meaningful, lasting change while nurturing the leaders of tomorrow through our Futures Programme. Our culture prioritises diversity, inclusion, integrity, and people-first values, ensuring every team member thrives.




The Role

As a Graduate Data Analyst, you will be a key contributor on client projects, collaborating with senior staff, supporting decision-making, and helping drive business improvement. No two days are the same – your work will have real impact, turning data into actionable insight across a range of business areas.




Responsibilities include:

Analyse and interpret data to provide actionable business insights.
Create compelling visualisations using tools such as Power BI, PowerApps, and other BI or visualization platforms.
Support business improvement initiatives with data-driven recommendations.
Work closely with cross-functional teams to understand and define business requirements.
Assist in maintaining and optimising databases using SQL or other data management tools.
Contribute to project documentation, reporting, and stakeholder communications.




We welcome graduates with technical backgrounds who may already have some of these skills, or who are proficient in other analytical, coding, or visualization tools and are eager to learn and grow.




Requirements

A 2:1 in a technical undergraduate degree.
Right to work in the United Kingdom.
Strong analytical, problem-solving, and numerical skills.
Excellent communication and collaboration abilities.
Strong attention to detail and a proactive mindset.
Full driving licence and ability to travel to the office as needed (hybrid working).
Experience or familiarity with SQL, Power BI, PowerApps, Python, R, Excel, Tableau, or similar tools is a plus but not essential.




Benefits

Fully funded accredited qualifications
Bespoke flexible training
A health cash plan
26 days annual leave
Regular mentoring and personal development
Access to a comprehensive learning portal
Opportunities for progression and a robust fast-track into Business Change
Hybrid working and commercial exposure




At ea Change, you will find a vibrant community that wholeheartedly believes in pushing boundaries, embracing innovation, and celebrating diversity. We assure an ambience where everyone is encouraged to express themselves, contributing to our shared goal of shaping the future of business success.




How to Apply

If you're interested in this opportunity and fit the bill, we'd be thrilled to hear from you so kindly submit your CV. Together, let's shape the future!




ea Change are unable to sponsor work visas for international applicants as we are not on the UK Border Agency's Sponsor Register.




If we are fortunate in receiving a high volume of quality applications we may not be able to get back to everyone individually. Please assume if you have not heard back from the team within 14 days of application, your application has been unsuccessful.

Title: Data Analyst
Description:
At Frasers Group we’re rethinking retail. Through digital innovation and unique store experiences, we’re serving our consumers with the world’s best sports, premium and luxury brands globally. As a leader in the industry, we’re elevating the retail experience for our consumers through our collection of established brands, including Sports Direct, FLANNELS, USC, Frasers, and GAME.



Why join us?



Our vision – we are building the worlds most admired and compelling brand ecosystem



Our purpose – we are elevating the lives of the many with access to the world’s best brands and experiences



At Frasers Group, we fear less and do more. Our people are forward thinkers who are driven to operate outside of their comfort zone to change the future of retail, embracing challenges along the way. The potential to elevate your career is massive, the experience unrivalled. To be able to make the most of it you need to live and breathe our principles:



Think without limits – Think, think fast, think fearlessly
Own it and back yourself - Own the basics, own your role and own the results
Be relevant - Relevant to our people, our partners and the planet

Are you ready to join the Fearless?





Job Description



This is a Data Analyst role where you will be working as part of the analytics team within the Estates function, focusing on developing, analysing & creating tools/models/reports used by the business. You must have an analytical mindset, strong communication skills & be able to adapt to fast paced, ever changing business environments.



Role Description:



Serve as the key analytical point of contact for one of the fastest-growing areas of the business, leading forecasting and delivering analysis to support continued growth.
Liaise with c-suite and senior leadership, fulfilling data and report requests and developing strategies.
Create and implement AI and automation projects, primarily in Airtable.
Leverage VBA to automate manual processes.
Assist in the maintenance and development of Power BI reports.
Produce ad-hoc reports and perform detailed analysis of property data using Excel.
Write and optimise database queries.
Identify trends and provide strategic recommendations.
Collaborate with both technical and non-technical stakeholders across the business.


Qualifications



Essential Skills:



Advanced Excel skills, including VBA and Power Query.
Experience in developing Power BI reports, including writing and optimising DAX measures.
Proficient in SQL, and experienced working with relational databases.
Understanding of financial concepts such as EBITDA, or an interest in developing financial knowledge is advantageous.
Familiarity with Airtable is advantageous.
Experience with programming languages such as Python or R is advantageous.
Background in commercial property, utilities or retail analytics is advantageous.
Able to hold your own in meetings and confident talking to senior stakeholders
Able to manage and prioritise workload.
Strong problem-solving ability & a commercial mindset
Able to communicate with a wide range of stakeholders with various levels of technical understanding.
Proactive, independent, self-starter who is ready to jump in and get things done


Additional Information



Along with your benefits package we also offer a wide range of perks for our colleagues:



Frasers Champion- Our employees are at the heart of our business and we ensure individuals are recognised every single month for their hard work. Frasers Champion is a peer nominated scheme where 8 winners will receive double their pay for a month where they have lived the Frasers Group values.



Retail Reconnect – In order to build the planets most admired and compelling brand ecosystem, all employees must understand our business, product and customers. Each financial year, head office employees will gain insights by spending one to two days in one of our stores or the warehouse. The goal is to learn how the work you do impacts our teams on the frontline, and to bring ideas back to the office which will improve how we work.



Fearless 1200 – Fearless 1200 is our way of recognising our growth. It’s bold, ambitious, and designed to reward colleagues across the business for living our values and delivering impact. Fearless 1200 links how we perform to how we're rewarded – check out the video link to find out more - https://www.youtube.com/watch?v=O0qmvJofMew



Employee Welfare



Frasers Fit – Our Everlast Gyms Team are on a mission to make our workforce the best, and fittest on the planet! We run free gym classes for employees as well as discounted memberships to our clubs. Frasers Fit is our wellbeing programme which aims to support and improve colleagues Physical, Financial & Mental wellbeing. The app is accessible for every employee and includes training, nutrition and lifestyle advice- all completely free.



Retail Trust – We know that its not just about physical health, mental wellness is equally important which is why all of our employees get free access and support from the Retail Trust charity. This includes a 24 hour wellbeing helpline, wellness hub, counselling and financial/legal support.



What’s next?



Our Recruitment Team will be reviewing applications and all candidates will receive a response, whether you are successful or unsuccessful. Shortlisted applicants may be asked to confirm a few key details before being booked in for a first stage interview with the Recruiter - this will be behaviourally focussed and centred around how you align with our Culture and Values. If successful we anticipate one or two further interview stages with the Hiring Manager/wider team which will be more technically focussed and could include a presentation/task so we can see your skills in action.

Title: Data Analyst
Description:
About Us

Finalto is a global leader in liquidity provision and trading technology solutions, serving institutional and B2B clients across financial markets worldwide. With regulated entities in the UK, Singapore, Cyprus, Australia, and the UAE, and additional operational teams in Denmark and Bulgaria, we combine international reach with local expertise.




Our business spans multi-asset liquidity, risk management, and cutting-edge trading platforms, supporting clients in achieving more efficient and sustainable growth. At the core of our success is our commitment to innovation, operational excellence, and robust governance. 




As part of a global financial services group, Finalto combines the scale and stability of an established organisation with the agility of a fintech innovator. We are driven by collaboration, integrity, and performance. 




Joining Finalto means becoming part of a diverse and dynamic team where your contributions have real impact. We invest in our people, offering opportunities for professional growth, international exposure, and the chance to shape the future of trading technology and liquidity solutions. 




Role Description

As a Data Analyst at Finalto, you will play a key role in transforming data into actionable insights through impactful dashboards and reporting solutions. You will work closely with stakeholders across the business to support data-driven decision-making and help drive continuous improvement in reporting and analytics.




Key Responsibilities

Develop and maintain interactive dashboards and reports using Power BI
Analyse datasets to uncover trends and support business decision-making
Work with stakeholders to understand reporting and data requirements
Ensure data accuracy, quality, and consistency in reporting outputs
Work with structured datasets to perform data analysis and transformations
Support the integration of data from multiple sources
Collaborate with data engineers to ensure data is accessible and fit for analysis
Contribute to documentation and best practices for reporting and analytics




Requirements

Demonstrated experience in data analytics, with a proven ability to deliver actionable insights
Strong hands-on experience with SQL and Power BI (mandatory)
Understanding of data modelling and data warehousing concepts
Strong analytical thinking and problem-solving skills
Good communication and stakeholder management skills
Experience with Databricks or other modern data platforms
Experience in banking, financial services, or fast-paced, data-driven environments is preferred.




Desired Skills

Basic knowledge of Python (nice to have)
Experience working in an Agile environment
 Relevant industry certifications




Preferred Qualifications

A degree in STEM or Computer Science.

Title: Data Analyst - Flexibility Team
Description:
Octopus Energy is at the forefront of the energy transition, making energy cheaper and greener through its cutting-edge technology. The Flexibility team is building the next generation of products and tools to make energy more flexible, including automating electric vehicle charging, saving customers money by demand shifting through Saving Sessions and Free Electricity, and managing large grid-scale batteries to balance the system. The team develops products that are used by millions of customers, helping boost renewables use and decarbonise energy systems worldwide.




As we continue to grow our product portfolio, we are looking for a Data Analyst to lead on investigations evaluating our performance and determining the best trading strategies across a wide range of market opportunities.




What you will do:
Perform regular analysis on internal and external data sets. For example understanding customer charging behaviour, and exploring bidding strategies in Flexibility markets

Title: Data Analyst
Description:
LOCATION: Pier Walk, London

SHIFT PATTERN: Monday - Friday, 8am - 5pm, 40 hours per week

SALARY: £38,000.00 per annum

If you require any additional support or adjustments during the recruitment process, please don't hesitate to contact our Recruitment Department at recruitment@abm.com. We're here to help!

Role Overview And Purpose

The Data Analyst role will support the TfL Mechanical & Electrical contract to support contractual performance and data consistency. The candidate will work within the performance management team working alongside a lead data analyst to ensure consistent operational performance management of our data.

Key Responsibilities


Data entry
Data processing
Data analysis and reporting.
Consolidating data.
Understand the current systems and work towards improving the data relationships between different systems.
Reviewing data collection processes and assessing the validity of the information stored
Identifying anomalous data and examining and eliminating any processes or procedures that contribute to unreliable data being collected.
Working closely with the relevant teams to ensure that the data collection and storage systems are secure and robust.
Create interactive reports & dashboards with Power BI


This is a challenging role that will require good analytical and project management skills.

Required Skills And Experience


Microsoft Excel High level of skill and experience
Collect, review, analyse and maintain contract performance data and use it to support contract delivery.
Produce dashboard reports from contract delivery data to assist contract compliance, budget and strategic reviews and trends.
Identify and implement quality and efficiency improvement initiatives. Identifying inefficiencies across the contract.
Assist in the development of the innovation plan for service delivery improvements.
Engage business leaders and senior operational managers across the business to ensure best practice and operational efficiencies are implemented.
Understand, anticipate and deliver customer (internal and external) needs while building effective relationships.
Efficiently respond to both internal and external customers through effective communication and personal accessibility.
Ensure customer satisfaction is high.
Carry out other admin tasks as directed.


Benefits

We're proud to offer a great range of benefits including:


24/7 GP: Both you and your immediate family can speak to a UK-based GP from the comfort of your own home
Mental Health support and Life Event Counseling
Get Fit Programme
Financial and legal support
Cycle to work scheme
Access Perks at Work, our innovative employee app where you can find:
Perks: discounts, gift cards, cashback, and exclusive offers
Life: Search for resources and tools on topics ranging from family and life to health, money and work
Support: Online chat or telephone service for urgent support in a crisis


For More Information About ABM's Benefits, Visit Our

ABM (NYSE: ABM) is one of the world's largest providers of integrated facility, engineering, and infrastructure solutions. Every day, over 100,000 team members deliver essential services that make spaces cleaner, safer, and efficient, enhancing the overall occupant experience.

ABM serves a wide range of market sectors including commercial real estate, aviation, mission critical, and manufacturing and distribution. With over $8 billion in annual revenue and a blue-chip client base, ABM delivers innovative technologies and sustainable solutions that enhance facilities and empower clients to achieve their goals. Committed to creating smarter, more connected spaces, ABM is investing in the future to meet evolving challenges and build a healthier, thriving world. ABM: Driving possibility, together.

Title: Data Analyst
Description:
Data Analyst for London Private Equity Firm (On-site)

The client, an established London private equity firm with a small, high-performing team, is seeking a Data Analyst to take end-to-end ownership of its data and analytics function. This is a hands-on technical role spanning data analysis, data engineering and debugging. As the sole technical hire, you will build and maintain the firm's data pipelines, resolve issues across the stack, and turn raw data into something the investment and operations teams can act on, working directly with senior stakeholders. It suits a technically strong, early-career engineer who wants genuine ownership and breadth rather than a narrow slot in a large team.

Data Analyst Responsibilities

Build and maintain Python-first data pipelines
Run analysis to support investment and operational decisions
Debug and resolve data issues across the stack
Own data tooling and workflows end to end as the sole technical hire
Standardise and improve how data is structured and consumed across the firm
Work directly with senior stakeholders, translating their questions into technical solutions

Key Requirements

0 to 2 years experience, internships included. Strong recent graduates welcome
Computer Science, or a closely related STEM degree, from a leading global university (top 200), 2:1 or above
Strong, hands-on Python and SQL
Exposure to ETL or data pipelines is an advantage
Genuinely technical with real debugging ability, not a finance-first analyst
Comfortable working independently and owning outcomes as the only technical person
Able to work 5 days a week on-site in London
